entries.source:
// Tests for Builder Pattern — HtmlBuilder / HtmlElement // Note: use intermediate variables to chain builder calls, because newline is // a statement separator in Beamtalk (multi-line parenthesised chains fail). TestCase subclass: HtmlBuilderTest testSimpleElement => b := HtmlBuilder buildTag: "p" b := b withText: "hello" el := b build self assert: el render equals: "

hello

" testDivElement => b := HtmlBuilder buildTag: "div" b := b withText: "content" el := b build self assert: el render equals: "
content
" testWithAttribute => b := HtmlBuilder buildTag: "a" b := b withAttr: "href" value: "https://example.com" b := b withText: "click" el := b build self assert: el render equals: "click" testMultipleAttributes => b := HtmlBuilder buildTag: "input" b := b withAttr: "type" value: "text" b := b withAttr: "id" value: "name" b := b withText: "" el := b build rendered := el render self assert: (rendered includesSubstring: "type=""text""") self assert: (rendered includesSubstring: "id=""name""") testEmptyContent => b := HtmlBuilder buildTag: "br" el := b build self assert: el render equals: "

" testTwoIndependentBuilds => b1 := HtmlBuilder buildTag: "p" b1 := b1 withText: "first" b2 := HtmlBuilder buildTag: "p" b2 := b2 withText: "second" self assert: b1 build render equals: "

first

" self assert: b2 build render equals: "

second

"
entries.explanation: Tests for Builder Pattern — HtmlBuilder / HtmlElement Note: use intermediate variables to chain builder calls, because newline is a statement separator in Beamtalk (multi-line parenthesised chains fail).

entries.title: CommandHistoryTest
entries.source:
// Tests for Command Pattern — CommandHistory, InsertCommand, DeleteCommand

TestCase subclass: CommandHistoryTest

  testInsertCommand =>
    buf := TextBuffer withText: "hello"
    cmd := InsertCommand at: 6 insert: " world"
    result := cmd execute: buf
    self assert: result text equals: "hello world"

  testDeleteCommand =>
    buf := TextBuffer withText: "hello world"
    cmd := DeleteCommand from: 6 length: 6
    result := cmd execute: buf
    self assert: result text equals: "hello"

  testHistoryExecute =>
    h := CommandHistory on: (TextBuffer withText: "hello")
    h := h execute: (InsertCommand at: 6 insert: " world")
    self assert: h buffer text equals: "hello world"

  testHistoryUndoInsert =>
    h := CommandHistory on: (TextBuffer withText: "hello")
    h := h execute: (InsertCommand at: 6 insert: " world")
    h := h undo
    self assert: h buffer text equals: "hello"

  testHistoryUndoDelete =>
    h := CommandHistory on: (TextBuffer withText: "hello world")
    h := h execute: (DeleteCommand from: 6 length: 6)
    h := h undo
    self assert: h buffer text equals: "hello world"

  testHistoryMultipleUndos =>
    h := CommandHistory on: (TextBuffer withText: "")
    h := h execute: (InsertCommand at: 1 insert: "foo")
    h := h execute: (InsertCommand at: 4 insert: "bar")
    h := h undo
    self assert: h buffer text equals: "foo"
    h := h undo
    self assert: h buffer text equals: ""

  testCanUndo =>
    h := CommandHistory on: (TextBuffer withText: "hi")
    self deny: h canUndo
    h := h execute: (InsertCommand at: 3 insert: "!")
    self assert: h canUndo
    h := h undo
    self deny: h canUndo

  testUndoOnEmptyHistoryIsNoop =>
    h := CommandHistory on: (TextBuffer withText: "hi")
    h := h undo
    self assert: h buffer text equals: "hi"

  testDeleteThenInsertThenUndoBoth =>
    h := CommandHistory on: (TextBuffer withText: "hello world")
    h := h execute: (DeleteCommand from: 6 length: 6)
    h := h execute: (InsertCommand at: 6 insert: " there")
    h := h undo
    self assert: h buffer text equals: "hello"
    h := h undo
    self assert: h buffer text equals: "hello world"
entries.explanation: Tests for Command Pattern — CommandHistory, InsertCommand, DeleteCommand

entries.title: FsEntryTest
entries.source:
// Tests for Composite Pattern — FsFile and FsDir

TestCase subclass: FsEntryTest

  testFileNameAndSize =>
    f := FsFile name: "readme.txt" size: 42
    self assert: f name equals: "readme.txt"
    self assert: f size equals: 42

  testFileDescribe =>
    f := FsFile name: "main.bt" size: 100
    self assert: (f describe: "") equals: "main.bt (100 bytes)"

  testFileDescribeWithIndent =>
    f := FsFile name: "lib.bt" size: 50
    self assert: (f describe: "  ") equals: "  lib.bt (50 bytes)"

  testEmptyDirSize =>
    d := FsDir name: "empty"
    self assert: d size equals: 0

  testDirWithOneFile =>
    f := FsFile name: "a.bt" size: 10
    d := (FsDir name: "src") add: f
    self assert: d size equals: 10

  testDirWithMultipleFiles =>
    f1 := FsFile name: "a.bt" size: 100
    f2 := FsFile name: "b.bt" size: 200
    d := ((FsDir name: "src") add: f1) add: f2
    self assert: d size equals: 300

  testNestedDirs =>
    leaf := FsFile name: "x.bt" size: 50
    inner := (FsDir name: "inner") add: leaf
    outer := (FsDir name: "outer") add: inner
    self assert: outer size equals: 50

  testDirDescribeFlat =>
    f := FsFile name: "main.bt" size: 120
    d := (FsDir name: "src") add: f
    result := d describe: ""
    self assert: result equals: "src/ (120 bytes)\n  main.bt (120 bytes)"

  testDirIsImmutable =>
    d1 := FsDir name: "src"
    d2 := d1 add: (FsFile name: "a.bt" size: 1)
    self assert: d1 size equals: 0
    self assert: d2 size equals: 1
entries.explanation: Tests for Composite Pattern — FsFile and FsDir

entries.title: EventBusTest
entries.source:
// Tests for Observer Pattern — EventBus and EventCollector
//
// With sync-by-default dispatch, bus notify: calls receive: on each subscriber
// synchronously. When notify: returns, all subscribers have processed the event

TestCase subclass: EventBusTest

  testSubscribeAndNotify =>
    bus := EventBus spawn
    col := EventCollector spawn
    bus subscribe: col
    bus notify: "hello"
    self assert: col eventCount equals: 1
    self assert: col events first equals: "hello"
    bus stop
    col stop

  testMultipleNotifications =>
    bus := EventBus spawn
    col := EventCollector spawn
    bus subscribe: col; notify: "a"; notify: "b"; notify: "c"
    self assert: col eventCount equals: 3
    bus stop
    col stop

  testUnsubscribeStopsNotifications =>
    bus := EventBus spawn
    col := EventCollector spawn
    bus subscribe: col; notify: "before"; unsubscribe: col; notify: "after"
    self assert: col eventCount equals: 1
    self assert: col events first equals: "before"
    bus stop
    col stop

  testMultipleSubscribers =>
    bus := EventBus spawn
    c1 := EventCollector spawn
    c2 := EventCollector spawn
    bus subscribe: c1; subscribe: c2; notify: "event"
    self assert: c1 eventCount equals: 1
    self assert: c2 eventCount equals: 1
    bus stop
    c1 stop
    c2 stop

  testNoSubscribersNoError =>
    bus := EventBus spawn
    col := EventCollector spawn
    bus notify: "ghost"
    self assert: col eventCount equals: 0
    bus stop
    col stop
entries.explanation: Tests for Observer Pattern — EventBus and EventCollector  With sync-by-default dispatch, bus notify: calls receive: on each subscriber synchronously. When notify: returns, all subscribers have processed the event

entries.title: AppLoggerTest
entries.source:
// Tests for Singleton Pattern — AppLogger

TestCase subclass: AppLoggerTest

  testInitiallyEmpty =>
    logger := AppLogger spawn
    self assert: logger size equals: 0
    logger stop

  testLogEntry =>
    logger := AppLogger spawn
    logger log: "first"
    self assert: logger size equals: 1
    self assert: logger entries first equals: "first"
    logger stop

  testMultipleLogs =>
    logger := AppLogger spawn
    logger log: "a"; log: "b"; log: "c"
    self assert: logger size equals: 3
    logger stop

  testClearResets =>
    logger := AppLogger spawn
    logger log: "x"
    logger clear
    self assert: logger size equals: 0
    logger stop

  testEntriesOrdering =>
    logger := AppLogger spawn
    logger log: "first"
    logger log: "second"
    self assert: logger entries first equals: "first"
    self assert: logger entries last equals: "second"
    logger stop

  testSingletonBehavior =>
    // Two independently spawned loggers must not share state
    a := AppLogger spawn
    b := AppLogger spawn
    a log: "from a"
    self assert: a size equals: 1
    self assert: b size equals: 0
    a stop
    b stop
entries.explanation: Tests for Singleton Pattern — AppLogger

entries.title: SorterTest
entries.source:
// Tests for Strategy Pattern — Sorter

TestCase subclass: SorterTest

  testAscendingSort =>
    asc := Sorter ascending
    self assert: (asc sort: #(3, 1, 2)) equals: #(1, 2, 3)

  testDescendingSort =>
    desc := Sorter descending
    self assert: (desc sort: #(3, 1, 2)) equals: #(3, 2, 1)

  testCustomStrategy =>
    byLen := Sorter withStrategy: [:a :b | a length < b length]
    result := byLen sort: #("banana", "fig", "apple")
    self assert: result first equals: "fig"

  testAlreadySorted =>
    asc := Sorter ascending
    self assert: (asc sort: #(1, 2, 3)) equals: #(1, 2, 3)

  testSingleElement =>
    asc := Sorter ascending
    self assert: (asc sort: #(42)) equals: #(42)

  testEmptyList =>
    asc := Sorter ascending
    self assert: (asc sort: #()) equals: #()

  testStrategiesAreInterchangeable =>
    nums := #(5, 2, 8, 1)
    self assert: ((Sorter ascending) sort: nums) equals: #(1, 2, 5, 8)
    self assert: ((Sorter descending) sort: nums) equals: #(8, 5, 2, 1)
entries.explanation: Tests for Strategy Pattern — Sorter

entries.title: DataExporterTest

// Tests for Template Method Pattern — CsvExporter and HtmlExporter TestCase subclass: DataExporterTest testCsvSingleRow => csv := CsvExporter new result := csv export: #(#("Alice", "30")) self assert: result equals: "Alice,30\n" testCsvMultipleRows => csv := CsvExporter new result := csv export: #(#("Alice", "30"), #("Bob", "25")) self assert: result equals: "Alice,30\nBob,25\n" testCsvEmptyRows => csv := CsvExporter new self assert: (csv export: #()) equals: "" testCsvSingleCellRow => csv := CsvExporter new self assert: (csv export: #(#("only"))) equals: "only\n" testHtmlSingleRow => html := HtmlExporter new result := html export: #(#("Alice", "30")) self assert: result equals: "\n\n
Alice30
\n" testHtmlMultipleRows => html := HtmlExporter new result := html export: #(#("a", "1"), #("b", "2")) self assert: result equals: "\n\n\n
a1
b2
\n" testHtmlEmptyRows => html := HtmlExporter new self assert: (html export: #()) equals: "\n
\n"
entries.explanation: Tests for Template Method Pattern — CsvExporter and HtmlExporter

entries.title: AppSupervisor
entries.explanation: AppSupervisor — root supervisor for the otp-tree application.  Strategy #oneForOne: if a child crashes, only that child is restarted. EventLogger is a permanent worker — always restarted on crash. WorkerPool is a permanent DynamicSupervisor — always restarted, which also restarts any workers it was managing.  This class is referenced in beamtalk.toml [application] supervisor, so `beamtalk run` starts it automatically as an OTP application.

entries.title: EventLogger
entries.explanation: EventLogger — permanent actor that records events from across the system. Because its supervisionPolicy is #permanent, the supervisor always restarts it on crash.  Other actors can reach the running instance via the root supervisor: `(AppSupervisor current which: EventLogger) log: msg`.

entries.title: TaskWorker
entries.explanation: TaskWorker — transient actor managed by WorkerPool. supervisionPolicy #transient: the supervisor restarts it only if it exits abnormally (crash).  A clean shutdown is not restarted.  Error demo: sending `process: 0` raises a user_error back to the caller via self error:.  The actor process itself stays alive (the gen_server is not killed); the REPL walkthrough uses this to demonstrate fault isolation.

entries.title: WorkerPool
entries.explanation: WorkerPool — DynamicSupervisor that manages TaskWorker children. Workers are started on demand at runtime via `pool startChild`. Each worker is supervised independently: a crash in one worker does not affect the others or the rest of the tree.

entries.title: Main
entries.source:
Value subclass: Main

  class run => self new run

  run =>
    reader := SchemeReader new
    ev := SchemeEval new
    printer := SchemePrinter new
    env := ev defaultEnv

    eval := [:src | printer print: (ev eval: (reader read: src) in: env)]
    (WorkspaceInterface current) bind: eval as: #eval

    self traceCr: "Run: eval value: ""(* 6 7)""."
    self

entries.title: SchemeEnv
SchemeEval
SchemeLambda
SchemePrinter
SchemeReader
SchemeSymbol
SchemeTest
entries.source:
// SchemeTest — BUnit tests for the Scheme metacircular evaluator
//
// Run interactively in the REPL:
//   > :load src/scheme/symbol.bt
//   > :load src/scheme/reader.bt
//   > :load src/scheme/env.bt
//   > :load src/scheme/lambda.bt
//   > :load src/scheme/eval.bt
//   > :load src/scheme/printer.bt
//   > :load test/scheme_test.bt
//   > SchemeTest runAll

TestCase subclass: SchemeTest

  // Helpers shared across tests
  eval: str =>
    reader := SchemeReader new
    ev := SchemeEval new
    env := ev defaultEnv
    ev eval: (reader read: str) in: env

  evalStr: str => SchemePrinter new print: (self eval: str)

  // --- Reader ---
  testReaderInteger =>
    reader := SchemeReader new
    self assert: (reader read: "42") equals: 42

  testReaderSymbol =>
    reader := SchemeReader new
    sym := reader read: "foo"
    self assert: (sym class =:= SchemeSymbol)

  testReaderBoolTrue =>
    reader := SchemeReader new
    self assert: (reader read: "#t") equals: true

  testReaderBoolFalse =>
    reader := SchemeReader new
    self assert: (reader read: "#f") equals: false

  testReaderString =>
    reader := SchemeReader new
    self assert: (reader read: """hello""") equals: "hello"

  testReaderStringEscapedQuote =>
    reader := SchemeReader new
    self assert: (reader read: """say """"hi"""" please""") equals: "say ""hi"" please"

  testReaderList =>
    reader := SchemeReader new
    lst := reader read: "(1 2 3)"
    self assert: lst size equals: 3

  // --- Self-evaluating expressions ---
  testEvalInteger => self assert: (self eval: "99") equals: 99

  testEvalBoolTrue => self assert: (self eval: "#t") equals: true

  testEvalBoolFalse => self assert: (self eval: "#f") equals: false

  // --- Arithmetic ---
  testAdd => self assert: (self eval: "(+ 3 4)") equals: 7

  testMul => self assert: (self eval: "(* 6 7)") equals: 42

  testSub => self assert: (self eval: "(- 10 3)") equals: 7

  testDiv => self assert: (self eval: "(/ 10 2)") equals: 5

  // --- Quote ---
  testQuote =>
    result := self eval: "(quote hello)"
    self assert: (result class =:= SchemeSymbol)

  // --- Define and lookup ---
  testDefine =>
    reader := SchemeReader new
    ev := SchemeEval new
    env := ev defaultEnv
    ev eval: (reader read: "(define x 10)") in: env
    self assert: (ev eval: (reader read: "x") in: env) equals: 10

  // --- Lambda and application ---
  testLambdaApply =>
    self assert: (self eval: "((lambda (x) (* x x)) 5)") equals: 25

  testLambdaMultiParam =>
    self assert: (self eval: "((lambda (x y) (+ x y)) 3 4)") equals: 7

  // --- If ---
  testIfTrue => self assert: (self eval: "(if #t 1 2)") equals: 1

  testIfFalse => self assert: (self eval: "(if #f 1 2)") equals: 2

  testIfNoElse => self assert: (self eval: "(if #f 1)") isNil

  // --- Cond ---
  testCond =>
    self assert: (self eval: "(cond (#f 1) (#t 2) (else 3))") equals: 2

  testCondElse => self assert: (self eval: "(cond (#f 1) (else 99))") equals: 99

  // --- And / Or ---
  testAndAllTrue => self assert: (self eval: "(and 1 2 3)") equals: 3

  testAndShortCircuit => self assert: (self eval: "(and #f 2)") equals: false

  testOrFirstTrue => self assert: (self eval: "(or 1 2)") equals: 1

  testOrAllFalse => self assert: (self eval: "(or #f #f)") equals: false

  // --- Let ---
  testLet => self assert: (self eval: "(let ((x 3) (y 4)) (+ x y))") equals: 7

  // --- Printer ---
  testEvalString => self assert: (self eval: """hello""") equals: "hello"

  testPrintString =>
    self assert: (self evalStr: """hello""") equals: """hello"""

  testPrintInt => self assert: (self evalStr: "42") equals: "42"

  testPrintTrue => self assert: (self evalStr: "#t") equals: "#t"

  testPrintFalse => self assert: (self evalStr: "#f") equals: "#f"

  testPrintNil =>
    printer := SchemePrinter new
    self assert: (printer print: nil) equals: "()"

  testPrintList => self assert: (self evalStr: "(list 1 2 3)") equals: "(1 2 3)"

  // --- Closures ---
  testClosure =>
    reader := SchemeReader new
    ev := SchemeEval new
    env := ev defaultEnv
    ev eval: (reader read: "(define make-adder (lambda (n) (lambda (x) (+ n x))))") in: env
    ev eval: (reader read: "(define add5 (make-adder 5))") in: env
    self assert: (ev eval: (reader read: "(add5 10)") in: env) equals: 15

  // --- Error handling ---
  testNonProcedureError =>
    result := [self eval: "(1 + 2)"] on: Error do: [:e | e message]
    self assert: result equals: "Not a procedure: 1"

  testNonProcedureErrorInteger =>
    result := [self eval: "(42 1 2)"] on: Error do: [:e | e message]
    self assert: result equals: "Not a procedure: 42"

  // --- Cycle guard ---
  testSetParentCycleGuard =>
    ev := SchemeEval new
    env := ev defaultEnv
    result := [env setParent: env] on: Error do: [:e | e message]
    self assert: result equals: "Cycle detected: environment cannot be its own parent"

  // --- Recursion ---
  testFactorial =>
    reader := SchemeReader new
    ev := SchemeEval new
    env := ev defaultEnv
    ev eval: (reader read: "(define fact (lambda (n) (if (= n 0) 1 (* n (fact (- n 1))))))") in: env
    self assert: (ev eval: (reader read: "(fact 5)") in: env) equals: 120
entries.explanation: SchemeTest — BUnit tests for the Scheme metacircular evaluator  Run interactively in the REPL:   > :load src/scheme/symbol.bt   > :load src/scheme/reader.bt   > :load src/scheme/env.bt   > :load src/scheme/lambda.bt   > :load src/scheme/eval.bt   > :load src/scheme/printer.bt   > :load test/scheme_test.bt   > SchemeTest runAll

entries.title: ActorConditionalMutationsTest

entries.title: ActorCoordinationTest
entries.source:
// BT-898: Tests actor-to-actor coordination with sync-by-default dispatch
// bus notify: calls receive: on the collector synchronously — when notify:
// returns, the collector has already processed the event

TestCase subclass: ActorCoordinationTest

  testSyncEventDelivery =>
    col := EventCollector spawn
    bus := EventBus spawn
    bus subscribe: col
    bus notify: "hello"
    // notify: is synchronous; no barriers needed
    self assert: col eventCount equals: 1

  // Multiple events delivered in order
  testMultipleEventsDelivered =>
    col := EventCollector spawn
    bus := EventBus spawn
    bus subscribe: col; notify: "first"; notify: "second"; notify: "third"
    self assert: col eventCount equals: 3
    self assert: col events equals: #("first", "second", "third")
entries.explanation: BT-898: Tests actor-to-actor coordination with sync-by-default dispatch bus notify: calls receive: on the collector synchronously — when notify: returns, the collector has already processed the event

entries.title: ActorDeadlockTest
entries.explanation: Tests that mutual actor calls (A calls B, B calls back A) produce a clear #timeout error after the default 5000ms gen_server:call timeout  NOTE: This test intentionally triggers a 5000ms deadlock timeout It verifies deadlock detection behavior per ADR 0043

entries.title: ActorEarlyReturnUnpackTest
entries.explanation: BT-1432: Tests early return (^) with super/tier2/self-dispatch values in Actor methods. These return tuples that must be unpacked correctly by the early return handler.

entries.title: ActorInheritedSelfsendTest
entries.source:
// Tests for actor self-sends to inherited methods (cross-file inheritance).
// InheritingCounter extends Counter (defined in separate file) and
// self-sends to getValue/increment which are defined on Counter.

TestCase subclass: ActorInheritedSelfsendTest

  // #1: Direct self-send to inherited method
  testDirectSelfsendToInherited =>
    c := InheritingCounter spawn
    c increment
    c increment
    self assert: c getValueViaSelfsend equals: 2

  // #1: Self-send to inherited method inside collect: block
  testSelfsendToInheritedInBlock =>
    c := InheritingCounter spawn
    result := c collectWithInheritedSend: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: result equals: #(1, 2, 3)

  // #1+#2: Self-send to inherited method + own state mutation
  testInheritedSelfsendWithOwnState =>
    c := InheritingCounter spawn
    c incrementAndLog
    c incrementAndLog
    c incrementAndLog
    self assert: c getValue equals: 3

  // #2: Access inherited state field directly via self.value
  testAccessInheritedStateField =>
    c := InheritingCounter spawn
    c increment
    c increment
    c increment
    self assert: c getValueDirect equals: 3
entries.explanation: Tests for actor self-sends to inherited methods (cross-file inheritance). InheritingCounter extends Counter (defined in separate file) and self-sends to getValue/increment which are defined on Counter.

entries.title: ActorInitializeTest
entries.explanation: Tests for Actor initialize lifecycle hook.  Fixtures: stdlib/test/fixtures/initialize_counter.bt

entries.title: ActorLifecycleTest
entries.explanation: BT-172, BT-397: Tests actor lifecycle — isAlive, stop, idempotent stop, and error behaviour when sending to a stopped actor

entries.title: ActorLocalMutationsTest
entries.source:
// BT-598/BT-1224: Tests that local variables inside actor method blocks are correctly
// mutated and scoped — covering do:, inject:into:, to:do:, and whileFalse:

TestCase subclass: ActorLocalMutationsTest

  testLocalMutationsInActorBlocks =>
    a := LocalMutationActor spawn
    self assert: (a sumWithDo: #(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)) equals: 15
    b := LocalMutationActor spawn
    self assert: (b sumWithInject: #(10, 20, 30)) equals: #{
      #count => 3,
      #result => 60
    }
    c := LocalMutationActor spawn
    self assert: (c countToN: 5) equals: 5
    d := LocalMutationActor spawn
    self assert: (d sumRange: 5) equals: 15
    e := LocalMutationActor spawn
    self assert: (e countDownFrom: 3) equals: 3
    h := LocalMutationActor spawn
    self assert: (h countUpWithWhileFalseFrom: 3) equals: 3
    i := LocalMutationActor spawn
    self assert: (i sumEvens: 10) equals: 30
    f := LocalMutationActor spawn
    self assert: (f processWithTracking: #(5, 10, 15)) equals: #{
      #localCount => 3,
      #log => 30
    }
    g := FieldMutationAccumulator spawn
    self assert: (g processWithLocal: #(10, 20, 30)) equals: #{
      #localSum => 3,
      #sum => 60
    }

  // BT-1224: Regression test — block-local variable assigned and read within the same
  // do: block iteration must not cause {unbound_var,'Doubled',{dispatch,4}} compile error.
  // The fix: compute_threaded_locals_for_loop uses captured_reads (not local_reads) for Actor,
  // so block-locally-defined vars are never packed into the pre-loop StateAcc map.
  testHybridParamsCountedLoop =>
    // BT-1326: counted loop with both local var AND field mutations uses hybrid mode.
    // Locals as direct fun params, actor State as explicit parameter.
    a := LocalMutationActor spawn
    r := a sumAndCountToN: 5
    self assert: (r at: #sum) equals: 15
    self assert: (r at: #log) equals: 5
    self assert: (a getLog) equals: 5
    b := LocalMutationActor spawn
    r2 := b sumAndCountTimesRepeat: 4
    self assert: (r2 at: #sum) equals: 4
    self assert: (r2 at: #log) equals: 4
    self assert: (b getLog) equals: 4

  // BT-1342: whileTrue: with both local var AND field mutations uses full-extract mode.
  testFullExtractWhileTrue =>
    a := LocalMutationActor spawn
    r := a sumAndCountWhileTrue: 5
    self assert: (r at: #sum) equals: 15
    self assert: (r at: #log) equals: 5
    self assert: (a getLog) equals: 5

  // BT-1342: Multiple field mutations in a counted loop (both step and log mutated).
  testMultiFieldMutation =>
    a := LocalMutationActor spawn
    r := a multiFieldMutation: 4
    // step = 1+2+3+4 = 10, log = 4
    self assert: (r at: #step) equals: 10
    self assert: (r at: #log) equals: 4

  testHybridReadonlyFieldOptimization =>
    // BT-1326: Read-only field (self.step=1) is pre-extracted before the letrec.
    // Verifies the optimization produces correct results at runtime.
    a := LocalMutationActor spawn
    r := a sumWithStep: 5
    // sum = 1+1+1+1+1 = 5 (step=1 each iteration), log = 5 iterations
    self assert: (r at: #sum) equals: 5
    self assert: (r at: #log) equals: 5
    self assert: (a getLog) equals: 5

  testBlockLocalVarInDoBody =>
    a := LocalMutationActor spawn
    self assert: (a doubleAndSum: #(1, 2, 3)) equals: 12

  // BT-1276: select:/reject: with local mutation should use tuple-acc and return correct results.
  testSelectWithLocalMutation =>
    a := LocalMutationActor spawn
    r := a selectPositiveWithCount: #(-1, 2, -3, 4, 5)
    self assert: (r at: #result) equals: #(2, 4, 5)
    self assert: (r at: #count) equals: 5

  testRejectWithLocalMutation =>
    a := LocalMutationActor spawn
    r := a rejectNegativeWithCount: #(-1, 2, -3, 4, 5)
    self assert: (r at: #result) equals: #(2, 4, 5)
    self assert: (r at: #count) equals: 5

  // BT-1329: Nested list op inside counted loop — direct-params with expanded tuple.
  testNestedDoInsideCountedLoop =>
    a := LocalMutationActor spawn
    // 3 items × 2 iterations = 6 total increments
    self assert: (a countItemsInRangeWithDo: #(10, 20, 30) times: 2) equals: 6

  testNestedInjectInsideCountedLoop =>
    a := LocalMutationActor spawn
    // inject accumulates: iteration 1: 0+1+2+3=6, iteration 2: 6+1+2+3=12
    self assert: (a sumItemsNTimes: #(1, 2, 3) times: 2) equals: 12

  testNestedCollectInsideCountedLoop =>
    a := LocalMutationActor spawn
    r := a collectAndCountNTimes: #(1, 2) times: 2
    // Last iteration (i=2): collect: [:x | x * 2] => #(2, 4)
    self assert: (r at: #result) equals: #(2, 4)
    // 2 items × 2 iterations = 4 count increments
    self assert: (r at: #count) equals: 4
entries.explanation: BT-598/BT-1224: Tests that local variables inside actor method blocks are correctly mutated and scoped — covering do:, inject:into:, to:do:, and whileFalse:

entries.title: ActorMessageChainingTest
entries.explanation: Tests that chained message sends on actors work correctly with sync-by-default dispatch. When `actor method1 method2` is written, method1 returns the value directly (sync gen_server:call), and method2 is dispatched on that value

entries.title: ActorMonitorTest
entries.source:
// BT-1442: Tests for Actor monitor/pid/onExit: process monitoring API

TestCase subclass: ActorMonitorTest

  testPidReturnsAPid =>
    counter := Counter spawn
    rawPid := counter pid
    self assert: rawPid class equals: Pid
    self assert: rawPid isAlive
    counter stop

  testMonitorReturnsReference =>
    counter := Counter spawn
    ref := counter monitor
    self assert: ref class equals: Reference
    ref demonitor
    counter stop

  testDemonitorCancelsMonitor =>
    counter := Counter spawn
    ref := counter monitor
    result := ref demonitor
    self assert: result
    counter stop

  testOnExitCallsBlockOnActorDeath =>
    counter := Counter spawn
    exitCounter := AtomicCounter new: #actorMonitorTestExit
    counter onExit: [:_reason | exitCounter increment]
    counter stop
    // Poll with bounded wait instead of fixed sleep (avoids timing flakiness)
    50 timesRepeat: [exitCounter value < 1 ifTrue: [Timer sleep: 10]]
    self assert: exitCounter value equals: 1
    exitCounter delete

  testMultipleOnExitCallbacks =>
    // Multiple onExit: callbacks can be registered on the same actor
    counter := Counter spawn
    exitCounter := AtomicCounter new: #actorMonitorTestMulti
    counter onExit: [:_reason | exitCounter increment]
    counter onExit: [:_reason | exitCounter increment]
    counter stop
    // Poll with bounded wait instead of fixed sleep
    50 timesRepeat: [exitCounter value < 2 ifTrue: [Timer sleep: 10]]
    self assert: exitCounter value equals: 2
    exitCounter delete

  testRespondsToMonitorMethods =>
    counter := Counter spawn
    self assert: (counter respondsTo: #pid)
    self assert: (counter respondsTo: #monitor)
    self assert: (counter respondsTo: #onExit:)
    counter stop

  testErlangMonitorWithActorProxy =>
    // Direct Erlang FFI monitor should work with actor proxy (auto-coercion)
    counter := Counter spawn
    ref := Erlang erlang monitor: #process arg: counter
    self assert: ref class equals: Reference
    Erlang erlang demonitor: ref
    counter stop
entries.explanation: BT-1442: Tests for Actor monitor/pid/onExit: process monitoring API

entries.title: ActorNonLocalReturnTest
entries.source:
// BT-761: Tests non-local return (^) inside block arguments in Actor subclass
// methods — empty/non-empty, classify, local var storage, mutation, recursion,
// on:do: with NLR, and NLR with persistent state

TestCase subclass: ActorNonLocalReturnTest

  testNonLocalReturnInActorBlocks =>
    a := ActorNlrBasic spawn
    self assert: (a test: #()) equals: "empty"
    self assert: (a test: #(1)) equals: "not empty"
    self assert: (a classify: 5) equals: "positive"
    self assert: (a classify: -1) equals: "non-positive"
    self assert: (a classify: 0) equals: "non-positive"
    self assert: (a checkFalse: #()) equals: "empty"
    self assert: (a checkFalse: #(1)) equals: "not empty"
    b := ActorNlrLocalVar spawn
    b store: "x" val: 42
    self assert: (b fetch: "x") equals: 42
    self assert: (b fetch: "y") equals: "not found"
    c := ActorNlrComputed spawn
    self assert: (c doubleIfPositive: 5) equals: 10
    self assert: (c doubleIfPositive: -1) equals: 0
    d := ActorNlrRecursive spawn
    self assert: (d countDown: 3) equals: "done"
    self assert: (d countDown: 0) equals: "done"
    e := ActorNlrOnDo spawn
    self assert: (e test: #()) equals: "early"
    self assert: (e test: #(1)) equals: "not early"
    f := ActorNlrMutate spawn
    // count starts at 0; incAndCheck: 1 sets count=1, 1>=1 triggers NLR
    self assert: (f incAndCheck: 1) equals: 1
    // count is now 1 (mutation was preserved); incAndCheck: 5 sets count=2, 2<5 falls through
    self assert: (f incAndCheck: 5) equals: "not yet"
    // count is now 2; incAndCheck: 3 sets count=3, 3>=3 triggers NLR
    self assert: (f incAndCheck: 3) equals: 3
    // count is now 3 (verified mutation persisted across NLR and non-NLR paths)
    self assert: (f incAndCheck: 10) equals: "not yet"
entries.explanation: BT-761: Tests non-local return (^) inside block arguments in Actor subclass methods — empty/non-empty, classify, local var storage, mutation, recursion, on:do: with NLR, and NLR with persistent state

entries.source:
// BT-904: Tests that collect:, select:, reject:, and do: with self-sends
// work correctly in actor methods (state threading through blocks)

TestCase subclass: ActorSelfSendCollectTest

  testCollectWithSelfSend =>
    c := ActorCollectCounter spawn
    result := c doubleAll: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: result equals: #(2, 4, 6)
    self assert: c getCount equals: 3

  testCollectWithFieldMutation =>
    c := ActorCollectCounter spawn
    result := c doubleAllWithFieldMutation: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: result equals: #(2, 4, 6)
    self assert: c getCount equals: 3

  testCollectWithSelfSendImplicitReturn =>
    c := ActorCollectCounter spawn
    result := c doubleAllImplicit: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: result equals: #(2, 4, 6)
    self assert: c getCount equals: 3

  testDoWithSelfSend =>
    c := ActorCollectCounter spawn
    result := c countAll: #(10, 20, 30)
    self assert: result equals: 3
    self assert: c getCount equals: 3

  testSelectWithSelfSend =>
    c := ActorCollectCounter spawn
    result := c selectPositive: #(-1, 2, -3, 4)
    self assert: result equals: #(2, 4)
    self assert: c getCount equals: 4

  testRejectWithSelfSend =>
    c := ActorCollectCounter spawn
    result := c rejectNegative: #(-1, 2, -3, 4)
    self assert: result equals: #(2, 4)
    self assert: c getCount equals: 4

  testCollectWithSelfSendEmptyList =>
    c := ActorCollectCounter spawn
    result := c doubleAll: #()
    self assert: result equals: #()
    self assert: c getCount equals: 0

  testSelectWithSelfSendEmptyList =>
    c := ActorCollectCounter spawn
    result := c selectPositive: #()
    self assert: result equals: #()
    self assert: c getCount equals: 0

entries.explanation: BT-916: Tests for self.slot := assignment in actor methods Verifies that state is correctly threaded through:   - multiple slot assignments in one method   - slot assignment inside ifTrue:ifFalse: conditional   - slot assignment inside block (do:, collect:)   - slot assignment with early return (^)  Compile-time checks (value type error, withSlot: bypass warning) are validated by Rust unit tests in semantic_analysis/validators.rs (BT-914) and by the E2E @load-error test in tests/e2e/cases/actor_slot.bt

entries.title: ActorTerminateTest
entries.source:
// Tests for Actor terminate: lifecycle hook.
//
// Fixtures: stdlib/test/fixtures/terminate_actor.bt
//           stdlib/test/fixtures/failing_terminate_actor.bt

TestCase subclass: ActorTerminateTest

  // === Basic terminate protocol ===
  testTerminateCalledOnStop =>
    table := Ets new: #terminateOnStop type: #set
    actor := TerminateActor spawnWith: #{#tableName => #terminateOnStop}
    actor stop
    self assert: (table at: #called) equals: true
    table delete

  testTerminateReceivesNormalReason =>
    table := Ets new: #terminateReason type: #set
    actor := TerminateActor spawnWith: #{#tableName => #terminateReason}
    actor stop
    self assert: (table at: #reason) equals: #normal
    table delete

  testTerminateCanReadActorState =>
    table := Ets new: #terminateState type: #set
    actor := TerminateActor spawnWith: #{
      #tableName => #terminateState,
      #value => 42
    }
    actor stop
    self assert: (table at: #value) equals: 42
    table delete

  testTerminateCanReadMutatedState =>
    table := Ets new: #terminateMutated type: #set
    actor := TerminateActor spawnWith: #{#tableName => #terminateMutated}
    actor setValue: 99
    actor stop
    self assert: (table at: #value) equals: 99
    table delete

  // === Kill bypasses terminate ===
  testTerminateNotCalledOnKill =>
    table := Ets new: #terminateKill type: #set
    actor := TerminateActor spawnWith: #{#tableName => #terminateKill}
    // Unlink so the kill signal doesn't propagate to the test process
    Erlang erlang unlink: actor pid
    actor kill
    // Poll until the actor is dead
    50 timesRepeat: [actor isAlive ifTrue: [Timer sleep: 10]]
    self deny: actor isAlive
    // terminate: should NOT have been called
    self assert: (table at: #called) equals: nil
    table delete

  // === Exception handling ===
  testTerminateExceptionDoesNotPreventShutdown =>
    actor := FailingTerminateActor spawn
    self assert: actor isAlive
    actor stop
    self deny: actor isAlive

  // === Actors without terminate ===
  testActorWithoutTerminateStopsCleanly =>
    counter := Counter spawn
    self assert: counter isAlive
    counter stop
    self deny: counter isAlive

  // === respondsTo: ===
  testRespondsToTerminate =>
    actor := TerminateActor spawn
    self assert: (actor respondsTo: #terminate:)
    actor stop
entries.explanation: Tests for Actor terminate: lifecycle hook.  Fixtures: stdlib/test/fixtures/terminate_actor.bt           stdlib/test/fixtures/failing_terminate_actor.bt

// ADR 0014 Phase 2: Tests basic arithmetic operations — addition, subtraction,
// negation, equality, and denial — as a canonical BUnit TestCase example

TestCase subclass: ArithmeticTest

  testAddition => self assert: (1 + 2) equals: 3

  testSubtraction => self assert: (5 - 3) equals: 2

  testNegation => self assert: (0 - 1) equals: -1

  testEquality => self assert: (2 + 2 == 4)

entries.title: ArrayTest
entries.source:
// BUnit tests for the Array collection type (BT-822)

TestCase subclass: ArrayTest

  testClassAndCreation =>
    self assert: #[1, 2, 3] class equals: Array
    self assert: #[] class equals: Array

  testSizeAndIsEmpty =>
    self assert: #[] size equals: 0
    self assert: #[1, 2, 3] size equals: 3
    self assert: #[] isEmpty
    self deny: #[1] isEmpty
    self assert: #[1] isNotEmpty

  testAtIndexedAccess =>
    a := #[10, 20, 30]
    self assert: (a at: 1) equals: 10
    self assert: (a at: 2) equals: 20
    self assert: (a at: 3) equals: 30

  testAtPutFunctionalUpdate =>
    a := #[1, 2, 3]
    b := a at: 2 put: 99
    self assert: (b at: 2) equals: 99
    // Original is unchanged (immutable)
    self assert: (a at: 2) equals: 2

  testIncludes =>
    a := #[1, 2, 3]
    self assert: (a includes: 2)
    self deny: (a includes: 9)
    self deny: (#[] includes: 1)

  testDo =>
    // do: returns nil
    self assert: (#[1, 2, 3] do: [:x | x + 1]) equals: nil
    // do: on empty array also returns nil
    self assert: (#[] do: [:x | x]) equals: nil

  testCollect =>
    self assert: (#[1, 2, 3] collect: [:x | x * 2]) equals: #[2, 4, 6]
    self assert: (#[] collect: [:x | x * 2]) equals: #[]
    // collect: preserves order
    self assert: (#[3, 1, 2] collect: [:x | x + 10]) equals: #[13, 11, 12]

  testCollectReturnsArray =>
    result := #[1, 2, 3] collect: [:x | x * 2]
    self assert: result class equals: Array

  testSelect =>
    self assert: (#[1, 2, 3, 4] select: [:x | x > 2]) equals: #[3, 4]
    self assert: (#[1, 2, 3] select: [:x | x > 10]) equals: #[]
    self assert: (#[1, 2, 3] select: [:x | true]) equals: #[1, 2, 3]

  testSelectReturnsArray =>
    result := #[1, 2, 3, 4] select: [:x | x > 2]
    self assert: result class equals: Array

  testReject =>
    self assert: (#[1, 2, 3, 4] reject: [:x | x > 2]) equals: #[1, 2]
    self assert: (#[1, 2, 3] reject: [:x | false]) equals: #[1, 2, 3]

  testInjectInto =>
    self assert: (#[1, 2, 3] inject: 0 into: [:acc :x | acc + x]) equals: 6
    self assert: (#[1, 2, 3, 4] inject: 1 into: [:acc :x | acc * x]) equals: 24
    self assert: (#[] inject: 0 into: [:acc :x | acc + x]) equals: 0

  testPrintString =>
    self assert: #[1, 2, 3] printString equals: "#[1, 2, 3]"
    self assert: #[] printString equals: "#[]"
    self assert: #["a", "b"] printString equals: "#[""a"", ""b""]"

  testWithAll =>
    a := Array withAll: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: a class equals: Array
    self assert: a size equals: 3
    self assert: (a at: 1) equals: 1
    self assert: (a at: 3) equals: 3

  testEmptyArray =>
    self assert: #[] size equals: 0
    self assert: #[] isEmpty
    self assert: #[] printString equals: "#[]"

  testLiteralEquality =>
    self assert: #[1, 2, 3] equals: #[1, 2, 3]
    @expect dnu
    self deny: (#[1, 2, 3] =:= #[1, 2, 4])
    @expect dnu
    self deny: (#[1, 2] =:= #[1, 2, 3])
entries.explanation: BUnit tests for the Array collection type (BT-822)

entries.title: AtomicCounterTest
entries.source:
// BUnit tests for AtomicCounter class (BT-1250)

TestCase subclass: AtomicCounterTest
  field: counter = nil

  setUp =>
    // Defensively clean up any leftover table from a crashed previous run
    [
      (AtomicCounter named: #atomicCounterBUnitTest) delete
    ] on: Error do: [:e | nil]
    self withCounter: (AtomicCounter new: #atomicCounterBUnitTest)

  tearDown =>
    self.counter isNil ifFalse: [[self.counter delete] on: Error do: [:e | nil]]

  testStartsAtZero => self assert: self.counter value equals: 0

  testIncrementReturnsNewValue => self assert: self.counter increment equals: 1

  testIncrementAccumulates =>
    self.counter increment
    self.counter increment
    self assert: self.counter increment equals: 3

  testIncrementByN => self assert: (self.counter incrementBy: 5) equals: 5

  testIncrementByAccumulates =>
    self.counter incrementBy: 3
    self assert: (self.counter incrementBy: 7) equals: 10

  testDecrementReturnsNewValue => self assert: self.counter decrement equals: -1

  testDecrementByN => self assert: (self.counter decrementBy: 4) equals: -4

  testValueReflectsIncrements =>
    self.counter increment
    self.counter increment
    self assert: self.counter value equals: 2

  testResetSetsToZero =>
    self.counter incrementBy: 42
    self.counter reset
    self assert: self.counter value equals: 0

  testResetReturnsNil =>
    self.counter increment
    result := self.counter reset
    self assert: result equals: nil

  testNamedLookup =>
    other := AtomicCounter named: #atomicCounterBUnitTest
    self.counter increment
    self assert: other value equals: 1

  testNewAlreadyExistsRaisesError =>
    self should: [
      AtomicCounter new: #atomicCounterBUnitTest
    ] raise: #already_exists

  testNamedNotFoundRaisesError =>
    self should: [AtomicCounter named: #noSuchCounterXyz] raise: #not_found

  testDeleteMakesCounterStale =>
    self.counter delete
    // Creating a fresh counter with the same name succeeds
    fresh := AtomicCounter new: #atomicCounterBUnitTest
    self assert: fresh value equals: 0
    fresh delete

  testIncrementAndDecrementCombined =>
    self.counter incrementBy: 10
    self.counter decrementBy: 3
    self assert: self.counter value equals: 7
entries.explanation: BUnit tests for AtomicCounter class (BT-1250)

entries.title: BagTest
entries.source:
// BUnit tests for Bag — immutable multiset collection (BT-1249)

TestCase subclass: BagTest

  testCreationClass => self assert: Bag new class equals: Bag

  testNewIsEmpty =>
    self assert: Bag new isEmpty
    self assert: Bag new size equals: 0

  testWithAll =>
    b := Bag withAll: #(1, 2, 1, 3)
    self assert: b class equals: Bag
    self assert: b size equals: 4

  testOccurrencesOf =>
    b := Bag withAll: #(1, 1, 2)
    self assert: (b occurrencesOf: 1) equals: 2
    self assert: (b occurrencesOf: 2) equals: 1
    self assert: (b occurrencesOf: 3) equals: 0

  testIncludes =>
    b := Bag withAll: #(1, 2)
    self assert: (b includes: 1)
    self assert: (b includes: 2)
    self deny: (b includes: 3)

  testAdd =>
    b := Bag new add: 1
    self assert: (b occurrencesOf: 1) equals: 1
    b2 := b add: 1
    self assert: (b2 occurrencesOf: 1) equals: 2
    // Original unchanged
    self assert: (b occurrencesOf: 1) equals: 1

  testAddWithCount =>
    b := (Bag new) add: 1 withCount: 3
    self assert: (b occurrencesOf: 1) equals: 3
    self assert: b size equals: 3

  testRemoveOneOccurrence =>
    b := Bag withAll: #(1, 1, 2)
    b2 := b remove: 1
    self assert: (b2 occurrencesOf: 1) equals: 1
    self assert: b2 size equals: 2

  testRemoveLastOccurrence =>
    b := Bag withAll: #(1, 2)
    b2 := b remove: 1
    self deny: (b2 includes: 1)
    self assert: b2 size equals: 1

  testRemoveAbsentIsNoop =>
    b := Bag withAll: #(1, 2)
    b2 := b remove: 3
    self assert: b2 size equals: 2

  testSizeIsTotal =>
    b := Bag withAll: #(1, 1, 1, 2, 2)
    self assert: b size equals: 5

  testDoIteratesEachOccurrence =>
    b := Bag withAll: #(1, 1, 2)
    result := Bag new
    b do: [:x | result := result add: x]
    self assert: result size equals: 3
    self assert: (result occurrencesOf: 1) equals: 2
    self assert: (result occurrencesOf: 2) equals: 1

  testInjectInto =>
    b := Bag withAll: #(1, 2, 3)
    sum := b inject: 0 into: [:acc :x | acc + x]
    self assert: sum equals: 6

  testInjectIntoWithDuplicates =>
    b := Bag withAll: #(1, 1, 2)
    sum := b inject: 0 into: [:acc :x | acc + x]
    self assert: sum equals: 4

  testCollect =>
    b := Bag withAll: #(1, 2)
    b2 := b collect: [:x | x * 2]
    self assert: b2 class equals: Bag
    self assert: b2 size equals: 2
    self assert: (b2 occurrencesOf: 2) equals: 1
    self assert: (b2 occurrencesOf: 4) equals: 1

  testSelect =>
    b := Bag withAll: #(1, 2, 3, 4)
    b2 := b select: [:x | x > 2]
    self assert: b2 class equals: Bag
    self assert: b2 size equals: 2
    self assert: (b2 occurrencesOf: 3) equals: 1
    self assert: (b2 occurrencesOf: 4) equals: 1

  testReject =>
    b := Bag withAll: #(1, 2, 3, 4)
    b2 := b reject: [:x | x > 2]
    self assert: b2 class equals: Bag
    self assert: b2 size equals: 2
    self assert: (b2 occurrencesOf: 1) equals: 1
    self assert: (b2 occurrencesOf: 2) equals: 1

  testPrintString =>
    self assert: Bag new printString notNil
    self assert: (Bag withAll: #(1, 2)) printString notNil

  testAddWithCountZeroIsNoop =>
    b := (Bag new) add: 1 withCount: 0
    self assert: b isEmpty

  testAddWithCountNegativeRaisesError =>
    self should: [(Bag new) add: 1 withCount: -1] raise: #user_error
entries.explanation: BUnit tests for Bag — immutable multiset collection (BT-1249)

entries.title: BinaryTest
entries.source:
// Stdlib tests for Binary class — instance methods, Collection protocol,
// byte access, slicing, conversion, and serialization (BT-1594, ADR 0069 Phase 3a)
//
// Note: On BEAM, all binaries dispatch as String at runtime. Binary-only
// methods (byteSize, byteAt:, part:size:, concat:, toBytes, asString,
// asStringUnchecked) work correctly. String-overridden methods (size, at:,
// do:, printString) and inherited Collection methods use grapheme-level
// semantics via String's dispatch.

TestCase subclass: BinaryTest

  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  // Serialization (class methods — existing)
  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  testSerializeAndDeserializeInteger =>
    bin := Binary serialize: 42
    self assert: (Binary deserialize: bin) equals: 42

  testSerializeAndDeserializeString =>
    bin := Binary serialize: "hello"
    self assert: (Binary deserialize: bin) equals: "hello"

  testSerializeAndDeserializeArray =>
    bin := Binary serialize: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: (Binary deserialize: bin) equals: #(1, 2, 3)

  testSerializeAndDeserializeNil =>
    bin := Binary serialize: nil
    self assert: (Binary deserialize: bin) equals: nil

  testSerializeAndDeserializeBoolean =>
    binTrue := Binary serialize: true
    @expect type
    self assert: (Binary deserialize: binTrue)
    binFalse := Binary serialize: false
    @expect type
    self deny: (Binary deserialize: binFalse)

  testSerializeProducesBinary =>
    bin := Binary serialize: 42
    self assert: bin byteSize > 0

  testDeserializeTypeError =>
    self should: [Binary deserialize: 42] raise: #type_error

  testClassIdentity =>
    // BT-802 (ADR 0036): class on class returns a real metaclass object
    self assert: (Binary class) isMeta equals: true

  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  // fromIolist: and fromBytes: (class methods)
  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  testFromIolist =>
    result := Binary fromIolist: #("hello", " ", "world")
    self assert: result equals: "hello world"

  testFromIolistEmpty =>
    result := Binary fromIolist: #()
    self assert: result equals: ""

  testFromBytes =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(104, 101, 108, 108, 111)
    self assert: bin equals: "hello"

  testFromBytesEmpty =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #()
    self assert: bin equals: ""

  testFromBytesSingleByte =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(65)
    self assert: bin equals: "A"

  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  // Collection protocol: size, isEmpty, isNotEmpty
  // At runtime, size dispatches to String (grapheme count).
  // For ASCII data, grapheme count == byte count.
  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  testSize =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(65, 66, 67, 68, 69)
    self assert: bin size equals: 5

  testSizeEmpty =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #()
    self assert: bin size equals: 0

  testSizeOfSerializedValue =>
    bin := Binary serialize: 42
    self assert: bin byteSize > 0

  testIsEmpty => self assert: (Binary fromBytes: #()) isEmpty

  testIsEmptyFalse => self deny: (Binary fromBytes: #(1)) isEmpty

  testIsNotEmpty => self assert: (Binary fromBytes: #(1, 2)) isNotEmpty

  testIsNotEmptyFalse => self deny: (Binary fromBytes: #()) isNotEmpty

  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  // Collection protocol: do:
  // At runtime, dispatches to String's do: (grapheme iteration).
  // Elements are single-character strings, not byte integers.
  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  testDoIteratesGraphemes =>
    // At runtime, do: goes through String's grapheme iteration
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(65, 66, 67)
    collected := List new
    bin do: [:ch | collected := collected add: ch]
    self assert: collected equals: #("A", "B", "C")

  testDoOnEmptyBinary =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #()
    count := 0
    bin do: [:ch | count := count + 1]
    self assert: count equals: 0

  testDoReturnValue =>
    // do: returns nil
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(65, 66)
    result := bin do: [:ch | ch]
    self assert: result equals: nil

  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  // Collection protocol: includes:
  // Uses String's grapheme iteration; compares grapheme strings.
  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  testIncludesGrapheme =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(65, 66, 67)
    // At runtime, includes: compares graphemes (single-char strings)
    self assert: (bin includes: "B")

  testIncludesGraphemeNotFound =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(65, 66, 67)
    self deny: (bin includes: "Z")

  testIncludesOnEmpty =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #()
    self deny: (bin includes: "A")

  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  // Collection protocol: collect:
  // Uses String's grapheme iteration; returns String via species.
  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  testCollectTransformsGraphemes =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(65, 66, 67)
    // collect: iterates graphemes and returns String (via species)
    result := bin collect: [:ch | ch]
    self assert: result equals: "ABC"

  testCollectOnEmpty =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #()
    result := bin collect: [:ch | ch]
    self assert: result equals: ""

  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  // Collection protocol: select:, reject:
  // Uses String's grapheme iteration; returns String via species.
  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  testSelectFiltersGraphemes =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(65, 66, 67, 68, 69)
    // Selects graphemes matching condition
    result := bin select: [:ch | ch /= "C"]
    self assert: result equals: "ABDE"

  testSelectNoneMatch =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(65, 66, 67)
    result := bin select: [:ch | ch =:= "Z"]
    self assert: result equals: ""

  testRejectFiltersGraphemes =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(65, 66, 67, 68, 69)
    result := bin reject: [:ch | ch =:= "C"]
    self assert: result equals: "ABDE"

  testRejectNoneMatch =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(65, 66, 67)
    result := bin reject: [:ch | ch =:= "Z"]
    self assert: result equals: "ABC"

  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  // Collection protocol: inject:into:
  // Uses String's grapheme iteration; accumulates grapheme strings.
  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  testInjectIntoConcatsGraphemes =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(65, 66, 67)
    result := bin inject: "" into: [:acc :ch | acc ++ ch]
    self assert: result equals: "ABC"

  testInjectIntoOnEmpty =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #()
    result := bin inject: "start" into: [:acc :ch | acc ++ ch]
    self assert: result equals: "start"

  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  // Collection protocol: detect:
  // Uses String's grapheme iteration.
  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  testDetectFindsFirstMatch =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(65, 66, 67, 68, 69)
    result := bin detect: [:ch | ch =:= "C"]
    self assert: result equals: "C"

  testDetectReturnsNilWhenNotFound =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(65, 66, 67)
    result := bin detect: [:ch | ch =:= "Z"]
    self assert: result equals: nil

  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  // Collection protocol: anySatisfy:, allSatisfy:
  // Uses String's grapheme iteration.
  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  testAnySatisfyTrue =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(65, 66, 67)
    self assert: (bin anySatisfy: [:ch | ch =:= "B"])

  testAnySatisfyFalse =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(65, 66, 67)
    self deny: (bin anySatisfy: [:ch | ch =:= "Z"])

  testAllSatisfyTrue =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(65, 66, 67)
    self assert: (bin allSatisfy: [:ch | ch size > 0])

  testAllSatisfyFalse =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(65, 66, 67)
    self deny: (bin allSatisfy: [:ch | ch =:= "A"])

  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  // Byte access: at: (1-based)
  // At runtime, dispatches to String's at: (grapheme access).
  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  testAtReturnsGrapheme =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(104, 101, 108)
    // String's at: returns grapheme string
    self assert: (bin at: 1) equals: "h"

  testAtLastGrapheme =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(104, 101, 108)
    self assert: (bin at: 3) equals: "l"

  testAtOutOfBoundsHigh =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(1, 2, 3)
    self should: [bin at: 4] raise: #index_out_of_bounds

  testAtOutOfBoundsZero =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(1, 2, 3)
    self should: [bin at: 0] raise: #index_out_of_bounds

  testAtOnEmptyBinary =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #()
    self should: [bin at: 1] raise: #index_out_of_bounds

  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  // Byte access: byteAt: (0-based) — Binary-only, not overridden
  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  testByteAtFirstByte =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(104, 101, 108)
    self assert: (bin byteAt: 0) equals: 104

  testByteAtLastByte =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(104, 101, 108)
    self assert: (bin byteAt: 2) equals: 108

  testByteAtMiddleByte =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(104, 101, 108)
    self assert: (bin byteAt: 1) equals: 101

  testByteAtOutOfBoundsHigh =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(1, 2, 3)
    self should: [bin byteAt: 3] raise: #index_out_of_bounds

  testByteAtOutOfBoundsNegative =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(1, 2, 3)
    self should: [bin byteAt: -1] raise: #index_out_of_bounds

  testByteAtOnEmptyBinary =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #()
    self should: [bin byteAt: 0] raise: #index_out_of_bounds

  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  // byteSize — Binary-only, not overridden by String
  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  testByteSize =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: bin byteSize equals: 3

  testByteSizeEmpty =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #()
    self assert: bin byteSize equals: 0

  testByteSizeOfString => self assert: "hello" byteSize equals: 5

  testByteSizeOfEmptyString => self assert: "" byteSize equals: 0

  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  // part:size: — zero-copy slicing (Binary-only, not overridden)
  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  testPartSlice =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
    result := bin part: 1 size: 3
    self assert: result equals: (Binary fromBytes: #(2, 3, 4))

  testPartFromStart =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
    result := bin part: 0 size: 3
    self assert: result equals: (Binary fromBytes: #(1, 2, 3))

  testPartToEnd =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
    result := bin part: 3 size: 2
    self assert: result equals: (Binary fromBytes: #(4, 5))

  testPartEmptySlice =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(1, 2, 3)
    result := bin part: 0 size: 0
    self assert: result equals: (Binary fromBytes: #())

  testPartFullSlice =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(1, 2, 3)
    result := bin part: 0 size: 3
    self assert: result equals: bin

  testPartOutOfBounds =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(1, 2, 3)
    self should: [bin part: 0 size: 10] raise: #index_out_of_bounds

  testPartNegativeOffset =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(1, 2, 3)
    self should: [bin part: -1 size: 1] raise: #index_out_of_bounds

  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  // concat: — binary concatenation (Binary-only, not overridden)
  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  testConcatTwoBinaries =>
    a := Binary fromBytes: #(1, 2)
    b := Binary fromBytes: #(3, 4)
    result := a concat: b
    self assert: result equals: (Binary fromBytes: #(1, 2, 3, 4))

  testConcatBinaryAndString =>
    // Strings are Binaries, so concat: works
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(104, 105)
    result := bin concat: "!"
    self assert: result equals: "hi!"

  testConcatWithEmptyBinary =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(1, 2, 3)
    empty := Binary fromBytes: #()
    self assert: (bin concat: empty) equals: bin

  testConcatEmptyWithBinary =>
    empty := Binary fromBytes: #()
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: (empty concat: bin) equals: bin

  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  // toBytes and fromBytes: roundtrip
  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  testToBytes =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(104, 101, 108, 108, 111)
    self assert: bin toBytes equals: #(104, 101, 108, 108, 111)

  testToBytesEmpty =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #()
    self assert: bin toBytes equals: #()

  testToBytesFromBytesRoundtrip =>
    bytes := #(0, 127, 255, 42, 1)
    bin := Binary fromBytes: bytes
    self assert: bin toBytes equals: bytes

  testToBytesOnString =>
    self assert: "hello" toBytes equals: #(104, 101, 108, 108, 111)

  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  // asString — UTF-8 validation
  // At runtime, dispatches to String's asString (returns self).
  // Binary's primitive (returns {ok, ...} tuple) is tested via
  // string_binary_inheritance_test.bt.
  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  testAsStringOnBinaryReturnsString =>
    // At runtime, asString dispatches to String's override → returns self
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(104, 101, 108, 108, 111)
    result := bin asString
    self assert: result equals: "hello"

  testAsStringEmptyBinary =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #()
    result := bin asString
    self assert: result equals: ""

  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  // asStringUnchecked — no validation
  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  testAsStringUncheckedValidUtf8 =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(104, 101, 108, 108, 111)
    result := bin asStringUnchecked
    self assert: result equals: "hello"

  testAsStringUncheckedEmpty =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #()
    result := bin asStringUnchecked
    self assert: result equals: ""

  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  // deserializeWithUsed: — ETF decode with byte count
  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  testDeserializeWithUsed =>
    bin := Binary serialize: 42
    result := Binary deserializeWithUsed: bin
    @expect type
    self assert: result size equals: 2
    @expect type
    self assert: (result at: 1) equals: 42
    @expect type
    self assert: (result at: 2) > 0

  testDeserializeWithUsedString =>
    bin := Binary serialize: "hello"
    result := Binary deserializeWithUsed: bin
    @expect type
    self assert: (result at: 1) equals: "hello"
    @expect type
    self assert: (result at: 2) equals: bin byteSize

  testDeserializeWithUsedTypeError =>
    self should: [Binary deserializeWithUsed: 42] raise: #type_error

  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  // printString — display representation
  // At runtime, dispatches to String's printString (quoted string)
  // for valid UTF-8 binaries.
  // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  testPrintStringValidUtf8 =>
    bin := Binary fromBytes: #(104, 105)
    result := bin printString
    self assert: (result includesSubstring: "hi")

  testPrintStringOnString =>
    result := "hello" printString
    self assert: (result includesSubstring: "hello")
entries.explanation: Stdlib tests for Binary class — instance methods, Collection protocol, byte access, slicing, conversion, and serialization (BT-1594, ADR 0069 Phase 3a)  Note: On BEAM, all binaries dispatch as String at runtime. Binary-only methods (byteSize, byteAt:, part:size:, concat:, toBytes, asString, asStringUnchecked) work correctly. String-overridden methods (size, at:, do:, printString) and inherited Collection methods use grapheme-level semantics via String's dispatch.

entries.title: BlockEvaluationTest
entries.explanation: Tests block evaluation — basic value/value:/value:value: dispatch, nested blocks, blocks used as conditionals, and variable persistence across expressions in a test method

entries.title: BlocksTest
entries.explanation: Tests for block (closure) evaluation — argument passing, arity, whileTrue/False, valueWithArguments:, closure variable capture, and early return with ^

entries.title: BT1226ExcDoMutationsTest
entries.explanation: BT-1226: Tests for exception handler followed by state mutation in do: block.  Acceptance criterion 1: [risky] on: Exception do: [handler] followed by self.field := ... in the same do: block iteration must compile and run correctly.

entries.title: BT1226IfNotNilMutationsTest
entries.explanation: BT-1226: Tests for ifNotNil: with state mutation threading in actor methods.  Previously, ifNotNil: blocks that mutated actor state did not thread state correctly: the mutation was applied inside a closure but the updated state was never returned to the handle_call level.

entries.title: BT1226MultiStateMutationsTest
entries.source:
// BT-1226: Regression tests for multi-statement do: block bodies with state mutations.
//
// Previously, do: block bodies with multiple field mutations (where at least one RHS
// involves a nested collection operation like reject:) generated invalid Core Erlang
// with a "syntax error before: in" compile error.

TestCase subclass: BT1226MultiStateMutationsTest

  // Exact issue pattern: removeKey: then reject: with =:= identity check
  testDoBlockWithRemoveKeyAndReject =>
    a := BT1226MultiStateActor spawn
    a addEntry: "x"
    a addEntry: "y"
    a addEntry: "z"
    result := a removeAll: #("x", "z")
    // Both field mutations must be correctly threaded through StateAcc
    self assert: (result at: #claimed) equals: #("y")
    self deny: ((result at: #running) includesKey: "x")
    self deny: ((result at: #running) includesKey: "z")
    self assert: ((result at: #running) includesKey: "y")

  // removeAll: with single-element list (single iteration)
  testDoBlockSingleIterationTwoMutations =>
    b := BT1226MultiStateActor spawn
    b addEntry: "a"
    b addEntry: "b"
    result := b removeAll: #("a")
    // Both mutations applied: running loses "a" key, claimed loses "a" element
    self assert: (result at: #claimed) equals: #("b")
    self deny: ((result at: #running) includesKey: "a")
    self assert: ((result at: #running) includesKey: "b")

  // Two field mutations per iteration with simple integer values
  testDoBlockTwoSimpleMutationsPerIteration =>
    c := BT1226MultiStateActor spawn
    result := c accumulatePairs: #(#p, #q, #r)
    self assert: (result at: #claimed) equals: #(#p, #q, #r)
    self assert: ((result at: #running) at: #p) equals: 1
    self assert: ((result at: #running) at: #q) equals: 2
    self assert: ((result at: #running) at: #r) equals: 3

  // Three field mutations per iteration
  testDoBlockThreeMutationsPerIteration =>
    d := BT1226MultiStateActor spawn
    result := d processAll: #(#a, #b)
    self assert: (result at: #claimed) equals: #(#a, #b)
    // All three mutations must survive: first write (#a=>true) and second write (#b=>true)
    // must not be clobbered by the third mutation (at: #count put: size)
    self assert: ((result at: #running) at: #a) equals: true
    self assert: ((result at: #running) at: #b) equals: true
    self assert: ((result at: #running) at: #count) equals: 2

  // Exact issue pattern: local var before do: with two field mutations
  testLocalVarBeforeDoWithTwoMutations =>
    e := BT1226MultiStateActor spawn
    result := e tickLocalVar
    self assert: (result at: #claimed) equals: #()
    self assert: (result at: #running) equals: #{}
entries.explanation: BT-1226: Regression tests for multi-statement do: block bodies with state mutations.  Previously, do: block bodies with multiple field mutations (where at least one RHS involves a nested collection operation like reject:) generated invalid Core Erlang with a "syntax error before: in" compile error.

entries.title: CastSendTest
entries.explanation: BT-920: Tests fire-and-forget cast sends (! suffix) Cast sends route through beamtalk_actor:cast_send/3 via gen_server:cast, delivering messages asynchronously. A subsequent sync send acts as a barrier ensuring the cast was processed before asserting

entries.title: CharacterTest
entries.source:
// BT-339: Tests for the Character class — integer codes, equality, comparison,
// predicates (isLetter, isDigit, isUppercase, isLowercase, isWhitespace),
// arithmetic on char codes, and conversions (asString, asFloat)

TestCase subclass: CharacterTest

  testCharacterOperations =>
    self assert: $A equals: 65
    self assert: $a equals: 97
    self assert: $0 equals: 48
    self assert: $\n equals: 10
    self assert: $\t equals: 9
    self assert: ($A =:= $A)
    self deny: ($A =:= $B)
    self assert: ($A /= $B)
    self assert: ($A < $B)
    self assert: ($B > $A)
    self assert: ($A <= $A)
    self assert: ($B >= $A)
    self assert: $A isLetter
    self assert: $z isLetter
    self deny: $0 isLetter
    self assert: $5 isDigit
    self deny: $A isDigit
    self assert: $A isUppercase
    self deny: $a isUppercase
    self assert: $a isLowercase
    self deny: $A isLowercase
    self assert: $\n isWhitespace
    self assert: $\t isWhitespace
    self deny: $A isWhitespace
    self assert: ($A + 1) equals: 66
    self assert: ($z - $a) equals: 25
    self assert: $A asString equals: "65"
    self assert: $A asFloat equals: 65 asFloat
entries.explanation: BT-339: Tests for the Character class — integer codes, equality, comparison, predicates (isLetter, isDigit, isUppercase, isLowercase, isWhitespace), arithmetic on char codes, and conversions (asString, asFloat)

entries.title: ClassBuilderTest
entries.explanation: ClassBuilder validation tests (ADR 0038)  Tests ClassBuilder error cases: validation failures caught before class registration. Exercises the validate/2 logic in beamtalk_class_builder.erl

entries.title: ClassHierarchyQueryTest
entries.source:
// Class hierarchy query tests - migrated from bootstrap/class_hierarchy.bt (BT-827)
// Tests subclasses, allSubclasses, and class identity queries

TestCase subclass: ClassHierarchyQueryTest

  testNumberSubclasses =>
    // Number has Integer and Float as direct subclasses
    numSubs := Number subclasses
    self assert: (numSubs includes: Integer)
    self assert: (numSubs includes: Float)

  testBooleanSubclasses =>
    // Boolean has True and False as direct subclasses
    boolSubs := Boolean subclasses
    self assert: (boolSubs includes: True)
    self assert: (boolSubs includes: False)

  testFloatIsSealed =>
    // Float is sealed — always a leaf
    self assert: Float subclasses isEmpty
    self assert: True subclasses isEmpty

  testActorSubclassesNotEmpty =>
    // Actor should have some subclasses
    actorSubs := Actor subclasses
    self deny: actorSubs isEmpty

  testNumberAllSubclasses =>
    // Number allSubclasses includes Integer and Float
    numAllSubs := Number allSubclasses
    self assert: (numAllSubs includes: Integer)
    self assert: (numAllSubs includes: Float)

  testBooleanAllSubclasses =>
    // Boolean allSubclasses includes True and False
    boolAllSubs := Boolean allSubclasses
    self assert: (boolAllSubs includes: True)
    self assert: (boolAllSubs includes: False)

  testCollectionAllSubclasses =>
    // Collection allSubclasses includes List and Dictionary
    collSubs := Collection allSubclasses
    self assert: (collSubs includes: List)
    self assert: (collSubs includes: Dictionary)

  testFloatAllSubclassesEmpty =>
    // Float is sealed — always a leaf, allSubclasses returns empty
    self assert: Float allSubclasses isEmpty

  testClassOnPrimitives =>
    // class returns the class name for primitives
    self assert: 42 class equals: Integer
    self assert: "hello" class equals: String
    self assert: true class equals: True
    self assert: nil class equals: UndefinedObject
    self assert: 3.14 class equals: Float

  testClassOnClassObject =>
    // class on a class object returns something
    classObj := Integer class
    self assert: classObj isNil equals: false
entries.explanation: Class hierarchy query tests - migrated from bootstrap/class_hierarchy.bt (BT-827) Tests subclasses, allSubclasses, and class identity queries

entries.title: ClassMethodBlockTest
entries.source:
// BT-1397: Tests that class methods can contain blocks that call other
// class methods via `self` without producing Core Erlang parse errors.

TestCase subclass: ClassMethodBlockTest

  // sort: block calling class method via self
  testSortWithClassMethodBlock =>
    result := ClassMethodBlock sortItems: #(3, 1, 2)
    self assert: result equals: #(1, 2, 3)

  // collect: block calling class method via self
  testCollectWithClassMethodBlock =>
    result := ClassMethodBlock doubleAll: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: result equals: #(2, 4, 6)

  // do: block calling class method via self (discarded result)
  testDoWithClassMethodBlock =>
    result := ClassMethodBlock processAll: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: result equals: #(1, 2, 3)

  // inject:into: block calling class method via self
  testInjectIntoWithClassMethodBlock =>
    result := ClassMethodBlock sumAll: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: result equals: 6

  // Block with local assignments whose RHS is a class method self-send
  testLocalAssignmentWithClassMethodSelfSend =>
    result := ClassMethodBlock doubleAndSort: #(3, 1, 2)
    self assert: result equals: #(1, 2, 3)

  // Multiple class method self-sends in a block
  testMultipleClassMethodSelfSends =>
    result := ClassMethodBlock processWithMultipleSends: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: result equals: #(4, 8, 12)

  // BT-1414: do: with captured local variable mutation in class method
  testDoWithCapturedMutation =>
    result := ClassMethodBlock countItems: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: result equals: 3

  // BT-1414: do: with two captured mutations simultaneously
  testDoWithTwoCapturedMutations =>
    result := ClassMethodBlock sumAndCount: #(10, 20, 30)
    self assert: result equals: #(60, 3)

  // BT-1414: do: with captured mutation on Actor class method (context=Actor)
  testActorClassMethodDoWithMutation =>
    result := ActorClassMethodBlock countItems: #(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
    self assert: result equals: 5
entries.explanation: BT-1397: Tests that class methods can contain blocks that call other class methods via `self` without producing Core Erlang parse errors.

entries.title: ClassMethodConditionalSelfCallTest

entries.title: ClassMethodEnsureTest

// BT-1346: Tests that ensure: works correctly in class methods.

// referencing a non-existent State variable.

TestCase subclass: ClassMethodEnsureTest

    self assert: result equals: 42

    self assert: result equals: 100

entries.title: ClassObjectDispatchTest
entries.source:
// BT-753: Class objects correctly dispatch Object base class methods
// and pure Beamtalk Behaviour methods

TestCase subclass: ClassObjectDispatchTest

  // --- Object base class methods on class objects (BT-753 core fix) ---

  // Note: respondsTo: on class objects is a known limitation (virtual metaclass
  // tag isn't registered in the class registry). Tracked in BT-776
  testClassObjectYourself => self assert: Counter yourself equals: Counter

  testClassObjectHash =>
    h := Counter hash
    self assert: (h > 0)

  testClassObjectIsNilNotNil =>
    self deny: Counter isNil
    self assert: Counter notNil

  testClassObjectPrintString =>
    ps := Counter printString
    self deny: ps isEmpty

  testClassObjectInspect =>
    i := Counter inspect
    self deny: i isEmpty

  testClassObjectPerform =>
    self assert: (Counter perform: #name) equals: #Counter

  // --- Primitive class objects also work ---
  testPrimitiveClassObjectReflection =>
    self assert: (Integer respondsTo: #isNil)
    self assert: Integer yourself equals: Integer
    self assert: Integer notNil

  // --- Pure Beamtalk Behaviour methods (BT-753 converted from @primitive) ---
  testInheritsFrom =>
    self assert: (Counter inheritsFrom: Actor)
    self assert: (Counter inheritsFrom: Object)
    self deny: (Counter inheritsFrom: Counter)
    self deny: (Counter inheritsFrom: Integer)

  testIncludesBehaviour =>
    self assert: (Counter includesBehaviour: Counter)
    self assert: (Counter includesBehaviour: Actor)
    self assert: (Counter includesBehaviour: Object)
    self deny: (Counter includesBehaviour: Integer)

  testCanUnderstand =>
    self assert: (Counter canUnderstand: #increment)
    self assert: (Counter canUnderstand: #isNil)
    self deny: (Counter canUnderstand: #bogus)

  testWhichClassIncludesSelector =>
    self assert: (Counter whichClassIncludesSelector: #increment) equals: Counter
    self assert: (Counter whichClassIncludesSelector: #bogus) equals: nil
entries.explanation: BT-753: Class objects correctly dispatch Object base class methods and pure Beamtalk Behaviour methods

entries.title: ClosuresAdvancedTest
entries.source:
// E2E tests for closure behavior (blocks capturing outer scope)

TestCase subclass: ClosuresAdvancedTest

  testBasicClosureCapture =>
    outer := 10
    self assert: outer equals: 10
    closure := [:x | x + outer]
    self assert: (closure value: 5) equals: 15
    // Verify outer variable unchanged
    self assert: outer equals: 10

  testMultipleCaptures =>
    // Closure capturing multiple variables
    base := 100
    self assert: base equals: 100
    multiplier := 2
    self assert: multiplier equals: 2
    compute := [:x | (x + base) * multiplier]
    self assert: (compute value: 50) equals: 300

  testNestedClosures =>
    // Closure returning closure (currying pattern)
    makeAdder := [:amount | [:value | value + amount]]
    add10 := makeAdder value: 10
    self assert: (add10 value: 5) equals: 15
    self assert: (add10 value: 20) equals: 30
    // Different closure from same factory
    add100 := makeAdder value: 100
    self assert: (add100 value: 5) equals: 105

  testClosureChains =>
    // Multiple levels of nesting
    makeMultiplier := [:factor |
      [:makeAdder | [:value | (value + makeAdder) * factor]]
    ]
    times2 := makeMultiplier value: 2
    times2Plus10 := times2 value: 10
    self assert: (times2Plus10 value: 5) equals: 30

  testClosureWithOperators =>
    // Closure using binary operators
    threshold := 100
    self assert: threshold equals: 100
    isAboveThreshold := [:n | n > threshold]
    self assert: (isAboveThreshold value: 150)
    self deny: (isAboveThreshold value: 50)

  testSharedCaptures =>
    // Multiple closures sharing same captured variable
    shared := 1000
    self assert: shared equals: 1000
    addShared := [:x | x + shared]
    subtractShared := [:x | x - shared]
    multiplyShared := [:x | x * shared]
    self assert: (addShared value: 500) equals: 1500
    self assert: (subtractShared value: 1200) equals: 200
    self assert: (multiplyShared value: 2) equals: 2000

  testClosureComposition =>
    // Using one closure's result as input to another
    double := [:x | x * 2]
    addOne := [:x | x + 1]
    // Apply double first, then addOne
    doubleResult := double value: 5
    self assert: doubleResult equals: 10
    self assert: (addOne value: doubleResult) equals: 11

  testDeepNesting =>
    // Three levels deep
    level1 := [:a | [:b | [:c | a + b + c]]]
    level2 := level1 value: 1
    level3 := level2 value: 2
    self assert: (level3 value: 3) equals: 6
entries.explanation: E2E tests for closure behavior (blocks capturing outer scope)

entries.title: CodegenDocTest
entries.source:
// BUnit tests for doc comment codegen emission (ADR 0033, BT-771)

TestCase subclass: CodegenDocTest

  // --- Class doc from /// comment ---
  testClassDocFromTripleSlash =>
    self assert: DocCounter doc equals: "A counter that tracks a value."

  // --- Method docs from /// comments ---
  testMethodDocFromTripleSlash =>
    m := DocCounter >> #increment
    self assert: m doc equals: "Increment the counter by one."

  testValueMethodDoc =>
    m := DocCounter >> #value
    self assert: m doc equals: "Return the current count."

  // --- Method without doc comment has nil doc ---
  testMethodWithoutDocHasNilDoc =>
    m := DocCounter >> #reset
    self assert: m doc equals: nil
entries.explanation: BUnit tests for doc comment codegen emission (ADR 0033, BT-771)

entries.title: CollectionMutationsTest
entries.source:
// BT-524: Tests that the is_list guard in mutation-threading codegen works
// correctly when actor methods iterate over List, Set, and Dictionary arguments

TestCase subclass: CollectionMutationsTest

  testActorMutatingCollections =>
    counter := CollectionMutationCounter spawn
    self assert: counter getTotal equals: 0
    self assert: (counter sumSet: #(1, 2, 3)) equals: 6
    self assert: counter getTotal equals: 6
    counter2 := CollectionMutationCounter spawn
    s := (Set new) fromList: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: (counter2 sumSet: s) equals: 6
    self assert: counter2 getTotal equals: 6
    counter3 := CollectionMutationCounter spawn
    self assert: (counter3 sumDictValues: (Dictionary new: #{
      #a => 10,
      #b => 20,
      #c => 30
    })) equals: 60
    self assert: counter3 getTotal equals: 60
entries.explanation: BT-524: Tests that the is_list guard in mutation-threading codegen works correctly when actor methods iterate over List, Set, and Dictionary arguments
