entries.title: HomComposabilityActor
entries.explanation: BT-857: HOM composability fixture (ADR 0041 Phase 5 testing) Verifies that user-defined HOMs calling blocks in loops thread state correctly across multiple invocations, and that nested HOMs propagate mutations back to the caller  Implementation note: The Tier 2 protocol for block calls inside a do: loop body is activated when the loop body has at least one local write (which triggers the stateful do: codegen path). The HOMs below include a `last` variable assignment to activate this path, matching the ADR 0041 design where HOMs naturally have their own local state (e.g. index tracking)

entries.title: MutationMatrixActor
entries.source:
// BT-1480: Comprehensive Actor fixture for the state mutation test matrix.
// Exercises every combination of control flow × nesting × mutation pattern
// to prove (or disprove) the compiler handles all patterns correctly.

Actor subclass: MutationMatrixActor
  state: counter = 0
  state: log = #()
  state: flag = false

  getCounter => self.counter

  getLog => self.log

  getFlag => self.flag

  reset =>
    self.counter := 0
    self.log := #()
    self.flag := false

  // ── P0: Known bug patterns ──────────────────────────────────────────

  // P0-1: collect: + ifTrue: + mutation (BT-1477)
  collectIfTrueMutation: items =>
    result := items
      collect: [:x |
        x > 0 ifTrue: [self.counter := self.counter + 1]
        x * 2
      ]
    self.log := result
    self.counter

  // P0-2: select: + ifTrue: + mutation (BT-1477)
  selectIfTrueMutation: items =>
    result := items
      select: [:x |
        self.counter := self.counter + 1
        x > 0
      ]
    self.log := result
    self.counter

  // P0-3: Sequential mutations across multiple statements
  sequentialMutations =>
    self.counter := 10
    self.counter := self.counter + 1
    self.log := #(self.counter)
    self.counter := self.counter * 2
    self.counter

  // P0-4: Mutation inside nested conditional blocks
  nestedConditionalMutation: outer and: inner =>
    outer
      ifTrue: [
        inner ifTrue: [self.counter := self.counter + 100]
        self.counter := self.counter + 10
      ]
    self.counter

  // ── P1: High risk unexplored patterns ───────────────────────────────

  // P1-5: collect: inside collect: + mutation
  nestedCollectMutation: matrix =>
    result := matrix
      collect: [:row |
        row
          collect: [:x |
            self.counter := self.counter + 1
            x * 2
          ]
      ]
    self.log := result
    self.counter

  // P1-6: do: inside collect: + mutation
  doInsideCollectMutation: matrix =>
    result := matrix
      collect: [:row |
        row do: [:x | self.counter := self.counter + x]
        row size
      ]
    self.log := result
    self.counter

  // P1-7: inject:into: + ifTrue: + mutation
  injectIfTrueMutation: items =>
    result := items
      inject: 0
      into: [:acc :x |
        x > 0 ifTrue: [self.counter := self.counter + 1]
        x > 0 ifTrue: [acc + x] ifFalse: [acc]
      ]
    self.log := #(result)
    self.counter

  // P1-8: detect:ifNone: + mutation before match
  detectIfNoneMutation: items =>
    self.counter := 99
    result := items detect: [:x | x > 10] ifNone: [0]
    self.log := #(result)
    self.counter

  // P1-9: on:do: exception handler + mutation
  exceptionMutation =>
    [
      self.counter := 5
      1 / 0
    ] on: Exception do: [:e | self.counter := 0]
    self.counter

  // P1-10: Non-local return from inside collect: after mutation
  nonLocalReturnFromCollect: items =>
    items
      collect: [:x |
        self.counter := self.counter + 1
        self.counter > 2 ifTrue: [^self.counter]
        x * 2
      ]

  // P1-11: Multiple field mutations in single collect: block
  multiFieldMutation: items =>
    items
      collect: [:x |
        self.counter := self.counter + 1
        self.log := self.log ++ #(x)
        self.flag := x > 0
        x * 2
      ]

  // P1-12: ifTrue:ifFalse: inside do: with mutation
  ifTrueIfFalseInsideDo: items =>
    items
      do: [:x |
        x > 0 ifTrue: [
          self.counter := self.counter + x
        ] ifFalse: [self.counter := self.counter - x]
      ]
    self.counter

  // ── P2: Edge cases ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

  // P2-13: collect: + ifTrue: + mutation + non-local return
  collectIfTrueNlr: items =>
    items
      collect: [:x |
        x > 0 ifTrue: [self.counter := self.counter + 1]
        self.counter > 3 ifTrue: [^self.counter]
        x * 2
      ]

  // P2-14: 3-deep nesting: do: > collect: > ifTrue: + mutation
  threeDeepNesting: matrix =>
    matrix
      do: [:row |
        row
          collect: [:x |
            x > 0 ifTrue: [self.counter := self.counter + 1]
            x * 2
          ]
      ]
    self.counter

  // P2-17: reject: + mutation in filter block
  rejectMutation: items =>
    result := items
      reject: [:x |
        self.counter := self.counter + 1
        x < 0
      ]
    self.log := result
    self.counter

  // P2-18: Empty collection + mutation in iteration block
  emptyCollectionMutation =>
    self.counter := 42
    #() do: [:x | self.counter := self.counter + x]
    self.counter

  // ── BT-1479: Control flow RHS patterns ─────────────────────────────

  // P2-19: Field assign from conditional with mutation in RHS (BT-1479)
  fieldAssignFromConditionalRhs: flag =>
    self.counter := flag
      ifTrue: [
        self.flag := true
        42
      ]
      ifFalse: [0]
    self.counter

  // P2-20: Field assign from collect: with mutation in RHS (BT-1479)
  fieldAssignFromCollectRhs: items =>
    self.log := items
      collect: [:x |
        self.counter := self.counter + 1
        x * 2
      ]
    self.counter

  // P2-21: fieldAt:put: inside conditional branch (BT-1479)
  fieldAtPutInConditional: flag =>
    flag ifTrue: [self fieldAt: #counter put: 99]
    self.counter

  // ── BT-1481: anySatisfy:/allSatisfy: state threading ──────────────

  // P2-16: anySatisfy: block with mutation
  anySatisfyMutation: items =>
    result := items
      anySatisfy: [:x |
        self.counter := self.counter + 1
        x > 10
      ]
    self.flag := result
    self.counter

  // P2-16b: allSatisfy: block with mutation
  allSatisfyMutation: items =>
    result := items
      allSatisfy: [:x |
        self.counter := self.counter + 1
        x > 0
      ]
    self.flag := result
    self.counter

  // P2-22: Block value with mutation (BT-1481)
  blockValueMutation =>
    _result := [self.counter := self.counter + 42] value
    self.counter

  // ── BT-1485: Remaining block-accepting selectors ─────────────────────
  // Most methods below remain commented out — they produce unbound State
  // variables in codegen due to missing state threading for these selectors.
  // List high-risk cases P3-1..P3-4 are enabled (BT-1486).
  // Dictionary cases P3-10..P3-11 are enabled (BT-1488).
  // String cases P3-12..P3-15 are enabled (BT-1489).
  // Uncomment additional cases when their corresponding codegen fixes land.

  // ── List (high risk) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

  // P3-1: detect: with field mutation in predicate block (BT-1486)
  detectMutation: items =>
    result := items
      detect: [:x |
        self.counter := self.counter + 1
        x > 10
      ]
    self.log := #(result)
    self.counter

  // P3-2: detect:ifNone: with field mutation in predicate block (BT-1486)
  detectIfNonePredicateMutation: items =>
    result := items
      detect: [:x |
        self.counter := self.counter + 1
        x > 10
      ]
      ifNone: [-1]
    self.log := #(result)
    self.counter

  // P3-3: count: with field mutation in predicate block (BT-1486)
  countMutation: items =>
    result := items
      count: [:x |
        self.counter := self.counter + 1
        x > 0
      ]
    self.log := #(result)
    self.counter

  // P3-4: flatMap: with field mutation in mapping block (BT-1486)
  flatMapMutation: items =>
    result := items
      flatMap: [:x |
        self.counter := self.counter + 1
        #(x, x * 10)
      ]
    self.log := result
    self.counter

  // ── List (medium risk) ────────────────────────────────────────────────

  // P3-5: takeWhile: with field mutation in predicate block (BT-1487)
  takeWhileMutation: items =>
    result := items
      takeWhile: [:x |
        self.counter := self.counter + 1
        x < 10
      ]
    self.log := result
    self.counter

  // P3-6: dropWhile: with field mutation in predicate block (BT-1487)
  dropWhileMutation: items =>
    result := items
      dropWhile: [:x |
        self.counter := self.counter + 1
        x < 10
      ]
    self.log := result
    self.counter

  // P3-7: groupBy: with field mutation in key block (BT-1487)
  groupByMutation: items =>
    _result := items
      groupBy: [:x |
        self.counter := self.counter + 1
        x > 0
      ]
    self.counter

  // P3-8: partition: with field mutation in predicate block (BT-1487)
  partitionMutation: items =>
    result := items
      partition: [:x |
        self.counter := self.counter + 1
        x > 0
      ]
    self.log := result
    self.counter

  // P3-9: sort: with field mutation in comparator block (BT-1487)
  sortMutation: items =>
    result := items
      sort: [:a :b |
        self.counter := self.counter + 1
        a < b
      ]
    self.log := result
    self.counter

  // ── Dictionary ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  // P3-10: Dictionary do: with field mutation in iteration block (BT-1488)
  dictDoMutation: dict =>
    dict do: [:v | self.counter := self.counter + v]
    self.counter

  // P3-11: Dictionary doWithKey: with field mutation in iteration block (BT-1488)
  dictDoWithKeyMutation: dict =>
    dict
      doWithKey: [:k :v |
        self.counter := self.counter + v
        self.log := self.log ++ #(k)
      ]
    self.counter

  // ── String ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  // P3-12: String do: with field mutation in iteration block
  stringDoMutation: str =>
    str do: [:ch | self.counter := self.counter + 1]
    self.counter

  // P3-13: String collect: with field mutation in mapping block
  stringCollectMutation: str =>
    result := str
      collect: [:ch |
        self.counter := self.counter + 1
        ch
      ]
    self.log := #(result)
    self.counter

  // P3-14: String select: with field mutation in predicate block
  stringSelectMutation: str =>
    result := str
      select: [:ch |
        self.counter := self.counter + 1
        ch /= "b"
      ]
    self.log := #(result)
    self.counter

  // P3-15: String reject: with field mutation in predicate block
  stringRejectMutation: str =>
    result := str
      reject: [:ch |
        self.counter := self.counter + 1
        ch == "b"
      ]
    self.log := #(result)
    self.counter

entries.title: ReflectionSelfMutateActor
entries.source:
// BT-1324: Fixture actor for testing self fieldAt:put: state threading.
// Uses reflective mutation on self and reads back via fieldAt: and direct access.

Actor subclass: ReflectionSelfMutateActor
  state: x = 0
  state: y = 0

  // Mutate via fieldAt:put: on self, then read back via fieldAt:
  mutateAndReadReflective =>
    self fieldAt: #x put: 42
    self fieldAt: #x

  // Mutate via fieldAt:put: on self, then read back via direct field access
  mutateAndReadDirect =>
    self fieldAt: #x put: 99
    self.x

  // Mutate two fields via fieldAt:put: on self
  mutateTwoFields =>
    self fieldAt: #x put: 10
    self fieldAt: #y put: 20
    (self fieldAt: #x) + (self fieldAt: #y)

  // Compare: normal assignment then reflective read
  assignAndReadReflective =>
    self.x := 77
    self fieldAt: #x
entries.explanation: BT-1324: Fixture actor for testing self fieldAt:put: state threading. Uses reflective mutation on self and reads back via fieldAt: and direct access.

entries.title: SelfCallStateActor
entries.source:
// BT-1420: Fixture for testing that self-call state mutations are visible
// to the caller after the self-call returns.

Actor subclass: SelfCallStateActor
  state: value = 0
  state: count = 0

  // Helper that mutates state — called via self-send
  setupValue => self.value := 42

  // Calls self helper, then reads the mutated state
  callSetupThenRead =>
    self setupValue
    self.value

  // Helper that increments count
  incrementCount => self.count := self.count + 1

  // Multiple self-calls that each mutate state
  callThreeIncrements =>
    self incrementCount
    self incrementCount
    self incrementCount
    self.count

  // Self-call as the last expression (result should be returned)
  callSetupAsLast => self setupValue

  // Read value to verify state after callSetupAsLast
  getValue => self.value

  // Self-call that mutates state, then a field assignment after
  callSetupThenAssignField =>
    self setupValue
    self.count := 10
    self.value + self.count

  // BT-1421: Helper returning a value while mutating state
  bump =>
    self.count := self.count + 1
    self.count

  // BT-1421: Assignment RHS is a self-send — result gets return value, state threads
  callBumpAndCapture =>
    result := self bump
    result

  // BT-1421: Assignment RHS self-send, then read mutated state
  callBumpAndReadState =>
    _result := self bump
    self.count

  // BT-1421: Multiple assignment RHS self-sends
  callDoubleBumpAndCapture =>
    first := self bump
    second := self bump
    first + second
entries.explanation: BT-1420: Fixture for testing that self-call state mutations are visible to the caller after the self-call returns.

entries.title: StubDelegate
entries.source:
// StubDelegate — collects subprocessLine:from:, subprocessStderrLine:from:,
// and subprocessExit:from: casts from a ReactiveSubprocess for BUnit testing.

Actor subclass: StubDelegate
  state: stdoutLines = #()
  state: stderrLines = #()
  state: exitCode = nil
  state: exitReceived = false

  subprocessLine: line from: _proc =>
    self.stdoutLines := self.stdoutLines add: line

  subprocessStderrLine: line from: _proc =>
    self.stderrLines := self.stderrLines add: line

  subprocessExit: code from: _proc =>
    self.exitCode := code
    self.exitReceived := true

  stdoutLines => self.stdoutLines

  stderrLines => self.stderrLines

  exitCode => self.exitCode

  exitReceived => self.exitReceived
entries.explanation: StubDelegate — collects subprocessLine:from:, subprocessStderrLine:from:, and subprocessExit:from: casts from a ReactiveSubprocess for BUnit testing.

entries.title: TypedAccount
entries.source:
// TypedAccount — test fixture for typed actor features
//
// A typed actor with multiple typed state fields (Integer, String),
// typed parameters, and return annotations

typed Actor subclass: TypedAccount
  state: balance :: Integer = 0
  state: owner :: String = ""

  deposit: amount :: Integer -> Integer =>
    amount < 0 ifTrue: [self error: "Amount must be non-negative"]
    self.balance := self.balance + amount
    self.balance

  withdraw: amount :: Integer -> Integer =>
    amount < 0 ifTrue: [self error: "Amount must be non-negative"]
    self.balance < amount
      ifTrue: [
        self
          error: "Insufficient funds (balance: "
            ++ self.balance printString
            ++ ", requested: "
            ++ amount printString
            ++ ")"
      ]
    self.balance := self.balance - amount
    self.balance

  balance -> Integer => self.balance

  setOwner: name :: String -> String =>
    self.owner := name
    self.owner

  getOwner -> String => self.owner
entries.explanation: TypedAccount — test fixture for typed actor features  A typed actor with multiple typed state fields (Integer, String), typed parameters, and return annotations

entries.title: FunctionalSetupTest
entries.explanation: BT-1533: Tests that the functional setUp pattern works end-to-end with TestCase as a Value subclass.  setUp returns a new self with fields set. The test runner threads this return value to each test method. Each test gets a fresh setUp'd instance — tests are isolated.  Note: uses `self withSlot:` functional update syntax (value semantics).

entries.title: FutureAutoAwaitTest
entries.explanation: Tests that actor method results work correctly in binary operations with sync-by-default dispatch. Actor method calls return values directly (via gen_server:call), so binary ops (+, ==, <, >, etc.) receive the resolved value, not a Future. The codegen wraps operands with beamtalk_future:maybe_await/1 for backward compatibility during migration

entries.title: GenericStdlibTest
entries.explanation: BUnit tests verifying generic type annotations on stdlib collection classes (BT-1576, ADR 0068 Phase 1e).  These tests exercise the generic type parameters declared on Result(T, E), Array(E), Dictionary(K, V), and Set(E). The tests verify that: 1. Constructor inference propagates type params through unwrap/value/error 2. Collection methods preserve type params through transformations 3. @primitive method signatures carry correct generic annotations 4. Existing runtime behavior is unchanged (generics are compile-time only)

entries.title: HomComposabilityTest
entries.explanation: BT-857: Tests for HOM composability (ADR 0041 Phase 5)  BT-912: Tier 2 block StateAcc threading through stateful do: loop bodies  The BT-912 bug: when `last := aBlock value: each` is in a stateful do: loop body, the Tier 2 block's NewStateAcc was discarded by codegen, so mutations to captured variables (e.g., `count`) were not propagated back  Fixed by: 1. generate_local_var_assignment_in_loop: unpack {Result, NewStateAcc} from    Tier 2 block calls and thread NewStateAcc into the maps:put 2. detect_tier2_self_send: treat identifier Tier 2 block parameters as Tier 2    HOM arguments, enabling state threading through nested HOM delegation chains  Stored blocks passed to HOMs (testStoredBlockPassedToCustomHOM) require type-level tracking of block variables (BT-909) and are not yet tested

entries.title: HomFieldWriteTest
entries.explanation: BT-1140: Tests that self.field := ... blocks promoted to Tier 2 correctly propagate field mutations when the block is passed through a user-defined HOM.  Before this fix, field-write blocks were compiled as plain Tier 1 funs, so field mutations inside the block were silently discarded. The HOM received a fun(X) -> ... that never threaded state back to the caller.

entries.title: HTTPClientTest

Tests cover:   - Builder API (build:)   - Route array API (routes:)   - Exact match routing   - Parameterized routes and param extraction   - Wildcard routes   - Method-based dispatch   - 404 Not Found (default and custom)   - 405 Method Not Allowed   - Controller-object dispatch via block closures   - printString

entries.title: HttpTest
entries.source:
// BT-1117: BUnit integration tests for HTTPResponse and HTTPClient.
//
// Starts a local cowboy test server (beamtalk_http_test_server) and runs
// integration tests against it.  Tests cover:
//   - HTTPResponse value methods (status, ok, body, headers, bodyAsJson)
//   - HTTPClient class-side one-shot methods (get:, post:body:, put:body:,
//     delete:, request:url:options:)
//   - HTTPClient instance methods (get:, post:body:, put:body:, delete:)
//   - Custom request headers
//   - Json body round-trips
//   - Non-2xx status codes
//   - Error cases (connection refused, invalid URL, type errors)
//   - HTTPS connections (requires network access)
//
// Note: HTTPClient methods return Result (ADR 0060). Successful responses are
// unwrapped with `unwrap`. With generic Result(T, E) (BT-1576), unwrap returns
// Dynamic for unparameterized Results, removing most DNU hints.

TestCase subclass: HttpTest
  field: baseUrl = ""

  // Start the local test server and record the base URL.
  setUp =>
    port := (Erlang beamtalk_http_test_server) start
    self withBaseUrl: ("http://localhost:" ++ port asString)

  // Stop the local test server after each test.
  tearDown => (Erlang beamtalk_http_test_server) stop

  // ===== HTTPResponse — status =====
  testResponseStatusEquals200 =>
    resp := (HTTPClient get: self.baseUrl ++ "/get") unwrap
    self assert: resp status equals: 200

  testResponseStatusEquals404 =>
    resp := (HTTPClient get: self.baseUrl ++ "/status/404") unwrap
    self assert: resp status equals: 404

  testResponseStatusEquals500 =>
    resp := (HTTPClient get: self.baseUrl ++ "/status/500") unwrap
    self assert: resp status equals: 500

  // ===== HTTPResponse — ok =====
  testResponseOkTrueForSuccessStatus =>
    resp := (HTTPClient get: self.baseUrl ++ "/get") unwrap
    self assert: resp ok

  testResponseOkFalseForClientError =>
    resp := (HTTPClient get: self.baseUrl ++ "/status/404") unwrap
    self deny: resp ok

  testResponseOkFalseForServerError =>
    resp := (HTTPClient get: self.baseUrl ++ "/status/500") unwrap
    self deny: resp ok

  testResponseOkTrueFor201 =>
    // 201 Created is in the 2xx range
    resp := (HTTPClient get: self.baseUrl ++ "/status/201") unwrap
    self assert: resp ok

  // ===== HTTPResponse — body =====
  testResponseBodyIsNonEmpty =>
    resp := (HTTPClient get: self.baseUrl ++ "/get") unwrap
    self assert: (resp body size > 0)

  testResponseBodyIsString =>
    resp := (HTTPClient get: self.baseUrl ++ "/get") unwrap
    self assert: (resp body class name) equals: #String

  // ===== HTTPResponse — headers =====
  testResponseHeadersIsList =>
    resp := (HTTPClient get: self.baseUrl ++ "/get") unwrap
    self assert: (resp headers class name) equals: #List

  // ===== HTTPResponse — bodyAsJson =====
  testResponseBodyAsJsonReturnsDictionary =>
    resp := (HTTPClient get: self.baseUrl ++ "/json") unwrap
    json := resp bodyAsJson
    // bodyAsJson -> Object; at: is valid on Dictionary at runtime
    self assert: (json at: "type") equals: "json"

  testResponseBodyAsJsonReturnsInteger =>
    resp := (HTTPClient get: self.baseUrl ++ "/json") unwrap
    json := resp bodyAsJson
    // bodyAsJson -> Object; at: is valid on Dictionary at runtime
    self assert: (json at: "value") equals: 42

  testResponseBodyAsJsonRaisesParseErrorForEmptyBody =>
    // /status/204 returns an empty body; bodyAsJson must raise parse_error
    resp := (HTTPClient get: self.baseUrl ++ "/status/204") unwrap
    self should: [resp bodyAsJson] raise: #parse_error

  // ===== HTTPClient class-side — get: =====
  testClassGetReturns200 =>
    resp := (HTTPClient get: self.baseUrl ++ "/get") unwrap
    self assert: resp status equals: 200

  testClassGetBodyContainsMethod =>
    resp := (HTTPClient get: self.baseUrl ++ "/get") unwrap
    json := resp bodyAsJson
    // json: Unknown at type level, Dictionary at runtime
    self assert: (json at: "method") equals: "GET"

  // ===== HTTPClient class-side — post:body: =====
  testClassPostBodyReturns200 =>
    resp := (HTTPClient post: self.baseUrl ++ "/post" body: "hello") unwrap
    self assert: resp status equals: 200

  testClassPostBodyEchosRequestBody =>
    resp := (HTTPClient post: self.baseUrl ++ "/post" body: "beamtalk") unwrap
    self assert: ((resp bodyAsJson) at: "body") equals: "beamtalk"

  // ===== HTTPClient class-side — put:body: =====
  testClassPutBodyReturns200 =>
    resp := (HTTPClient put: self.baseUrl ++ "/put" body: "update") unwrap
    self assert: resp status equals: 200

  testClassPutBodyEchosRequestBody =>
    resp := (HTTPClient put: self.baseUrl ++ "/put" body: "updated-value") unwrap
    self assert: ((resp bodyAsJson) at: "body") equals: "updated-value"

  // ===== HTTPClient class-side — delete: =====
  testClassDeleteReturns200 =>
    resp := (HTTPClient delete: self.baseUrl ++ "/delete") unwrap
    self assert: resp status equals: 200

  testClassDeleteBodyContainsMethod =>
    resp := (HTTPClient delete: self.baseUrl ++ "/delete") unwrap
    json := resp bodyAsJson
    // json: Unknown at type level, Dictionary at runtime
    self assert: (json at: "method") equals: "DELETE"

  // ===== HTTPClient class-side — request:url:options: =====
  testClassRequestGetReturns200 =>
    resp := (HTTPClient
      request: #get
      url: self.baseUrl ++ "/get"
      options: #{}) unwrap
    self assert: resp status equals: 200

  testClassRequestPostWithBodyOption =>
    resp := (HTTPClient
      request: #post
      url: self.baseUrl ++ "/post"
      options: #{#body => "payload"}) unwrap
    self assert: resp status equals: 200
    self assert: ((resp bodyAsJson) at: "body") equals: "payload"

  // ===== HTTPClient instance — get: =====
  testInstanceGetReturns200 =>
    client := HTTPClient spawnWith: #{#baseUrl => self.baseUrl}
    resp := (client get: "/get") unwrap
    self assert: resp status equals: 200
    self assert: client stop equals: #ok

  testInstanceGetBodyContainsMethod =>
    client := HTTPClient spawnWith: #{#baseUrl => self.baseUrl}
    resp := (client get: "/get") unwrap
    json := resp bodyAsJson
    // json: Unknown at type level, Dictionary at runtime
    self assert: (json at: "method") equals: "GET"
    self assert: client stop equals: #ok

  // ===== HTTPClient instance — post:body: =====
  testInstancePostBodyReturns200 =>
    client := HTTPClient spawnWith: #{#baseUrl => self.baseUrl}
    resp := (client post: "/post" body: "instance-post") unwrap
    self assert: resp status equals: 200
    self assert: client stop equals: #ok

  testInstancePostBodyEchosRequestBody =>
    client := HTTPClient spawnWith: #{#baseUrl => self.baseUrl}
    resp := (client post: "/post" body: "data") unwrap
    self assert: ((resp bodyAsJson) at: "body") equals: "data"
    self assert: client stop equals: #ok

  // ===== HTTPClient instance — put:body: =====
  testInstancePutBodyReturns200 =>
    client := HTTPClient spawnWith: #{#baseUrl => self.baseUrl}
    resp := (client put: "/put" body: "put-data") unwrap
    self assert: resp status equals: 200
    self assert: client stop equals: #ok

  // ===== HTTPClient instance — delete: =====
  testInstanceDeleteReturns200 =>
    client := HTTPClient spawnWith: #{#baseUrl => self.baseUrl}
    resp := (client delete: "/delete") unwrap
    self assert: resp status equals: 200
    self assert: client stop equals: #ok

  // ===== Custom headers =====
  testCustomHeaderSentInRequest =>
    resp := (HTTPClient
      request: #get
      url: self.baseUrl ++ "/echo-headers"
      options: #{#headers => #(#("x-test-key", "test-value"))}) unwrap
    self assert: resp status equals: 200
    headers := resp bodyAsJson
    // headers: Unknown at type level, Dictionary at runtime
    self assert: (headers at: "x-test-key") equals: "test-value"

  testInstanceCustomHeaderSentInRequest =>
    client := HTTPClient spawnWith: #{
      #baseUrl => self.baseUrl,
      #headers => #(#("x-client-header", "persistent"))
    }
    resp := (client get: "/echo-headers") unwrap
    self assert: resp status equals: 200
    headers := resp bodyAsJson
    // headers: Unknown at type level, Dictionary at runtime
    self assert: (headers at: "x-client-header") equals: "persistent"
    self assert: client stop equals: #ok

  // ===== Json body round-trip =====
  testJsonBodyRoundTrip =>
    // Generate a Json body, POST it, verify the server echoed it correctly
    jsonBody := Json generate: #{"message" => "hello"}
    resp := (HTTPClient post: self.baseUrl ++ "/post" body: jsonBody) unwrap
    echoed := (resp bodyAsJson) at: "body"
    self assert: ((Json parse: echoed) unwrap at: "message") equals: "hello"

  // ===== Non-2xx status codes =====
  testGet301StatusCode =>
    resp := (HTTPClient get: self.baseUrl ++ "/status/301") unwrap
    self assert: resp status equals: 301
    self deny: resp ok

  testGet400StatusCode =>
    resp := (HTTPClient get: self.baseUrl ++ "/status/400") unwrap
    self assert: resp status equals: 400
    self deny: resp ok

  // ===== Error cases =====
  testConnectionRefusedReturnsHttpError =>
    // Use a short timeout so gun fails fast
    result := HTTPClient
      request: #get
      url: "http://localhost:1/get"
      options: #{#timeout => 2000}
    self deny: result isOk

  testInvalidUrlSchemeReturnsHttpError =>
    result := HTTPClient get: "not-a-url"
    self deny: result isOk

  testTypeErrorNonStringUrl =>
    @expect type
    self should: [HTTPClient get: 42] raise: #type_error

  testTypeErrorNonStringPostBody =>
    @expect type
    self should: [
      HTTPClient post: self.baseUrl ++ "/post" body: 42
    ] raise: #type_error

  // ===== HTTPS connections (requires network access) =====
  //
  // These tests connect to a public HTTPS endpoint.  They pass in
  // environments with internet access; they will fail if the host is
  // unreachable (e.g. offline CI).
  testHttpsGetReturnsOkStatus =>
    resp := (HTTPClient get: "https://httpbin.org/get") unwrap
    self assert: resp ok
entries.explanation: BT-1117: BUnit integration tests for HTTPResponse and HTTPClient.  Starts a local cowboy test server (beamtalk_http_test_server) and runs integration tests against it.  Tests cover:   - HTTPResponse value methods (status, ok, body, headers, bodyAsJson)   - HTTPClient class-side one-shot methods (get:, post:body:, put:body:,     delete:, request:url:options:)   - HTTPClient instance methods (get:, post:body:, put:body:, delete:)   - Custom request headers   - Json body round-trips   - Non-2xx status codes   - Error cases (connection refused, invalid URL, type errors)   - HTTPS connections (requires network access)  Note: HTTPClient methods return Result (ADR 0060). Successful responses are unwrapped with `unwrap`. With generic Result(T, E) (BT-1576), unwrap returns Dynamic for unparameterized Results, removing most DNU hints.

entries.title: InjectIntoPureBtTest
entries.explanation: BT-1053: Tests for inject:into: as pure Beamtalk on abstract Collection (implemented using `self do:` with local variable accumulator mutation).  These tests cover:  1. inject:into: on List (the concrete subclass)  2. inject:into: on NumberList (user-defined Collection subclass — exercises     the abstract Collection.inject:into: via `self do:` mutation threading)  3. inject:into: on built-in abstract collections: Set, Dictionary  4. collect:/select:/reject: on NumberList (they call inject:into: internally)  5. Edge cases: empty, single-element, non-commutative argument order

entries.title: InspectTest
entries.explanation: BT-1167: Tests for inspect output format on user-defined classes. Target format: ClassName(field: inspectValue, ...) Value subclasses use the Beamtalk inspect method in Value.bt. Actor subclasses use the Erlang inspect dispatch in beamtalk_object_ops.  Fixtures:   fixtures/empty_value.bt  — Value subclass with no state   fixtures/named_item.bt   — Value subclass with a String state field   fixtures/inspect_pair.bt — Value subclass with left/right fields   fixtures/value_point.bt  — Value subclass with x, y integer fields   fixtures/counter.bt      — Actor subclass with value integer field

entries.title: IntervalTest
entries.source:
// BUnit tests for Interval — arithmetic sequence of integers (BT-1249)

TestCase subclass: IntervalTest

  testCreationClass =>
    iv := 1 to: 10
    self assert: iv class equals: Interval

  testCreationByClass =>
    iv := 1 to: 10 by: 2
    self assert: iv class equals: Interval

  testSizeSimple => self assert: (1 to: 10) size equals: 10

  testSizeByStep => self assert: (1 to: 10 by: 2) size equals: 5

  testSizeByStepThree => self assert: (1 to: 10 by: 3) size equals: 4

  testSizeSingleElement => self assert: (5 to: 5) size equals: 1

  testSizeEmpty => self assert: (1 to: 0) size equals: 0

  testSizeNegativeStep => self assert: (10 to: 1 by: -1) size equals: 10

  testSizeNegativeStepByThree => self assert: (10 to: 1 by: -3) size equals: 4

  testIsEmpty =>
    self assert: (1 to: 0) isEmpty
    self deny: (1 to: 10) isEmpty

  testFirst => self assert: (1 to: 10) first equals: 1

  testFirstByStep => self assert: (5 to: 20 by: 3) first equals: 5

  testLast => self assert: (1 to: 10) last equals: 10

  testLastByStep => self assert: (1 to: 10 by: 3) last equals: 10

  testLastByStepNotExact =>
    // 1, 4, 7, 10 — last is 10
    self assert: (1 to: 11 by: 3) last equals: 10

  testDoCollectsElements =>
    result := #()
    (1 to: 3) do: [:i | result := result addFirst: i]
    self assert: result equals: #(3, 2, 1)

  testDoNegativeStep =>
    result := #()
    (3 to: 1 by: -1) do: [:i | result := result addFirst: i]
    self assert: result equals: #(1, 2, 3)

  testInjectInto =>
    sum := (1 to: 5) inject: 0 into: [:acc :x | acc + x]
    self assert: sum equals: 15

  testInjectIntoByStep =>
    sum := (1 to: 10 by: 2) inject: 0 into: [:acc :x | acc + x]
    self assert: sum equals: 25

  testCollect =>
    result := (1 to: 3) collect: [:x | x * 2]
    self assert: result equals: #(2, 4, 6)

  testSelect =>
    result := (1 to: 6) select: [:x | x isEven]
    self assert: result equals: #(2, 4, 6)

  testReject =>
    result := (1 to: 6) reject: [:x | x isEven]
    self assert: result equals: #(1, 3, 5)

  testIncludes =>
    self assert: ((1 to: 10) includes: 5)
    self deny: ((1 to: 10) includes: 11)
    self deny: ((1 to: 10) includes: 0)

  testIncludesByStep =>
    self assert: ((1 to: 10 by: 2) includes: 3)
    self deny: ((1 to: 10 by: 2) includes: 2)

  testPrintString => self assert: (1 to: 10) printString equals: "(1 to: 10)"

  testPrintStringByStep =>
    self assert: (1 to: 10 by: 2) printString equals: "(1 to: 10 by: 2)"

  testEmptyDoIsNoop =>
    count := 0
    (1 to: 0) do: [:_x | count := count + 1]
    self assert: count equals: 0

  testSizeWrongDirectionPositiveStep =>
    // step > 0 but from > to — must be empty (do: iterates nothing)
    self assert: (1 to: 0 by: 2) size equals: 0

  testSizeWrongDirectionNegativeStep =>
    // step < 0 but from < to — must be empty (do: iterates nothing)
    self assert: (1 to: 10 by: -1) size equals: 0

  testZeroStepRaisesError => self should: [1 to: 10 by: 0] raise: #user_error
entries.explanation: BUnit tests for Interval — arithmetic sequence of integers (BT-1249)

  testIntegerIsKindOfInteger => self assert: (42 isKindOf: Integer)

  testSymbolIsNotKindOfString => self deny: (#foo isKindOf: String)

  testListIsKindOfList => self assert: (#() isKindOf: List)

  testListIsKindOfObject => self assert: (#() isKindOf: Object)

  testStringIsKindOfString => self assert: ("hello" isKindOf: String)

  testStringIsKindOfObject => self assert: ("hello" isKindOf: Object)

  testIntegerIsNotKindOfString => self deny: (42 isKindOf: String)

  testFloatIsKindOfFloat => self assert: (3.14 isKindOf: Float)

  testFloatIsKindOfObject => self assert: (3.14 isKindOf: Object)

  testTrueIsKindOfBoolean => self assert: (true isKindOf: Boolean)

  testFalseIsKindOfBoolean => self assert: (false isKindOf: Boolean)

entries.title: KeywordMessagesTest
entries.source:
// Keyword message tests (block value: dispatch)

TestCase subclass: KeywordMessagesTest

  testSingleKeywordMessages =>
    self assert: ([:x | x] value: 42) equals: 42
    self assert: ([:x | x + 10] value: 5) equals: 15
    self assert: ([:n | n * n] value: 4) equals: 16

  testMultiKeywordMessages =>
    self assert: ([:x :y | x + y] value: 3 value: 4) equals: 7
    self assert: ([:a :b | a - b] value: 10 value: 3) equals: 7
    self assert: ([:x :y | x * y] value: 6 value: 7) equals: 42

  testThreeKeywordMessages =>
    self
      assert: ([:a :b :c | a + b + c]
        value: 1
        value: 2
        value: 3)
      equals: 6
    self
      assert: ([:x :y :z | x * y + z]
        value: 2
        value: 3
        value: 4)
      equals: 10

  testNestedKeywordMessages =>
    self assert: ([:x | [:y | x + y] value: 10] value: 5) equals: 15
    self assert: ([:a |
      [:b | [:c | a + b + c] value: 3] value: 2
    ] value: 1) equals: 6

  testKeywordMessagesWithLiterals =>
    self assert: ([:s | s] value: "test") equals: "test"
    self assert: ([:n | n * 10] value: 0) equals: 0
    self assert: ([:x | 0 - x] value: 5) equals: -5

  testComplexExpressionsAsArguments =>
    self assert: ([:x | x * 2] value: 3 + 2) equals: 10
    self assert: ([:a :b | a + b] value: 2 * 3 value: 4 + 1) equals: 11

  testHigherOrderBlocksVariablePersistenceAcrossExpressions =>
    // Block that returns a block
    makeMultiplier := [:factor | [:n | n * factor]]
    times3 := makeMultiplier value: 3
    self assert: (times3 value: 7) equals: 21
    // Passing block as argument
    applyBlock := [:block :arg | block value: arg]
    self assert: (applyBlock value: [:x | x + 100] value: 42) equals: 142

  testMultiLineKeywordMessage =>
    // BT-890: keyword messages should continue across newlines (single-line until BT-890 lands)
    flag := 3 > 1
    result := flag ifTrue: [42] ifFalse: [0]
    self assert: result equals: 42

  testMultiLineInjectInto =>
    // BT-890: multi-line inject:into: keyword message (single-line until BT-890 lands)
    sum := #(1, 2, 3) inject: 0 into: [:acc :each | acc + each]
    self assert: sum equals: 6

  test4KeywordMessagesOnObjects =>
    mk := MultiKeyword spawn
    // 4-keyword method: sum four arguments
    self
      assert: (mk
        add: 1
        and: 2
        and: 3
        and: 4)
      equals: 10
    // 4-keyword method: verify argument order (weighted sum)
    self
      assert: (mk
        first: 1
        second: 2
        third: 3
        fourth: 4)
      equals: 1234
    // Different argument values to confirm ordering
    self
      assert: (mk
        first: 9
        second: 0
        third: 5
        fourth: 7)
      equals: 9057
    // 5-keyword method: sum five arguments
    self
      assert: (mk
        add: 10
        and: 20
        and: 30
        and: 40
        and: 50)
      equals: 150
    // Verify state was updated by add:and:and:and:and:
    self assert: mk getResult equals: 150
entries.explanation: Keyword message tests (block value: dispatch)

entries.title: ListLiteralsTest
entries.source:
// E2E tests for list literal syntax #(1, 2, 3) (ADR 0012, BT-402)

TestCase subclass: ListLiteralsTest

  testEmptyList =>
    self assert: #() equals: #()
    self assert: #() size equals: 0
    self assert: #() isEmpty

  testSingleElementList =>
    self assert: #(256) equals: #(256)
    self assert: #(256) size equals: 1
    self deny: #(256) isEmpty

  testMultipleElementList =>
    self assert: #(1, 2, 3) equals: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: #(1, 2, 3) size equals: 3

  testNestedLists =>
    self assert: #(#(1, 2), #(3, 4)) equals: #(#(1, 2), #(3, 4))

  testListMethods =>
    self assert: #(1, 2, 3) first equals: 1
    self assert: #(1, 2, 3) rest equals: #(2, 3)

  // Trailing commas are accepted by the parser and normalized away (formatter removes them)
  testTrailingComma => self assert: #(10, 20, 30) equals: #(10, 20, 30)

  testListWithExpressionsParenthesizedForBinaryOps =>
    self assert: #((1 + 2), (3 * 4)) equals: #(3, 12)

  testNewFromList =>
    self assert: (List new: #(1, 2, 3)) equals: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: (List new: #()) equals: #()

  testSetNewFromList => self assert: (Set new: #(1, 2, 2, 3)) size equals: 3

  testTupleNewFromList => self assert: (Tuple new: #(1, 2, 3)) size equals: 3

entries.title: MetaclassTest
entries.source:
// BT-412, BT-803, ADR 0036 Phase 2: Tests the metaclass tower — primitive class
// identity (42 class == Integer), isMeta/isClass predicates, metaclass name,
// thisClass, superclass hierarchy, self-grounding fixed point, and sealed Metaclass

TestCase subclass: MetaclassTest

  testPrimitiveClassIdentityAndTower =>
    self assert: 42 class equals: Integer
    self assert: 3.14 class equals: Float
    self assert: "hello" class equals: String
    self assert: true class equals: True
    self assert: false class equals: False
    self assert: nil class equals: UndefinedObject
    self assert: #symbol class equals: Symbol
    // BT-802 (ADR 0036 Phase 1): Metaclass objects replace the #Metaclass sentinel
    self assert: (42 class class) isMeta equals: true
    self assert: ("hello" class class) isMeta equals: true
    self assert: (true class class) isMeta equals: true
    // Metaclass objects are not plain classes
    self assert: (42 class class) isClass equals: false
    // Metaclass tower terminates: self-grounding invariant (class class class == class class)
    self assert: 42 class class class equals: 42 class class

  // BT-803: isMetaclass predicate
  testIsMetaclass =>
    self assert: (42 class class) isMetaclass equals: true
    self assert: ("hello" class class) isMetaclass equals: true
    self assert: (true class class) isMetaclass equals: true
    self assert: (nil class class) isMetaclass equals: true
    // isBehaviour is inherited from Behaviour
    self assert: (42 class class) isBehaviour equals: true
    // Regular class objects are not metaclasses
    self assert: (42 class) isMetaclass equals: false
    self assert: Integer isMetaclass equals: false

  // BT-803: name — e.g. 42 class class name == 'Integer class'
  testName =>
    self assert: (42 class class) name equals: "Integer class"
    self assert: (3.14 class class) name equals: "Float class"
    self assert: ("hello" class class) name equals: "String class"
    self assert: (true class class) name equals: "True class"
    self assert: (false class class) name equals: "False class"
    self assert: (nil class class) name equals: "UndefinedObject class"
    self assert: (#sym class class) name equals: "Symbol class"

  // BT-803: thisClass — e.g. 42 class class thisClass == Integer
  testThisClass =>
    self assert: (42 class class) thisClass equals: Integer
    self assert: (3.14 class class) thisClass equals: Float
    self assert: ("hello" class class) thisClass equals: String
    self assert: (true class class) thisClass equals: True
    self assert: (false class class) thisClass equals: False
    self assert: (nil class class) thisClass equals: UndefinedObject

  // BT-803: printString — same as name
  testPrintString =>
    self assert: (42 class class) printString equals: "Integer class"
    self assert: (3.14 class class) printString equals: "Float class"
    self assert: ("hello" class class) printString equals: "String class"

  // BT-803: superclass invariant — Counter class superclass == Actor class
  testSuperclassInvariant =>
    // Parallel hierarchy: Counter class class superclass == Actor class class
    self assert: Counter class class superclass equals: Actor class class
    // Actor's metaclass superclass is Object's metaclass
    self assert: Actor class class superclass equals: Object class class
    // Object's metaclass superclass is ProtoObject's metaclass
    self assert: Object class class superclass equals: ProtoObject class class

  // BT-803: self-grounding fixed point — Metaclass class class == Metaclass class
  testSelfGrounding =>
    self assert: Metaclass class class equals: Metaclass class
    // Three-deep also terminates
    self assert: Metaclass class class class equals: Metaclass class class

  // BT-803: Metaclass allSubclasses returns [] (sealed)
  testAllSubclassesSealed => self assert: Metaclass allSubclasses isEmpty

  // BT-803: Metaclass new raises error
  testNewRaisesError => self should: [Metaclass new] raise: #user_error

  // BT-1169: allMethods includes class-side 'spawn' on Actor subclasses
  testAllMethodsIncludesSpawn =>
    methods := Counter class allMethods
    self assert: (methods includes: #spawn)

  // BT-1169: allMethods includes Behaviour protocol 'reload'
  testAllMethodsIncludesReload =>
    methods := Counter class allMethods
    self assert: (methods includes: #reload)

  // BT-1169: allMethods includes Behaviour protocol 'superclass'
  testAllMethodsIncludesSuperclass =>
    methods := Counter class allMethods
    self assert: (methods includes: #superclass)

  // BT-1169: allMethods is non-empty
  testAllMethodsNonEmpty =>
    methods := Counter class allMethods
    self deny: methods isEmpty

  // BT-1169: localClassMethods unchanged — Counter has no local class-side methods
  testLocalClassMethodsUnchanged =>
    localMethods := Counter class localClassMethods
    self deny: (localMethods includes: #reload)

  // Class identity tests for domain classes
  testClassIdentityForDomainClasses =>
    // Random class isMeta returns true (BT-802 / ADR 0036)
    self assert: (Random class) isMeta equals: true
    // Regex class isMeta returns true (BT-802 / ADR 0036)
    self assert: (Regex class) isMeta equals: true
entries.explanation: BT-412, BT-803, ADR 0036 Phase 2: Tests the metaclass tower — primitive class identity (42 class == Integer), isMeta/isClass predicates, metaclass name, thisClass, superclass hierarchy, self-grounding fixed point, and sealed Metaclass

entries.title: MixedCallSiteTest
entries.explanation: BT-870: Mixed Tier 1/Tier 2 call site test (ADR 0041) Verifies that a HOM called from one method with a plain Tier 1 block and from another method with a Tier 2 (captured-mutation) block works correctly for both  The Tier 1 call site was previously a runtime crash: the Tier 2 method body calls blocks with arity 2 but the un-promoted block had arity 1

entries.title: MutationMatrixTest
entries.source:
// BT-1480: State mutation test matrix — proves compiler handles all
// control flow × nesting × mutation patterns for Actor state threading.
// Each test exercises one specific pattern and asserts correct state.
//
// Also covers BT-1479 control-flow RHS patterns (P2-19 through P2-21).
// P2-16 (anySatisfy:/allSatisfy:) fixed in BT-1481.
// P3 tests (BT-1485) are commented out — they expose state threading bugs
// for remaining block-accepting selectors (detect:, count:, flatMap:, etc.).

TestCase subclass: MutationMatrixTest

  // ── P0: Known bug patterns (verify fixes) ──────────────────────────

  // P0-1: collect: + ifTrue: + mutation (BT-1477)
  testCollectIfTrueMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a collectIfTrueMutation: #(1, -2, 3)
    self assert: count equals: 2
    self assert: a getLog equals: #(2, -4, 6)

  // P0-2: select: + ifTrue: + mutation (BT-1477)
  testSelectIfTrueMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a selectIfTrueMutation: #(1, -2, 3, -4)
    self assert: count equals: 4
    self assert: a getLog equals: #(1, 3)

  // P0-3: Sequential mutations across multiple statements
  testSequentialMutations =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a sequentialMutations
    self assert: count equals: 22
    self assert: a getLog equals: #(11)

  // P0-4: Mutation inside nested conditional blocks
  testNestedConditionalMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    // Both true: inner mutation (100) + outer mutation (10) = 110
    count := a nestedConditionalMutation: true and: true
    self assert: count equals: 110
    a reset
    // Outer true, inner false: only outer mutation (10)
    count := a nestedConditionalMutation: true and: false
    self assert: count equals: 10
    a reset
    // Outer false: no mutation
    count := a nestedConditionalMutation: false and: true
    self assert: count equals: 0

  // ── P1: High risk unexplored patterns ──────────────────────────────

  // P1-5: collect: inside collect: + mutation
  testNestedCollectMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a nestedCollectMutation: #(#(1, 2), #(3, 4))
    self assert: count equals: 4
    self assert: a getLog equals: #(#(2, 4), #(6, 8))

  // P1-6: do: inside collect: + mutation
  testDoInsideCollectMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a doInsideCollectMutation: #(#(1, 2), #(3, 4))
    self assert: count equals: 10
    self assert: a getLog equals: #(2, 2)

  // P1-7: inject:into: + ifTrue: + mutation
  testInjectIfTrueMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a injectIfTrueMutation: #(1, -2, 3, -4, 5)
    self assert: count equals: 3
    self assert: a getLog equals: #(9)

  // P1-8: detect:ifNone: + mutation before match (no match)
  testDetectIfNoneMutationNoMatch =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a detectIfNoneMutation: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: count equals: 99
    self assert: a getLog equals: #(0)

  // P1-8b: detect:ifNone: + mutation before match (with match)
  testDetectIfNoneMutationWithMatch =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a detectIfNoneMutation: #(1, 20, 3)
    self assert: count equals: 99
    self assert: a getLog equals: #(20)

  // P1-9: on:do: exception handler + mutation
  testExceptionMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a exceptionMutation
    self assert: count equals: 0

  // P1-10: Non-local return from inside collect: after mutation
  testNonLocalReturnFromCollect =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    result := a nonLocalReturnFromCollect: #(10, 20, 30, 40, 50)
    self assert: result equals: 3
    self assert: a getCounter equals: 3

  // P1-11: Multiple field mutations in single collect: block
  testMultiFieldMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    result := a multiFieldMutation: #(1, -2, 3)
    self assert: result equals: #(2, -4, 6)
    self assert: a getCounter equals: 3
    self assert: a getLog equals: #(1, -2, 3)
    self assert: a getFlag equals: true

  // P1-12: ifTrue:ifFalse: inside do: with mutation
  testIfTrueIfFalseInsideDo =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a ifTrueIfFalseInsideDo: #(3, -1, 5, -2)
    self assert: count equals: 11

  // ── P2: Edge cases ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

  // P2-13: collect: + ifTrue: + mutation + non-local return
  testCollectIfTrueNlr =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    result := a collectIfTrueNlr: #(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
    self assert: result equals: 4
    self assert: a getCounter equals: 4

  // P2-14: 3-deep nesting: do: > collect: > ifTrue: + mutation
  testThreeDeepNesting =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a threeDeepNesting: #(#(1, -2, 3), #(-4, 5))
    self assert: count equals: 3

  // P2-17: reject: + mutation in filter block
  testRejectMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a rejectMutation: #(1, -2, 3, -4)
    self assert: count equals: 4
    self assert: a getLog equals: #(1, 3)

  // P2-18: Empty collection + mutation in iteration block
  testEmptyCollectionMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a emptyCollectionMutation
    self assert: count equals: 42

  // ── BT-1479: Control flow RHS patterns ─────────────────────────────

  // P2-19: Field assign from conditional with mutation in RHS
  testFieldAssignFromConditionalRhs =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a fieldAssignFromConditionalRhs: true
    self assert: count equals: 42
    self assert: a getFlag equals: true
    a reset
    count := a fieldAssignFromConditionalRhs: false
    self assert: count equals: 0

  // P2-20: Field assign from collect: with mutation in RHS
  testFieldAssignFromCollectRhs =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a fieldAssignFromCollectRhs: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: count equals: 3
    self assert: a getLog equals: #(2, 4, 6)

  // P2-21: fieldAt:put: inside conditional branch
  testFieldAtPutInConditional =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    a fieldAtPutInConditional: true
    self assert: a getCounter equals: 99
    a reset
    a fieldAtPutInConditional: false
    self assert: a getCounter equals: 0

  // ── BT-1481: anySatisfy:/allSatisfy: state threading ──────────────

  // P2-16: anySatisfy: block with mutation
  testAnySatisfyMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a anySatisfyMutation: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: a getFlag equals: false
    self assert: count equals: 3

  // P2-16b: allSatisfy: with a match
  testAllSatisfyMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a allSatisfyMutation: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: a getFlag equals: true
    self assert: count equals: 3

  // P2-22: Block value with mutation (BT-1481)
  testBlockValueMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a blockValueMutation
    self assert: count equals: 42

  // ── BT-1485: Remaining block-accepting selectors ─────────────────────
  // Most tests below remain commented out — they expose state threading bugs
  // where the compiler loses mutated state across block-accepting selectors.
  // List high-risk cases P3-1..P3-4 are enabled (BT-1486).
  // Dictionary cases P3-10..P3-11 are enabled (BT-1488).
  // String cases P3-12..P3-15 are enabled (BT-1489).
  // Uncomment additional cases when the corresponding codegen fixes land.

  // ── List (high risk) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

  // P3-1: detect: with field mutation in predicate block (BT-1486)
  // Mutation in the predicate block should persist after detect: returns.
  testDetectMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a detectMutation: #(1, 20, 3)
    self assert: count equals: 3
    self assert: a getLog equals: #(20)

  // P3-1b: detect: with no match (returns nil) + mutation (BT-1486)
  testDetectMutationNoMatch =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a detectMutation: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: count equals: 3
    self assert: a getLog equals: #(nil)

  // P3-2: detect:ifNone: with field mutation in predicate block (BT-1486)
  // Unlike P1-8 which mutates *before* detect:ifNone:, this mutates *inside* the predicate.
  // All elements are processed (no short-circuit) to ensure mutations are applied.
  testDetectIfNonePredicateMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a detectIfNonePredicateMutation: #(1, 20, 3)
    self assert: count equals: 3
    self assert: a getLog equals: #(20)

  // P3-2b: detect:ifNone: with mutation in predicate, no match (BT-1486)
  testDetectIfNonePredicateMutationNoMatch =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a detectIfNonePredicateMutation: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: count equals: 3
    self assert: a getLog equals: #(-1)

  // P3-3: count: with field mutation in predicate block (BT-1486)
  testCountMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a countMutation: #(1, -2, 3)
    self assert: count equals: 3
    self assert: a getLog equals: #(2)

  // P3-4: flatMap: with field mutation in mapping block (BT-1486)
  testFlatMapMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a flatMapMutation: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: count equals: 3
    self assert: a getLog equals: #(1, 10, 2, 20, 3, 30)

  // ── List (medium risk) ────────────────────────────────────────────────

  // P3-5: takeWhile: with field mutation in predicate block (BT-1487)
  // With foldl-based approach, ALL elements are processed (counter = 4),
  // but only elements before first predicate failure are included.
  testTakeWhileMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a takeWhileMutation: #(1, 2, 30, 4)
    self assert: count equals: 4
    self assert: a getLog equals: #(1, 2)

  // P3-6: dropWhile: with field mutation in predicate block (BT-1487)
  // With foldl-based approach, ALL elements are processed (counter = 4).
  testDropWhileMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a dropWhileMutation: #(1, 2, 30, 4)
    self assert: count equals: 4
    self assert: a getLog equals: #(30, 4)

  // P3-7: groupBy: with field mutation in key block (BT-1487)
  testGroupByMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a groupByMutation: #(1, -2, 3, -4)
    self assert: count equals: 4

  // P3-8: partition: with field mutation in predicate block (BT-1487)
  testPartitionMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a partitionMutation: #(1, -2, 3)
    self assert: count equals: 3
    self assert: a getLog equals: #(#(1, 3), #(-2))

  // P3-9: sort: with field mutation in comparator block (BT-1487)
  testSortMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a sortMutation: #(3, 1, 2)
    // count depends on sort algorithm's comparison count, assert > 0
    self assert: count > 0 equals: true
    self assert: a getLog equals: #(1, 2, 3)

  // ── Dictionary ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  // P3-10: Dictionary do: with field mutation in iteration block (BT-1488)
  testDictDoMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a dictDoMutation: #{#a => 10, #b => 20}
    self assert: count equals: 30

  // P3-11: Dictionary doWithKey: with field mutation (BT-1488)
  // Verifies both counter and log mutations are threaded through doWithKey:
  testDictDoWithKeyMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a dictDoWithKeyMutation: #{#a => 10, #b => 20}
    self assert: count equals: 30
    self assert: (a getLog size) equals: 2

  // ── String ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  // P3-12: String do: with field mutation in iteration block (BT-1489)
  testStringDoMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a stringDoMutation: "abc"
    self assert: count equals: 3

  // P3-13: String collect: with field mutation in mapping block (BT-1489)
  testStringCollectMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a stringCollectMutation: "abc"
    self assert: count equals: 3
    self assert: a getLog equals: #("abc")

  // P3-14: String select: with field mutation in predicate block (BT-1489)
  testStringSelectMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a stringSelectMutation: "abc"
    self assert: count equals: 3
    self assert: a getLog equals: #("ac")

  // P3-15: String reject: with field mutation in predicate block (BT-1489)
  testStringRejectMutation =>
    a := MutationMatrixActor spawn
    count := a stringRejectMutation: "abc"
    self assert: count equals: 3
    self assert: a getLog equals: #("ac")
entries.explanation: BT-1480: State mutation test matrix — proves compiler handles all control flow × nesting × mutation patterns for Actor state threading. Each test exercises one specific pattern and asserts correct state.  Also covers BT-1479 control-flow RHS patterns (P2-19 through P2-21). P2-16 (anySatisfy:/allSatisfy:) fixed in BT-1481. P3 tests (BT-1485) are commented out — they expose state threading bugs for remaining block-accepting selectors (detect:, count:, flatMap:, etc.).

entries.title: NlrStateThreadingTest
entries.source:
// BT-854/BT-857: Tests NLR (non-local return) for value types
// Verifies that ^ inside blocks works correctly for Value subclass fixtures

TestCase subclass: NlrStateThreadingTest

  testFindFirstReturnsCorrectValue =>
    // NLR returns the matched element via ^each
    obj := NlrFieldMutation new
    result := obj findFirst: 3 in: #(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
    self assert: result equals: 3

  testFindFirstNoMatchReturnsNil =>
    // Normal (non-NLR) path: method returns nil
    obj := NlrFieldMutation new
    result := obj findFirst: 100 in: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: result equals: nil

  testFindAndReturnSelfUpdatesField =>
    // findAndReturnSelf: uses withLastQuery: then returns updated via ^
    obj := NlrFieldMutation new
    updated := obj findAndReturnSelf: 3 in: #(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
    self assert: updated lastQuery equals: 3

  testDetectReturnsMatchingElement =>
    // detect:in: uses a block predicate and returns the matched element via ^
    obj := NlrFieldMutation new
    result := obj detect: [:x | x > 2] in: #(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
    self assert: result equals: 3

  testDetectNoMatchReturnsNil =>
    // detect:in: returns nil when no element satisfies the predicate
    obj := NlrFieldMutation new
    result := obj detect: [:x | x > 100] in: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: result equals: nil

  testFindNoneNlrNeverFires =>
    // Method has NLR infrastructure but ^ never fires
    obj := NlrFieldMutation new
    result := obj findNone: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: result equals: nil

  testFindAndReturnSelfNoMatchReturnsSelf =>
    // Normal (non-NLR) path: findAndReturnSelf:in: returns updated at end of method
    obj := NlrFieldMutation new
    updated := obj findAndReturnSelf: 100 in: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: updated lastQuery equals: 100

  // BT-857: anySatisfy: — early return via ^
  testAnySatisfyReturnsTrueOnMatch =>
    // anySatisfy:in: uses ^ to short-circuit and return true when match found
    obj := NlrFieldMutation new
    result := obj anySatisfy: [:x | x > 3] in: #(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
    self assert: result equals: true

  testAnySatisfyReturnsFalseOnNoMatch =>
    // Normal (non-NLR) path: all elements fail the predicate; returns false
    obj := NlrFieldMutation new
    result := obj anySatisfy: [:x | x > 100] in: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: result equals: false

  // BT-857: ^ inside nested HOM — NLR propagates through multiple frames
  //
  // findViaSingleHOM:in: and findViaNestedHOM:in: use withVisitCount: to create
  // an updated instance, then return it via ^ through nested HOM frames.
  // The caller verifies visitCount on the returned object.
  testFindViaSingleHOMPreservesFieldMutation =>
    // NLR propagates through one HOM frame; visitCount set to 1 via withVisitCount:
    obj := NlrNestedHom new
    result := obj findViaSingleHOM: 3 in: #(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
    self assert: result visitCount equals: 1

  testFindViaNestedHOMPreservesFieldMutation =>
    // NLR propagates through two HOM frames; visitCount set to 2 via withVisitCount:
    obj := NlrNestedHom new
    result := obj findViaNestedHOM: 3 in: #(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
    self assert: result visitCount equals: 2

  testFindViaSingleHOMNoMatchPreservesFieldMutation =>
    // Non-NLR path through one HOM: ^ never fires, method returns updated
    obj := NlrNestedHom new
    result := obj findViaSingleHOM: 99 in: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: result visitCount equals: 1

  testFindViaNestedHOMNoMatchPreservesFieldMutation =>
    // Non-NLR path through two nested HOMs: ^ never fires, method returns updated
    obj := NlrNestedHom new
    result := obj findViaNestedHOM: 99 in: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: result visitCount equals: 2
entries.explanation: BT-854/BT-857: Tests NLR (non-local return) for value types Verifies that ^ inside blocks works correctly for Value subclass fixtures

entries.title: NumberMethodsTest
entries.source:
// Number methods tests - rounding, predicates, comparisons (migrated from bootstrap/float.bt BT-827)

TestCase subclass: NumberMethodsTest

  testFloatRounded =>
    // rounded — round to nearest integer
    self assert: 3.14 rounded equals: 3
    self assert: 3.75 rounded equals: 4

  testFloatCeiling =>
    // ceiling — smallest integer >= value
    self assert: 3.14 ceiling equals: 4

  testFloatFloor =>
    // floor — largest integer <= value
    self assert: 3.75 floor equals: 3

  testFloatTruncated =>
    // truncated — toward zero
    self assert: 3.75 truncated equals: 3
    self assert: -3.75 truncated equals: -3

  testNegativeRoundingEdgeCases =>
    self assert: -3.14 rounded equals: -3
    self assert: -3.75 rounded equals: -4
    self assert: -3.14 ceiling equals: -3
    self assert: -3.75 floor equals: -4

  testFloatIsZero =>
    self assert: 0.0 isZero
    self deny: 3.14 isZero

  testFloatIsPositive =>
    self assert: 3.14 isPositive
    self deny: -2.5 isPositive

  testFloatIsNegative =>
    self assert: -2.5 isNegative
    self deny: 3.14 isNegative

  testIntegerIsZero =>
    self assert: 0 isZero
    self deny: 5 isZero

  testIntegerIsPositive => self assert: 5 isPositive

  testIntegerIsNegative => self assert: -3 isNegative

  testFloatNegated => self assert: 3.14 negated equals: -3.14

  testFloatAbs => self assert: -3.14 abs equals: 3.14

  testFloatMinMax =>
    self assert: (3.14 min: 2.71) equals: 2.71
    self assert: (3.14 max: 2.71) equals: 3.14

  testFloatAsInteger =>
    // asInteger (truncates)
    self assert: 3.75 asInteger equals: 3

  testFloatAsString => self assert: 3.14 asString equals: "3.14"

  testFloatIsNaN => self deny: 3.14 isNaN

  testFloatIsInfinite => self deny: 3.14 isInfinite

  testIntegerSign =>
    self assert: 5 sign equals: 1
    self assert: -3 sign equals: -1
    self assert: 0 sign equals: 0

  testIntegerBetween =>
    self assert: (5 between: 1 and: 10)
    self deny: (15 between: 1 and: 10)

  testFloatSign =>
    self assert: 3.14 sign equals: 1
    self assert: -2.5 sign equals: -1

  testFloatBetween => self assert: (3.14 between: 1.0 and: 5.0)

  testRespondsToSign =>
    self assert: (42 respondsTo: #sign)
    self assert: (3.14 respondsTo: #sign)

  testRespondsToBetweenAnd =>
    self assert: (42 respondsTo: #between:and:)
    self assert: (3.14 respondsTo: #between:and:)
entries.explanation: Number methods tests - rounding, predicates, comparisons (migrated from bootstrap/float.bt BT-827)

entries.title: OSTest
entries.source:
// Stdlib tests for OS class (BT-1244, BT-1247)

TestCase subclass: OSTest
  field: isUnix = false

  setUp => self withIsUnix: (System osFamily == "unix")

  testRunReturnsString =>
    // echo is a shell builtin on both Unix and Windows
    result := OS run: "echo hello"
    self assert: result class equals: String

  // => _
  testRunTrimsTrailingWhitespace =>
    result := OS run: "echo hello"
    self assert: result equals: "hello"

  // => _
  testRunWithNonStringRaisesTypeError =>
    @expect type
    self should: [OS run: 42] raise: #type_error

  testRunTimeoutReturnsString =>
    result := OS run: "echo hello" timeout: 5000
    self assert: result equals: "hello"

  // => _
  testRunTimeoutWithNonStringRaisesTypeError =>
    @expect type
    self should: [OS run: 42 timeout: 5000] raise: #type_error

  testRunTimeoutExpiryRaisesTimeout =>
    cmd := self.isUnix ifTrue: ["sleep 60"] ifFalse: ["ping -n 100 127.0.0.1"]
    self should: [OS run: cmd timeout: 100] raise: #timeout
// => _
entries.explanation: Stdlib tests for OS class (BT-1244, BT-1247)

entries.title: PidTest
entries.source:
// BT-1553: Tests for Pid kill and exit: methods

TestCase subclass: PidTest

  testKillTerminatesProcess =>
    // Spawn a process that sleeps forever
    pid := Erlang erlang
      spawn: #timer
      module: #sleep
      args: #(#infinity)
    self assert: pid isAlive
    // Unlink so the kill signal doesn't propagate to the test process
    Erlang erlang unlink: pid
    pid kill
    // Poll until dead
    50 timesRepeat: [pid isAlive ifTrue: [Timer sleep: 10]]
    self deny: pid isAlive

  testExitWithReasonTerminatesProcess =>
    // Spawn a process that sleeps forever
    pid := Erlang erlang
      spawn: #timer
      module: #sleep
      args: #(#infinity)
    self assert: pid isAlive
    Erlang erlang unlink: pid
    pid exit: #shutdown
    // Poll until dead
    50 timesRepeat: [pid isAlive ifTrue: [Timer sleep: 10]]
    self deny: pid isAlive

  testKillReturnValue =>
    pid := Erlang erlang
      spawn: #timer
      module: #sleep
      args: #(#infinity)
    Erlang erlang unlink: pid
    result := pid kill
    self assert: result equals: true
    // Wait for process to terminate
    50 timesRepeat: [pid isAlive ifTrue: [Timer sleep: 10]]
    self deny: pid isAlive

  testExitReturnValue =>
    pid := Erlang erlang
      spawn: #timer
      module: #sleep
      args: #(#infinity)
    Erlang erlang unlink: pid
    result := pid exit: #shutdown
    self assert: result equals: true
    // Wait for process to terminate
    50 timesRepeat: [pid isAlive ifTrue: [Timer sleep: 10]]
    self deny: pid isAlive
entries.explanation: BT-1553: Tests for Pid kill and exit: methods

entries.title: ProtoobjectManualTest
entries.source:
// ProtoObject behavior tests - the root of the class hierarchy

TestCase subclass: ProtoobjectManualTest

  testIdentityComparison =>
    self assert: (42 == 42)
    self assert: (42 /= 43)
    self assert: (nil == nil)
    self deny: (nil == false)
    self assert: (nil /= false)
    self deny: (nil /= nil)

  testDoesNotUnderstandRaisesError =>
    // ProtoObject doesNotUnderstand:args: raises does_not_understand (BT-1158)
    @expect dnu
    self should: [42 noSuchMessage] raise: #does_not_understand
    @expect dnu
    self should: ["hello" noSuchMessage] raise: #does_not_understand
    @expect dnu
    self should: [nil noSuchMessage] raise: #does_not_understand

  testDoesNotUnderstandCarriesSelector =>
    // The error payload includes the unknown selector name
    @expect dnu
    result := [42 noSuchMessage] on: Exception do: [:e | e selector]
    self assert: result equals: #noSuchMessage

  testPerformWithargumentsDynamicDispatch =>
    self assert: (42 perform: #'+' withArguments: #(3)) equals: 45
    self assert: (42 perform: #asString withArguments: #()) equals: "42"
    self assert: ("hello" perform: #'++' withArguments: #(" world")) equals: "hello world"
entries.explanation: ProtoObject behavior tests - the root of the class hierarchy

entries.title: QueueTest
entries.source:
// BUnit tests for Queue class (BT-1250)

TestCase subclass: QueueTest

  testNewIsEmpty =>
    q := Queue new
    self assert: q isEmpty

  testNewSizeIsZero =>
    q := Queue new
    self assert: q size equals: 0

  testEnqueueIncreasesSize =>
    q := Queue new
    q2 := q enqueue: 1
    self assert: q2 size equals: 1

  testEnqueueDoesNotMutateOriginal =>
    q := Queue new
    q enqueue: 42
    self assert: q isEmpty

  testDequeueReturnsTupleWithValueAndQueue =>
    q := (Queue new) enqueue: 99
    result := q dequeue
    self assert: (result at: 1) equals: 99
    self assert: (result at: 2) isEmpty

  testDequeueReducesSize =>
    q := ((Queue new) enqueue: 1) enqueue: 2
    result := q dequeue
    self assert: (result at: 2) size equals: 1

  testDequeueOnEmptyQueueRaisesError =>
    self should: [Queue new dequeue] raise: #empty_queue

  testPeekReturnsFrontElement =>
    q := (Queue new) enqueue: 7
    self assert: q peek equals: 7

  testPeekDoesNotReduceSize =>
    q := (Queue new) enqueue: 7
    q peek
    self assert: q size equals: 1

  testPeekOnEmptyQueueRaisesError =>
    self should: [Queue new peek] raise: #empty_queue

  testIsEmptyFalseAfterEnqueue =>
    q := (Queue new) enqueue: 1
    self deny: q isEmpty

  testIsEmptyTrueAfterDequeueAll =>
    q := (Queue new) enqueue: 1
    result := q dequeue
    self assert: (result at: 2) isEmpty

  testFifoOrder =>
    q0 := Queue new
    q1 := q0 enqueue: "first"
    q2 := q1 enqueue: "second"
    q3 := q2 enqueue: "third"
    r1 := q3 dequeue
    r2 := (r1 at: 2) dequeue
    r3 := (r2 at: 2) dequeue
    self assert: (r1 at: 1) equals: "first"
    self assert: (r2 at: 1) equals: "second"
    self assert: (r3 at: 1) equals: "third"

  testPeekMatchesDequeue =>
    q := (Queue new) enqueue: "hello"
    front := q peek
    result := q dequeue
    self assert: front equals: (result at: 1)

  testEnqueueMultipleTypes =>
    q := Queue new
    q2 := ((q enqueue: 1) enqueue: "two") enqueue: #three
    self assert: q2 size equals: 3
entries.explanation: BUnit tests for Queue class (BT-1250)

entries.title: ReactiveSubprocessTest
entries.source:
// BT-1187: BUnit tests for ReactiveSubprocess actor — push-mode stdout/stderr
// delivery, exit notification, writeLine:, close, exitCode.
//
// Cross-platform: uses platform-appropriate commands on Unix and Windows.
// Interactive stdin-echo tests use beamtalk-exec --cat on Windows for line-buffered output.
//
// StubDelegate actor is defined in fixtures/stub_delegate_fixture.bt.

TestCase subclass: ReactiveSubprocessTest
  field: isUnix = false

  setUp => self withIsUnix: (System osFamily == "unix")

  // --- Platform command helpers ---
  shellExe => self.isUnix ifTrue: ["/bin/sh"] ifFalse: ["cmd"]

  shellArgs: cmd => self.isUnix ifTrue: [#("-c", cmd)] ifFalse: [#("/c", cmd)]

  sleepExe => self.isUnix ifTrue: ["/bin/sleep"] ifFalse: ["cmd"]

  sleepArgs =>
    self.isUnix ifTrue: [
      #("10")
    ] ifFalse: [#("/c", "ping -n 60 127.0.0.1 >nul")]

  trueExe => self.isUnix ifTrue: ["/usr/bin/env"] ifFalse: ["cmd"]

  trueArgs => self.isUnix ifTrue: [#("true")] ifFalse: [#("/c", "exit 0")]

  multilineScript =>
    self.isUnix ifTrue: [
      "printf 'alpha\nbeta\ngamma\n'"
    ] ifFalse: ["(echo alpha& echo beta& echo gamma)"]

  stderrMultilineScript =>
    self.isUnix ifTrue: [
      "printf 'x\ny\nz\n' >&2"
    ] ifFalse: ["(echo x& echo y& echo z)>&2"]

  partialLineScript =>
    self.isUnix ifTrue: ["printf hello"] ifFalse: ["<nul set /p =hello"]

  stdoutStderrScript =>
    self.isUnix ifTrue: [
      "echo out; echo err >&2"
    ] ifFalse: ["echo out& echo err>&2"]

  envScript =>
    self.isUnix ifTrue: [
      "echo $BT_TEST_SEED; pwd"
    ] ifFalse: ["echo %BT_TEST_SEED%& cd"]

  envDir => self.isUnix ifTrue: ["/tmp"] ifFalse: [System getEnv: "TEMP"]

  // cat executable: echoes stdin to stdout.
  // On Unix uses /bin/cat; on Windows uses beamtalk-exec --cat (built-in
  // cat mode with explicit flushing, bypassing pipe buffering).
  catExe => self.isUnix ifTrue: ["/bin/cat"] ifFalse: ["beamtalk-exec"]

  catArgs => self.isUnix ifTrue: [#()] ifFalse: [#("--cat")]

  // --- Tests ---
  testStdoutLinesPushedToNotify =>
    delegate := StubDelegate spawn
    proc := (ReactiveSubprocess
      open: self shellExe
      args: (self shellArgs: self multilineScript)
      notify: delegate) unwrap
    // Wait for exit
    self waitForExit: delegate from: proc
    self assert: delegate stdoutLines equals: #("alpha", "beta", "gamma")
    self assert: delegate stop equals: #ok
    self assert: proc stop equals: #ok

  testStderrLinesPushedToNotify =>
    delegate := StubDelegate spawn
    proc := (ReactiveSubprocess
      open: self shellExe
      args: (self shellArgs: self stderrMultilineScript)
      notify: delegate) unwrap
    self waitForExit: delegate from: proc
    self assert: delegate stderrLines equals: #("x", "y", "z")
    self assert: delegate stdoutLines equals: #()
    self assert: delegate stop equals: #ok
    self assert: proc stop equals: #ok

  testExitCodePushedToNotify =>
    delegate := StubDelegate spawn
    proc := (ReactiveSubprocess
      open: self trueExe
      args: self trueArgs
      notify: delegate) unwrap
    self waitForExit: delegate from: proc
    self assert: delegate exitCode equals: 0
    self assert: delegate stop equals: #ok
    self assert: proc stop equals: #ok

  testPartialLineFlushedOnExit =>
    delegate := StubDelegate spawn
    proc := (ReactiveSubprocess
      open: self shellExe
      args: (self shellArgs: self partialLineScript)
      notify: delegate) unwrap
    self waitForExit: delegate from: proc
    self assert: delegate stdoutLines equals: #("hello")
    self assert: delegate stop equals: #ok
    self assert: proc stop equals: #ok

  testStdoutAndStderrIndependent =>
    delegate := StubDelegate spawn
    proc := (ReactiveSubprocess
      open: self shellExe
      args: (self shellArgs: self stdoutStderrScript)
      notify: delegate) unwrap
    self waitForExit: delegate from: proc
    self assert: delegate stdoutLines equals: #("out")
    self assert: delegate stderrLines equals: #("err")
    self assert: delegate stop equals: #ok
    self assert: proc stop equals: #ok

  testWriteLineDeliversThroughCat =>
    delegate := StubDelegate spawn
    proc := (ReactiveSubprocess
      open: self catExe
      args: self catArgs
      notify: delegate) unwrap
    proc writeLine: "hello"
    // Poll up to 500 ms (50 × 10 ms) for cat to echo back
    50 timesRepeat: [
      (delegate stdoutLines includes: "hello") ifFalse: [Timer sleep: 10]
    ]
    self assert: (delegate stdoutLines includes: "hello")
    proc close
    self assert: delegate stop equals: #ok
    self assert: proc stop equals: #ok

  testExitCodeNilWhileRunning =>
    delegate := StubDelegate spawn
    proc := (ReactiveSubprocess
      open: self sleepExe
      args: self sleepArgs
      notify: delegate) unwrap
    self assert: proc exitCode equals: nil
    proc close
    self assert: delegate stop equals: #ok
    self assert: proc stop equals: #ok

  testCloseIsIdempotent =>
    delegate := StubDelegate spawn
    proc := (ReactiveSubprocess
      open: self sleepExe
      args: self sleepArgs
      notify: delegate) unwrap
    self assert: proc close equals: nil
    self assert: proc close equals: nil
    self assert: delegate stop equals: #ok
    self assert: proc stop equals: #ok

  testOpenWithEnvAndDir =>
    delegate := StubDelegate spawn
    // Print the env var and the working directory to verify both are passed through
    proc := (ReactiveSubprocess
      open: self shellExe
      args: (self shellArgs: self envScript)
      env: #{"BT_TEST_SEED" => "hello1187"}
      dir: self envDir
      notify: delegate) unwrap
    self waitForExit: delegate from: proc
    self assert: (delegate stdoutLines includes: "hello1187")
    // On Windows, cd prints path with drive letter; on Unix, pwd prints /tmp
    dir := self envDir
    self assert: (delegate stdoutLines anySatisfy: [:line |
      line includesSubstring: dir
    ])
    self assert: delegate stop equals: #ok
    self assert: proc stop equals: #ok

  // Helper: poll up to 50 times (10ms each) for delegate exitReceived.
  // Uses timesRepeat: to avoid mutable local variables across block boundaries.
  waitForExit: delegate from: _proc =>
    50 timesRepeat: [delegate exitReceived ifFalse: [Timer sleep: 10]]
    delegate exitReceived ifFalse: [
      self fail: "Timed out waiting for subprocess exit notification"
    ]
entries.explanation: BT-1187: BUnit tests for ReactiveSubprocess actor — push-mode stdout/stderr delivery, exit notification, writeLine:, close, exitCode.  Cross-platform: uses platform-appropriate commands on Unix and Windows. Interactive stdin-echo tests use beamtalk-exec --cat on Windows for line-buffered output.  StubDelegate actor is defined in fixtures/stub_delegate_fixture.bt.

entries.title: ResultMatchActorTest
entries.source:
// BT-1297: Tests constructor pattern matching inside Actor methods.
// Verifies that self.slot := inside a match: arm works correctly.

TestCase subclass: ResultMatchActorTest

  testOkPatternMutatesState =>
    a := ResultMatchActor spawn
    result := a process: (Result ok: 42)
    self assert: result equals: 42
    self assert: a getLastValue equals: 42
    self assert: a getErrorCount equals: 0

  testErrorPatternIncrementsErrorCount =>
    a := ResultMatchActor spawn
    a process: (Result error: #oops)
    self assert: a getLastValue equals: 0
    self assert: a getErrorCount equals: 1

  testAlternatingResults =>
    a := ResultMatchActor spawn
    a process: (Result ok: 10)
    a process: (Result error: #fail)
    a process: (Result ok: 99)
    self assert: a getLastValue equals: 99
    self assert: a getErrorCount equals: 1
