entries.title: ResultTest
entries.source:
// BUnit tests for the Result class (BT-1254, ADR 0060).

TestCase subclass: ResultTest

  // =========================================================================
  // CONSTRUCTORS AND QUERYING
  // =========================================================================
  testOkConstructorIsOk =>
    r := Result ok: 42
    self assert: r ok

  testOkConstructorIsNotError =>
    r := Result ok: 42
    self deny: r isError

  testErrorConstructorIsError =>
    r := Result error: #not_found
    self assert: r isError

  testErrorConstructorIsNotOk =>
    r := Result error: #not_found
    self deny: r ok

  testOkWithNilValue =>
    r := Result ok: nil
    self assert: r ok
    self assert: r value equals: nil

  testOkWithStringValue =>
    r := Result ok: "hello"
    self assert: r value equals: "hello"

  // =========================================================================
  // GUARDED ACCESSORS
  // =========================================================================
  testValueOnOkResult =>
    r := Result ok: 42
    self assert: r value equals: 42

  testValueOnErrorResultRaises =>
    self should: [(Result error: #x) value] raise: #signal

  testErrorOnErrorResult =>
    r := Result error: #not_found
    self assert: r error equals: #not_found

  testErrorOnOkResultRaises =>
    self should: [(Result ok: 42) error] raise: #signal

  // =========================================================================
  // SAFE EXTRACTORS
  // =========================================================================
  testValueOrOnOkReturnsValue =>
    self assert: ((Result ok: 42) valueOr: 0) equals: 42

  testValueOrOnErrorReturnsDefault =>
    self assert: ((Result error: #x) valueOr: 0) equals: 0

  testValueOrDoOnOkReturnsValue =>
    self assert: ((Result ok: 42) valueOrDo: [:e | -1]) equals: 42

  testValueOrDoOnErrorEvaluatesBlock =>
    self assert: ((Result error: #x) valueOrDo: [:e | -1]) equals: -1

  testValueOrDoPassesErrorToBlock =>
    result := (Result error: #my_error) valueOrDo: [:e | e printString]
    self assert: result equals: "#my_error"

  // =========================================================================
  // UNWRAP
  // =========================================================================
  testUnwrapOnOkReturnsValue => self assert: (Result ok: 99) unwrap equals: 99

  testUnwrapOnErrorWithRawSymbolRaises =>
    self should: [(Result error: #oops) unwrap] raise: #signal

  testUnwrapOnErrorWithExceptionReRaises =>
    // Result tryDo: captures an exception — unwrap re-raises it
    r := Result tryDo: [Exception signal: "test error"]
    self should: [r unwrap] raise: #signal

  // =========================================================================
  // TRANSFORMERS — map:
  // =========================================================================
  testMapOnOkTransformsValue =>
    r := (Result ok: 42) map: [:v | v + 1]
    self assert: r ok
    self assert: r value equals: 43

  testMapOnErrorPropagatesError =>
    r := (Result error: #x) map: [:v | v + 1]
    self assert: r isError
    self assert: r error equals: #x

  testMapChain =>
    r := (Result ok: 10) map: [:v | v * 2]
    r2 := r map: [:v | v + 5]
    self assert: r2 value equals: 25

  // =========================================================================
  // TRANSFORMERS — andThen:
  // =========================================================================
  testAndThenOnOkChainsBlock =>
    r := (Result ok: 42) andThen: [:v | Result ok: v * 2]
    self assert: r ok
    self assert: r value equals: 84

  testAndThenOnErrorPropagatesError =>
    r := (Result error: #x) andThen: [:v | Result ok: v * 2]
    self assert: r isError
    self assert: r error equals: #x

  testAndThenFlattensProperly =>
    // The block returns a Result — andThen: does not double-wrap
    r := (Result ok: 5) andThen: [:v | Result ok: v + 1]
    self assert: r value equals: 6

  testAndThenPropagatesInnerError =>
    r := (Result ok: 42) andThen: [:v | Result error: #inner_error]
    self assert: r isError
    self assert: r error equals: #inner_error

  // =========================================================================
  // TRANSFORMERS — mapError:
  // =========================================================================
  testMapErrorOnErrorTransformsReason =>
    r := (Result error: #old) mapError: [:e | #new]
    self assert: r isError
    self assert: r error equals: #new

  testMapErrorOnOkLeavesOkUnchanged =>
    r := (Result ok: 42) mapError: [:e | #transformed]
    self assert: r ok
    self assert: r value equals: 42

  // =========================================================================
  // TERMINAL HANDLER — ifError:
  // =========================================================================
  testIfErrorOnOkReturnsSelf =>
    r := Result ok: 42
    result := r ifError: [:e | 0]
    self assert: result equals: r
    // Verify we can still extract the value by creating a known Result and comparing
    self assert: result equals: (Result ok: 42)

  testIfErrorOnErrorEvaluatesBlock =>
    result := (Result error: #oops) ifError: [:e | -1]
    self assert: result equals: -1

  testIfErrorPassesReasonToBlock =>
    result := (Result error: #my_error) ifError: [:e | e printString]
    self assert: result equals: "#my_error"

  testIfErrorComposesWithAndThen =>
    // Error in an andThen: chain short-circuits to ifError:
    chain := (Result ok: 1) andThen: [:v | Result error: #chain_fail]
    result := chain ifError: [:_ | "caught"]
    self assert: result equals: "caught"

  testIfErrorOkChainPassesThrough =>
    // All steps succeed — ifError: block is never called
    chain := (Result ok: 10) andThen: [:v | Result ok: v * 2]
    result := chain ifError: [:_ | -1]
    self assert: result equals: (Result ok: 20)

  // =========================================================================
  // PATTERN MATCHING — ifOk:ifError:
  // =========================================================================
  testIfOkIfErrorWithOkEvaluatesOkBlock =>
    result := (Result ok: 42) ifOk: [:v | v + 1] ifError: [:e | -1]
    self assert: result equals: 43

  testIfOkIfErrorWithErrorEvaluatesErrorBlock =>
    result := (Result error: #x) ifOk: [:v | v + 1] ifError: [:e | -1]
    self assert: result equals: -1

  testIfOkIfErrorPassesValueToOkBlock =>
    result := (Result ok: "hello") ifOk: [:v | v ++ "!"] ifError: [:e | ""]
    self assert: result equals: "hello!"

  testIfOkIfErrorPassesReasonToErrorBlock =>
    result := (Result error: #my_err) ifOk: [:v |
      "ok"
    ] ifError: [:e | e printString]
    self assert: result equals: "#my_err"

  // =========================================================================
  // tryDo:
  // =========================================================================
  testTryDoWithSuccessfulBlock =>
    r := Result tryDo: [42]
    self assert: r ok
    self assert: r value equals: 42

  testTryDoWithExceptionReturnsError =>
    r := Result tryDo: [Exception signal: "boom"]
    self assert: r isError

  testTryDoErrorReasonIsException =>
    // isKindOf: does not work for actor-based class instances (Exception);
    // verify the error reason is non-nil (not nil) instead.
    r := Result tryDo: [Exception signal: "boom"]
    self deny: r error isNil

  testTryDoCanUnwrapException =>
    r := Result tryDo: [Exception signal: "boom"]
    self should: [r unwrap] raise: #signal

  // =========================================================================
  // printString
  // =========================================================================
  testPrintStringOk =>
    self assert: (Result ok: 42) printString equals: "Result ok: 42"

  testPrintStringError =>
    self assert: (Result error: #not_found) printString equals: "Result error: #not_found"

  testPrintStringOkString =>
    // "hello" printString produces "hello" (with embedded quotes)
    // In Beamtalk triple-quoted form, that becomes: """hello"""
    // So (Result ok: "hello") printString = "Result ok: " ++ """hello"""
    expected := "Result ok: " ++ """hello"""
    self assert: (Result ok: "hello") printString equals: expected
entries.explanation: BUnit tests for the Result class (BT-1254, ADR 0060).

entries.title: RuntimeDocTest
entries.source:
// BUnit tests for runtime-embedded documentation (ADR 0033, BT-769)

TestCase subclass: RuntimeDocTest

  // Reset global doc state before each test to avoid ordering dependencies.
  // Class-level and method-level docs are global mutations on the class object,
  // so earlier tests (e.g. testSetMethodDoc) would pollute later tests.
  setUp =>
    DocTestHelper doc: nil
    DocTestHelper setDocForMethod: #greet to: nil
    DocTestHelper setDocForMethod: #answer to: nil

  // --- Class doc ---
  testClassDocDefaultsToNil => self assert: DocTestHelper doc equals: nil

  testSetClassDoc =>
    DocTestHelper doc: "A helper for testing"
    self assert: DocTestHelper doc equals: "A helper for testing"

  testSetClassDocOverwrites =>
    DocTestHelper doc: "First doc"
    DocTestHelper doc: "Second doc"
    self assert: DocTestHelper doc equals: "Second doc"

  // --- Method doc via >> and doc message ---
  testMethodDocDefaultsToNil =>
    m := DocTestHelper >> #greet
    self assert: m doc equals: nil

  testSetMethodDoc =>
    DocTestHelper setDocForMethod: #greet to: "Say hello"
    m := DocTestHelper >> #greet
    self assert: m doc equals: "Say hello"

  testSetMethodDocDifferentMethods =>
    DocTestHelper setDocForMethod: #answer to: "Return 42"
    m := DocTestHelper >> #answer
    self assert: m doc equals: "Return 42"

  // --- CompiledMethod doc accessor ---
  testCompiledMethodDocField =>
    DocTestHelper setDocForMethod: #greet to: "Greeting method"
    m := DocTestHelper >> #greet
    self assert: m doc equals: "Greeting method"
    self assert: m selector equals: #greet

  // --- doc: returns self (chainable) ---
  testDocSetterReturnsSelf =>
    result := DocTestHelper doc: "Chainable"
    self assert: result equals: DocTestHelper

  // --- docForMethod: ---
  testDocForMethodReturnsDoc =>
    DocTestHelper setDocForMethod: #greet to: "Say hello"
    self assert: (DocTestHelper docForMethod: #greet) equals: "Say hello"

  testDocForMethodNilWhenNoDoc =>
    self assert: (DocTestHelper docForMethod: #add:to:) equals: nil

  testDocForMethodNilForNonExistent =>
    self assert: (DocTestHelper docForMethod: #nonExistent) equals: nil
entries.explanation: BUnit tests for runtime-embedded documentation (ADR 0033, BT-769)

entries.title: ServerHandleInfoTest
entries.source:
// BT-1457: Tests for Server handleInfo: codegen dispatch (ADR 0065)
//
// Fixtures: stdlib/test/fixtures/server_handle_info.bt
//           stdlib/test/fixtures/failing_handle_info_server.bt
//           stdlib/test/fixtures/plain_actor_for_info_test.bt

TestCase subclass: ServerHandleInfoTest

  // === Basic handleInfo: dispatch ===
  testServerReceivesTickMessage =>
    server := HandleInfoCounter spawn
    Erlang erlang send: server pid msg: #tick
    Timer sleep: 50
    self assert: server getCount equals: 1
    server stop

  testServerReceivesMultipleTicks =>
    server := HandleInfoCounter spawn
    Erlang erlang send: server pid msg: #tick
    Erlang erlang send: server pid msg: #tick
    Erlang erlang send: server pid msg: #tick
    Timer sleep: 50
    self assert: server getCount equals: 3
    server stop

  testServerHandlesNonTickMessage =>
    server := HandleInfoCounter spawn
    Erlang erlang send: server pid msg: #something_else
    Timer sleep: 50
    self assert: server getLastMsg equals: #something_else
    server stop

  // === Plain Actor ignores raw messages ===
  testPlainActorIgnoresRawMessages =>
    actor := PlainActorForInfoTest spawn
    actor increment
    self assert: actor getCount equals: 1
    Erlang erlang send: actor pid msg: #tick
    Timer sleep: 50
    self assert: actor getCount equals: 1
    actor stop

  testServerIsAliveAfterUnknownMessage =>
    server := HandleInfoCounter spawn
    self assert: server isAlive
    Erlang erlang send: server pid msg: #anything
    Timer sleep: 50
    self assert: server isAlive
    server stop

  // === Error recovery (log-and-continue) ===
  testHandleInfoErrorDoesNotCrashServer =>
    server := FailingHandleInfoServer spawn
    Erlang erlang send: server pid msg: #crash
    Timer sleep: 100
    self assert: server isAlive
    server stop

  testHandleInfoErrorPreservesPreCallState =>
    server := FailingHandleInfoServer spawn
    Erlang erlang send: server pid msg: #increment
    Timer sleep: 50
    self assert: server getCount equals: 1
    Erlang erlang send: server pid msg: #crash
    Timer sleep: 100
    self assert: server isAlive
    self assert: server getCount equals: 1
    server stop
entries.explanation: BT-1457: Tests for Server handleInfo: codegen dispatch (ADR 0065)  Fixtures: stdlib/test/fixtures/server_handle_info.bt           stdlib/test/fixtures/failing_handle_info_server.bt           stdlib/test/fixtures/plain_actor_for_info_test.bt

entries.title: SetTest
entries.source:
// E2E tests for Set (unique element collections using ordsets) (BT-73)

TestCase subclass: SetTest

  testCreationClass =>
    Set new
    self assert: (Set new) class equals: Set

  testIsemptySize =>
    self assert: (Set new) isEmpty
    self assert: (Set new) size equals: 0

  testAddIncludes =>
    s := (Set new) add: 1
    self assert: (s includes: 1)
    self deny: (s includes: 2)
    self assert: s size equals: 1

  testAddDeduplication =>
    s := (Set new) add: 1
    s2 := (s add: 2) add: 1
    self assert: s2 size equals: 2
    self assert: (s2 includes: 1)
    self assert: (s2 includes: 2)

  testRemove =>
    s2 := ((Set new) add: 1) add: 2
    s3 := s2 remove: 1
    self deny: (s3 includes: 1)
    self assert: (s3 includes: 2)
    self assert: s3 size equals: 1

  testFromList =>
    colors := Set new fromList: #(#red, #green, #blue)
    self assert: colors size equals: 3
    self assert: (colors includes: #red)
    self deny: (colors includes: #yellow)

  testUnion =>
    a := Set new fromList: #(1, 2, 3)
    b := Set new fromList: #(3, 4, 5)
    u := a union: b
    self assert: u size equals: 5
    self assert: (u includes: 1)
    self assert: (u includes: 5)

  testIntersection =>
    a := Set new fromList: #(1, 2, 3)
    b := Set new fromList: #(3, 4, 5)
    i := a intersection: b
    self assert: i size equals: 1
    self assert: (i includes: 3)
    self deny: (i includes: 1)

  testDifference =>
    a := Set new fromList: #(1, 2, 3)
    b := Set new fromList: #(3, 4, 5)
    d := a difference: b
    self assert: d size equals: 2
    self assert: (d includes: 1)
    self deny: (d includes: 3)

  testIssubsetof =>
    small := Set new fromList: #(1, 2)
    big := Set new fromList: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: (small isSubsetOf: big)
    self deny: (big isSubsetOf: small)

  testAslist =>
    self assert: ((Set new) fromList: #(3, 1, 2)) asList equals: #(1, 2, 3)

  testIteration =>
    self assert: (((Set new) fromList: #(1, 2, 3)) do: [:x | nil]) equals: nil
    // Block receives each element — inject:into: verifies correct values are passed
    self assert: (((Set new) fromList: #(1, 2, 3)) inject: 0 into: [:sum :x |
      sum + x
    ]) equals: 6

  testRespondstoMethodReflection =>
    s4 := (Set new) add: 1
    self assert: (s4 respondsTo: #size)
    self assert: (s4 respondsTo: #includes:)
    self deny: (s4 respondsTo: #nonExistent)

  testPrintstring =>
    self assert: (((Set new) fromList: #(1, 2, 3)) printString) notNil
    self assert: ((Set new) printString) notNil
entries.explanation: E2E tests for Set (unique element collections using ordsets) (BT-73)

The BUnit runner must NOT use that value as self — doing so would cause every test to fail with "False does not understand 'assert:'".

entries.title: SpeciesTest
entries.source:
// BUnit tests for the species pattern on Collection (BT-822)
//
// Verifies that collect:, select:, and reject: return the same
// collection type as the receiver (not always a List)

TestCase subclass: SpeciesTest

  // ===========================================================================
  // species method
  // ===========================================================================
  testListSpecies => self assert: #(1, 2, 3) species equals: List

  testArraySpecies => self assert: #[1, 2, 3] species equals: Array

  // ===========================================================================
  // List species — collect:/select:/reject: return List
  // ===========================================================================
  testListCollectReturnsListType =>
    result := #(1, 2, 3) collect: [:x | x * 2]
    self assert: result class equals: List
    self assert: result equals: #(2, 4, 6)

  testListSelectReturnsListType =>
    result := #(1, 2, 3, 4) select: [:x | x > 2]
    self assert: result class equals: List
    self assert: result equals: #(3, 4)

  testListRejectReturnsListType =>
    result := #(1, 2, 3, 4) reject: [:x | x > 2]
    self assert: result class equals: List
    self assert: result equals: #(1, 2)

  // ===========================================================================
  // Array species — collect:/select:/reject: return Array
  // ===========================================================================
  testArrayCollectReturnsArrayType =>
    result := #[1, 2, 3] collect: [:x | x * 2]
    self assert: result class equals: Array
    self assert: result equals: #[2, 4, 6]

  testArraySelectReturnsArrayType =>
    result := #[1, 2, 3, 4] select: [:x | x > 2]
    self assert: result class equals: Array
    self assert: result equals: #[3, 4]

  testArrayRejectReturnsArrayType =>
    result := #[1, 2, 3, 4] reject: [:x | x > 2]
    self assert: result class equals: Array
    self assert: result equals: #[1, 2]

  // ===========================================================================
  // Set species — collect:/select: return Set
  // ===========================================================================
  testSetCollectReturnsSetType =>
    s := Set new fromList: #(1, 2, 3)
    result := s collect: [:x | x * 2]
    self assert: result class equals: Set

  testSetSelectReturnsSetType =>
    s := Set new fromList: #(1, 2, 3, 4)
    result := s select: [:x | x > 2]
    self assert: result class equals: Set
    self assert: (result includes: 3)
    self assert: (result includes: 4)
    self deny: (result includes: 1)

  // ===========================================================================
  // withAll: factories
  // ===========================================================================
  testListWithAll =>
    result := List withAll: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: result class equals: List
    self assert: result equals: #(1, 2, 3)

  testArrayWithAll =>
    result := Array withAll: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: result class equals: Array
    self assert: (result at: 1) equals: 1
    self assert: (result at: 3) equals: 3

  testSetWithAll =>
    result := Set withAll: #(1, 2, 2, 3)
    self assert: result class equals: Set
    self assert: result size equals: 3
    self assert: (result includes: 1)
    self assert: (result includes: 3)

  testTupleWithAll =>
    result := Tuple withAll: #(1, 2, 3)
    self assert: result class equals: Tuple
    self assert: result size equals: 3

  testStringWithAll =>
    result := String withAll: #("h", "e", "l", "l", "o")
    self assert: result class equals: String
    self assert: result equals: "hello"

  // ===========================================================================
  // Dictionary collect: maps values preserving keys
  // ===========================================================================
  testDictionaryCollectPreservesKeys =>
    d := #{#a => 1, #b => 2, #c => 3}
    result := d collect: [:v | v * 10]
    self assert: result class equals: Dictionary
    self assert: (result at: #a) equals: 10
    self assert: (result at: #b) equals: 20
    self assert: (result at: #c) equals: 30

  testDictionaryCollectReturnsDictionary =>
    d := #{#x => 5}
    result := d collect: [:v | v + 1]
    self assert: result class equals: Dictionary
    self assert: (result at: #x) equals: 6
entries.explanation: BUnit tests for the species pattern on Collection (BT-822)  Verifies that collect:, select:, and reject: return the same collection type as the receiver (not always a List)

entries.explanation: Tests the Stream class — from:, from:by:, on: constructors; lazy pipeline ops (select:, collect:, reject:, drop:); terminals (take:, asList, do:, inject:into:, detect:, anySatisfy:, allSatisfy:); and empty-stream behaviour

entries.title: StrictEqualityTest
entries.explanation: BT-1562: Strict equality (=:=) and strict inequality (=/=). Erlang's =:= is exact equality — 5 =:= 5.0 is false because they are different types. =/= is the complement: (x =/= y) iff not (x =:= y). Loose equality (==) coerces, so 5 == 5.0 is true.

entries.title: StringAdvancedMethodsTest
entries.source:
// Advanced string operations tests - migrated from bootstrap/string_methods.bt (BT-827)

TestCase subclass: StringAdvancedMethodsTest

  testUppercase => self assert: "hello" uppercase equals: "HELLO"

  testLowercase => self assert: "HELLO" lowercase equals: "hello"

  testTrim =>
    // trim both sides
    self assert: "  hello  " trim equals: "hello"
    // trim with no whitespace (no-op)
    self assert: "hello" trim equals: "hello"

  testTrimLeft => self assert: "  hello" trimLeft equals: "hello"

  testTrimRight => self assert: "hello  " trimRight equals: "hello"

  testStringEquality =>
    self assert: ("hello" =:= "hello")
    self deny: ("hello" =:= "world")

  testStringInequality => self assert: ("hello" /= "world")

  testStringLessThan =>
    // String less than (lexicographic)
    self assert: ("abc" < "def")

  testStringGreaterThan =>
    // String greater than
    self assert: ("def" > "abc")

  testStringLessOrEqual =>
    // String less than or equal
    self assert: ("abc" <= "abc")
    self assert: ("abc" <= "def")

  testStringGreaterOrEqual =>
    // String greater than or equal
    self assert: ("def" >= "abc")
    self assert: ("abc" >= "abc")

  testSplit => self assert: ("a,b,c" split: ",") equals: #("a", "b", "c")

  testAsInteger => self assert: "42" asInteger equals: 42

  testAsFloat => self assert: "3.14" asFloat equals: 3.14
entries.explanation: Advanced string operations tests - migrated from bootstrap/string_methods.bt (BT-827)

entries.title: StringAsAtomTest
entries.source:
// String#asAtom tests (BT-1585)
// Verifies that asAtom reliably converts strings to atoms,
// including strings with underscores, digits, and interpolation.

TestCase subclass: StringAsAtomTest

  testSimpleString => self assert: "hello" asAtom equals: #hello

  testShortString => self assert: "abc" asAtom equals: #abc

  testStringWithUnderscore =>
    self assert: "hello_world" asAtom equals: #hello_world

  testStringWithDigits => self assert: "foo_42" asAtom equals: #foo_42

  testStringWithTrailingDigits => self assert: "item99" asAtom equals: #item99

  testStringWithMultipleUnderscores =>
    self assert: "a_b_c" asAtom equals: #a_b_c

  testInterpolatedString =>
    id := 42
    name := "tq_{id}" asAtom
    self assert: name equals: #tq_42

  testAsAtomClassIsSymbol => self assert: "test" asAtom class equals: Symbol
entries.explanation: String#asAtom tests (BT-1585) Verifies that asAtom reliably converts strings to atoms, including strings with underscores, digits, and interpolation.

entries.title: StringBinaryInheritanceTest
entries.explanation: String/Binary inheritance tests — verify byte-level methods inherited from Binary work correctly on String instances (ADR 0069 Phase 3b, BT-1595)

entries.title: StringCodePointTest
entries.source:
// Stdlib tests for String fromCodePoint: and fromCodePoints: (BT-1245)

TestCase subclass: StringCodePointTest

  testFromCodePointAscii =>
    // ASCII uppercase letter
    self assert: (String fromCodePoint: 65) equals: "A"
    // ASCII lowercase letter
    self assert: (String fromCodePoint: 122) equals: "z"
    // ASCII digit
    self assert: (String fromCodePoint: 48) equals: "0"

  testFromCodePointMultibyte =>
    // Euro sign (U+20AC)
    self assert: (String fromCodePoint: 8364) equals: "€"
    // Pound sign (U+00A3)
    self assert: (String fromCodePoint: 163) equals: "£"

  testFromCodePointResultIsString =>
    result := String fromCodePoint: 65
    self assert: result class equals: String

  testFromCodePointResultLength =>
    // Each call produces a single-grapheme string
    self assert: (String fromCodePoint: 65) length equals: 1
    self assert: (String fromCodePoint: 8364) length equals: 1

  testFromCodePointsBasic =>
    // Two ASCII code points
    self assert: (String fromCodePoints: #(72, 105)) equals: "Hi"
    // Opening and closing braces
    self assert: (String fromCodePoints: #(123, 125)) equals: "\{}"

  testFromCodePointsEmpty =>
    self assert: (String fromCodePoints: #()) equals: ""

  testFromCodePointsMultibyte =>
    // Euro and pound signs
    self assert: (String fromCodePoints: #(8364, 163)) equals: "€£"

  testFromCodePointsResultIsString =>
    result := String fromCodePoints: #(65, 66, 67)
    self assert: result class equals: String
    self assert: result equals: "ABC"
entries.explanation: Stdlib tests for String fromCodePoint: and fromCodePoints: (BT-1245)

entries.title: StringFromIolistTest
entries.source:
// Stdlib tests for String fromIolist: (BT-1244)

TestCase subclass: StringFromIolistTest

  testFromIolistWithBinaryPassthrough =>
    // A binary is already a String — returned as-is
    self assert: (String fromIolist: "hello") equals: "hello"

  testFromIolistWithCharlist =>
    // Charlist (list of code points) is coerced to String
    self assert: (String fromIolist: #(104, 105)) equals: "hi"

  testFromIolistWithNonAsciiUtf8Bytes =>
    // UTF-8 byte iolist [195, 169] must produce "é" (U+00E9), not corrupt bytes
    expected := String fromCodePoint: 233
    self assert: (String fromIolist: #(195, 169)) equals: expected

  testFromIolistWithNonAsciiCodepointCharlist =>
    // Codepoint charlist [233] must also produce "é" (U+00E9)
    expected := String fromCodePoint: 233
    self assert: (String fromIolist: #(233)) equals: expected

  testFromIolistWithNonIolistRaisesTypeError =>
    self should: [String fromIolist: 42] raise: #type_error
entries.explanation: Stdlib tests for String fromIolist: (BT-1244)

entries.title: StringInterpolationTest
entries.explanation: Stdlib tests for string interpolation (ADR 0023 Phase 3, BT-558)

entries.title: StringNewMethodsTest
entries.explanation: E2E tests for new String methods (BT-43)

entries.title: StringOperationsTest
entries.source:
// String operations tests - migrated from bootstrap/string_ops.bt (BT-827)

TestCase subclass: StringOperationsTest

  testAt =>
    // 1-based grapheme access
    self assert: ("hello" at: 1) equals: "h"
    self assert: ("hello" at: 5) equals: "o"

  testCapitalize =>
    self assert: "hello" capitalize equals: "Hello"
    self assert: "HELLO" capitalize equals: "HELLO"

  testReverse => self assert: "hello" reverse equals: "olleh"

  testIncludesSubstring =>
    self assert: ("hello world" includesSubstring: "world")
    self deny: ("hello" includesSubstring: "xyz")

  testStartsWith =>
    self assert: ("hello" startsWith: "hel")
    self deny: ("hello" startsWith: "xyz")

  testEndsWith =>
    self assert: ("hello" endsWith: "llo")
    self deny: ("hello" endsWith: "xyz")

  testIndexOf =>
    self assert: ("hello" indexOf: "ell") equals: 2
    self assert: ("hello" indexOf: "xyz") equals: nil

  testSplitOn => self assert: ("a,b,c" splitOn: ",") equals: #("a", "b", "c")

  testRepeat => self assert: ("ab" repeat: 3) equals: "ababab"

  testAsList => self assert: "hi" asList equals: #("h", "i")

  testDo =>
    // do: returns nil
    self assert: ("hi" do: [:ch | nil]) equals: nil

  testCollect =>
    // collect: - map over graphemes (returns string)
    self assert: ("hi" collect: [:ch | ch uppercase]) equals: "HI"
    self assert: ("abc" collect: [:ch | ch uppercase]) equals: "ABC"
    // on string variables (not just literals)
    s := "hello"
    self assert: (s collect: [:ch | ch uppercase]) equals: "HELLO"
    // identity collect returns same string
    self assert: ("abc" collect: [:ch | ch]) equals: "abc"

  testSelect =>
    // select: - filter graphemes (returns string)
    self assert: ("hello" select: [:ch | ch /= "l"]) equals: "heo"
    self assert: ("abcd" select: [:ch | ch < "c"]) equals: "ab"
    // on string variables
    s := "hello"
    self assert: (s select: [:ch | ch > "h"]) equals: "llo"

  testReject =>
    // reject: - remove graphemes (returns string)
    self assert: ("hello" reject: [:ch | ch =:= "l"]) equals: "heo"
    self assert: ("a1b2c3" reject: [:ch | ch isDigit]) equals: "abc"
    // on string variables
    s := "hello"
    self assert: (s reject: [:ch | ch < "l"]) equals: "llo"
    // empty result
    self assert: ("aaa" reject: [:ch | ch =:= "a"]) equals: ""
entries.explanation: String operations tests - migrated from bootstrap/string_ops.bt (BT-827)

entries.title: StringQuoteEscapeTest
entries.explanation: BT-743: Tests that string literals correctly parse doubled double-quote characters as a single embedded quote — single, middle, multiple, and adjacent embedded quotes

entries.title: SubprocessTest
entries.source:
// BT-1132: BUnit tests for Subprocess actor — lifecycle, readLine/writeLine,
// EOF, timeout, stderr, exitCode, close, stop, lines Stream, stderrLines Stream.
//
// Cross-platform: uses platform-appropriate commands on Unix and Windows.
// Interactive stdin-echo tests use beamtalk-exec --cat on Windows for line-buffered output.

TestCase subclass: SubprocessTest
  field: isUnix = false

  setUp => self withIsUnix: (System osFamily == "unix")

  // --- Platform command helpers ---
  echoExe => self.isUnix ifTrue: ["/bin/echo"] ifFalse: ["cmd"]

  echoArgs: text =>
    self.isUnix ifTrue: [#(text)] ifFalse: [#("/c", "echo " ++ text)]

  shellExe => self.isUnix ifTrue: ["/bin/sh"] ifFalse: ["cmd"]

  shellArgs: cmd => self.isUnix ifTrue: [#("-c", cmd)] ifFalse: [#("/c", cmd)]

  sleepExe => self.isUnix ifTrue: ["/bin/sleep"] ifFalse: ["cmd"]

  sleepArgs =>
    self.isUnix ifTrue: [
      #("60")
    ] ifFalse: [#("/c", "ping -n 60 127.0.0.1 >nul")]

  trueExe => self.isUnix ifTrue: ["/usr/bin/env"] ifFalse: ["cmd"]

  trueArgs => self.isUnix ifTrue: [#("true")] ifFalse: [#("/c", "exit 0")]

  stderrScript => self.isUnix ifTrue: ["echo err >&2"] ifFalse: ["echo err>&2"]

  stdoutStderrScript =>
    self.isUnix ifTrue: [
      "echo out; echo err >&2"
    ] ifFalse: ["echo out& echo err>&2"]

  multilineScript =>
    self.isUnix ifTrue: [
      "printf 'alpha\nbeta\ngamma\n'"
    ] ifFalse: ["(echo alpha& echo beta& echo gamma)"]

  stderrMultilineScript =>
    self.isUnix ifTrue: [
      "echo x >&2; echo y >&2; echo z >&2"
    ] ifFalse: ["(echo x& echo y& echo z)>&2"]

  partialLineScript =>
    self.isUnix ifTrue: ["printf hello"] ifFalse: ["<nul set /p =hello"]

  pwdScript => self.isUnix ifTrue: ["pwd"] ifFalse: ["cd"]

  envAndPwdScript =>
    self.isUnix ifTrue: [
      "echo $BT_TEST_SEED; pwd"
    ] ifFalse: ["echo %BT_TEST_SEED%& cd"]

  envDir => self.isUnix ifTrue: ["/tmp"] ifFalse: [System getEnv: "TEMP"]

  // cat executable: echoes stdin to stdout.
  // On Unix uses /bin/cat; on Windows uses beamtalk-exec --cat (built-in
  // cat mode with explicit flushing, bypassing pipe buffering).
  catExe => self.isUnix ifTrue: ["/bin/cat"] ifFalse: ["beamtalk-exec"]

  catArgs => self.isUnix ifTrue: [#()] ifFalse: [#("--cat")]

  // --- Tests ---
  testSpawnAndReadLine =>
    // echo outputs one line then exits
    agent := (Subprocess open: self echoExe args: (self echoArgs: "hello")) unwrap
    self assert: agent isAlive
    self assert: agent readLine equals: "hello"
    // After process exits and buffer is drained, readLine returns nil (EOF)
    self assert: agent readLine equals: nil
    self assert: agent stop equals: #ok

  testWriteLineAndReadLine =>
    // cat echoes stdin lines back to stdout
    agent := (Subprocess open: self catExe args: self catArgs) unwrap
    agent writeLine: "hello world"
    self assert: agent readLine equals: "hello world"
    self assert: agent stop equals: #ok

  testReadLineEofReturnsNil =>
    // echo produces one line; second readLine returns nil (EOF)
    agent := (Subprocess open: self echoExe args: (self echoArgs: "goodbye")) unwrap
    self assert: agent readLine equals: "goodbye"
    self assert: agent readLine equals: nil
    self assert: agent stop equals: #ok

  testReadLineWithTimeoutReturnsNil =>
    // sleep produces no stdout; readLine: returns nil after timeout
    agent := (Subprocess open: self sleepExe args: self sleepArgs) unwrap
    self assert: (agent readLine: 100) equals: nil
    self assert: agent stop equals: #ok

  testSeparateStderrChannel =>
    // shell writes only to stderr; stdout readLine returns nil, stderr returns the line
    agent := (Subprocess open: self shellExe args: (self shellArgs: self stderrScript)) unwrap
    self assert: agent readLine equals: nil
    self assert: agent readStderrLine equals: "err"
    self assert: agent readStderrLine equals: nil
    self assert: agent stop equals: #ok

  testStdoutAndStderrAreIndependent =>
    // shell writes to both channels independently
    agent := (Subprocess open: self shellExe args: (self shellArgs: self stdoutStderrScript)) unwrap
    self assert: agent readLine equals: "out"
    self assert: agent readStderrLine equals: "err"
    self assert: agent stop equals: #ok

  testExitCodeNilWhileRunning =>
    // sleep stays running; exitCode is nil until exit
    agent := (Subprocess open: self sleepExe args: self sleepArgs) unwrap
    self assert: agent exitCode equals: nil
    self assert: agent stop equals: #ok

  testExitCodeAfterExit =>
    // echo exits with code 0; after draining output exitCode is 0
    agent := (Subprocess open: self echoExe args: (self echoArgs: "done")) unwrap
    self assert: agent readLine equals: "done"
    // Second readLine blocks until subprocess exits; when nil is returned
    // the exit event has been processed and exitCode is set
    self assert: agent readLine equals: nil
    self assert: agent exitCode equals: 0
    self assert: agent stop equals: #ok

  testCloseRaisesPortClosedOnWriteLine =>
    // After close, writeLine: raises port_closed
    agent := (Subprocess open: self sleepExe args: self sleepArgs) unwrap
    self assert: agent close equals: nil
    self should: [agent writeLine: "after close"] raise: #port_closed
    self assert: agent stop equals: #ok

  testCloseIdempotent =>
    // close can be called multiple times safely
    agent := (Subprocess open: self sleepExe args: self sleepArgs) unwrap
    self assert: agent close equals: nil
    self assert: agent close equals: nil
    self assert: agent stop equals: #ok

  testStopLifecycle =>
    // Standard Actor lifecycle: isAlive, stop, deny isAlive
    agent := (Subprocess open: self sleepExe args: self sleepArgs) unwrap
    self assert: agent isAlive
    self assert: agent stop equals: #ok
    self deny: agent isAlive

  testLinesStream =>
    // printf writes three lines to stdout; lines asList materializes them
    agent := (Subprocess open: self shellExe args: (self shellArgs: self multilineScript)) unwrap
    self assert: agent lines asList equals: #("alpha", "beta", "gamma")
    self assert: agent stop equals: #ok

  testStderrLinesStream =>
    // echo writes three lines to stderr; stderrLines asList materializes them
    agent := (Subprocess open: self shellExe args: (self shellArgs: self stderrMultilineScript)) unwrap
    self assert: agent stderrLines asList equals: #("x", "y", "z")
    self assert: agent stop equals: #ok

  testReadStderrLineTimeout =>
    // sleep produces no stderr; readStderrLine: returns nil after timeout
    agent := (Subprocess open: self sleepExe args: self sleepArgs) unwrap
    self assert: (agent readStderrLine: 100) equals: nil
    self assert: agent stop equals: #ok

  testOpenWithDir =>
    // BT-1411: open:args:dir: sets the working directory
    agent := (Subprocess
      open: self shellExe
      args: (self shellArgs: self pwdScript)
      dir: self envDir) unwrap
    line := agent readLine
    self assert: (line includesSubstring: self envDir)
    self assert: agent stop equals: #ok

  testOpenWithEnvAndDir =>
    // BT-1411: open:args:env:dir: sets both env and working directory
    agent := (Subprocess
      open: self shellExe
      args: (self shellArgs: self envAndPwdScript)
      env: #{"BT_TEST_SEED" => "hello1411"}
      dir: self envDir) unwrap
    envLine := agent readLine
    self assert: envLine equals: "hello1411"
    dirLine := agent readLine
    self assert: (dirLine includesSubstring: self envDir)
    self assert: agent stop equals: #ok
entries.explanation: BT-1132: BUnit tests for Subprocess actor — lifecycle, readLine/writeLine, EOF, timeout, stderr, exitCode, close, stop, lines Stream, stderrLines Stream.  Cross-platform: uses platform-appropriate commands on Unix and Windows. Interactive stdin-echo tests use beamtalk-exec --cat on Windows for line-buffered output.

entries.title: SupervisionSpecTest
entries.explanation: BT-1218: Tests for supervisionSpec synthesis and supervisionPolicy override on Actor subclasses (ADR 0059 Phase 1).  Fixtures: stdlib/test/fixtures/spec_test_actor_default.bt           stdlib/test/fixtures/spec_test_actor_permanent.bt           stdlib/test/fixtures/spec_test_actor_transient.bt

entries.title: SupervisionSpecValueTest
entries.source:
// BT-1218: Tests for SupervisionSpec value-type basics and childSpec construction.
// Restored from BT-1217 after splitting supervision tests by concern (single-class-per-file rule).
//
// Fixtures: stdlib/test/fixtures/counter.bt (Counter — Actor subclass)

TestCase subclass: SupervisionSpecValueTest

  // === SupervisionSpec value type basics ===
  testDefaultSlotValues =>
    spec := SupervisionSpec new
    self assert: spec id equals: nil
    self assert: spec actorClass equals: nil
    self assert: spec restart equals: #temporary
    self assert: spec args equals: nil
    self assert: spec shutdown equals: nil

  testKeywordConstructor =>
    spec := SupervisionSpec
      id: #db
      actorClass: Counter
      restart: #permanent
      args: nil
      shutdown: nil
    self assert: spec id equals: #db
    self assert: spec actorClass equals: Counter
    self assert: spec restart equals: #permanent
    self assert: spec args equals: nil
    self assert: spec shutdown equals: nil

  testWithSetterReturnsFreshCopy =>
    spec := SupervisionSpec new
    spec2 := spec withRestart: #permanent
    // Original unchanged
    self assert: spec restart equals: #temporary
    // New instance updated
    self assert: spec2 restart equals: #permanent

  testChainedWithSetters =>
    spec := (SupervisionSpec new withActorClass: Counter) withRestart: #transient
    self assert: spec actorClass equals: Counter
    self assert: spec restart equals: #transient

  // === Multi-keyword fluent overrides ===
  testWithIdWithRestart =>
    spec := SupervisionSpec new withId: #primary withRestart: #permanent
    self assert: spec id equals: #primary
    self assert: spec restart equals: #permanent

  testWithIdWithArgs =>
    args := #{#role => #primary}
    spec := SupervisionSpec new withId: #db withArgs: args
    self assert: spec id equals: #db
    self assert: spec args equals: args

  testWithIdWithRestartWithArgs =>
    args := #{#role => #replica}
    spec := SupervisionSpec new
      withId: #replica
      withRestart: #transient
      withArgs: args
    self assert: spec id equals: #replica
    self assert: spec restart equals: #transient
    self assert: spec args equals: args

  // === childSpec construction ===
  testChildSpecDefaultId =>
    spec := SupervisionSpec new withActorClass: Counter
    child := spec childSpec
    // Default id is the class name as a symbol
    self assert: (child at: #id) equals: #Counter

  testChildSpecCustomId =>
    spec := (SupervisionSpec new withActorClass: Counter) withId: #myCounter
    child := spec childSpec
    self assert: (child at: #id) equals: #myCounter

  testChildSpecSpawnWithoutArgs =>
    spec := SupervisionSpec new withActorClass: Counter
    child := spec childSpec
    start := child at: #start
    // start is #[ActorClass, #spawn, #()]
    self assert: (start at: 2) equals: #spawn

  testChildSpecSpawnWithArgs =>
    args := #{#value => 10}
    spec := (SupervisionSpec new withActorClass: Counter) withArgs: args
    child := spec childSpec
    start := child at: #start
    self assert: (start at: 2) equals: #spawnWith:

  testChildSpecWorkerType =>
    spec := SupervisionSpec new withActorClass: Counter
    child := spec childSpec
    self assert: (child at: #type) equals: #worker
    self assert: (child at: #shutdown) equals: 5000

  testChildSpecSupervisorType =>
    spec := SupervisionSpec new withActorClass: Supervisor
    child := spec childSpec
    self assert: (child at: #type) equals: #supervisor
    self assert: (child at: #shutdown) equals: #infinity

  testChildSpecRestartPolicy =>
    spec := (SupervisionSpec new withActorClass: Counter) withRestart: #permanent
    child := spec childSpec
    self assert: (child at: #restart) equals: #permanent

  testChildSpecNilActorClassRaisesError =>
    spec := SupervisionSpec new
    self should: [spec childSpec] raise: #user_error

  // === Actor supervision defaults ===
  testActorSupervisionPolicyDefault =>
    self assert: Counter supervisionPolicy equals: #temporary

  testActorIsSupervisorDefault =>
    self assert: Counter isSupervisor equals: false

  testSupervisorIsSupervisor =>
    self assert: Supervisor isSupervisor equals: true

  testDynamicSupervisorIsSupervisor =>
    self assert: DynamicSupervisor isSupervisor equals: true

  testActorSupervisionSpec =>
    spec := Counter supervisionSpec
    self assert: spec class equals: SupervisionSpec
    self assert: spec actorClass name equals: #Counter
    self assert: spec restart equals: #temporary

  // === withShutdown: fluent override (BT-1456) ===
  testWithShutdownSetterReturnsFreshCopy =>
    spec := SupervisionSpec new withActorClass: Counter
    spec2 := spec withShutdown: 30000
    // Original unchanged
    self assert: spec shutdown equals: nil
    // New instance updated
    self assert: spec2 shutdown equals: 30000

  testChildSpecWorkerDefaultShutdown =>
    // Without withShutdown:, workers default to 5000
    spec := SupervisionSpec new withActorClass: Counter
    child := spec childSpec
    self assert: (child at: #shutdown) equals: 5000

  testChildSpecCustomShutdown =>
    // withShutdown: overrides the default for workers
    spec := (SupervisionSpec new withActorClass: Counter) withShutdown: 30000
    child := spec childSpec
    self assert: (child at: #shutdown) equals: 30000

  testChildSpecSupervisorDefaultShutdown =>
    // Without withShutdown:, supervisors default to #infinity
    spec := SupervisionSpec new withActorClass: Supervisor
    child := spec childSpec
    self assert: (child at: #shutdown) equals: #infinity

  testChildSpecSupervisorCustomShutdown =>
    // withShutdown: can override even supervisor shutdown
    spec := (SupervisionSpec new withActorClass: Supervisor) withShutdown: 10000
    child := spec childSpec
    self assert: (child at: #shutdown) equals: 10000
entries.explanation: BT-1218: Tests for SupervisionSpec value-type basics and childSpec construction. Restored from BT-1217 after splitting supervision tests by concern (single-class-per-file rule).  Fixtures: stdlib/test/fixtures/counter.bt (Counter — Actor subclass)

entries.title: SupervisorDefaultsTest
entries.source:
// BT-1222: Tests for Supervisor class-side defaults and subclass override.
//
// Fixtures: stdlib/test/fixtures/test_supervisor_custom.bt

TestCase subclass: SupervisorDefaultsTest

  // === Supervisor class-side defaults ===
  testSupervisorStrategyDefault =>
    self assert: Supervisor strategy equals: #oneForOne

  testSupervisorMaxRestartsDefault =>
    self assert: Supervisor maxRestarts equals: 10

  testSupervisorRestartWindowDefault =>
    self assert: Supervisor restartWindow equals: 60

  testSupervisorIsSupervisorTrue =>
    self assert: Supervisor isSupervisor equals: true

  // === Supervisor subclass can override defaults ===
  testSupervisorSubclassOverridesStrategy =>
    self assert: TestSupervisorCustom strategy equals: #oneForAll

  testSupervisorSubclassOverridesMaxRestarts =>
    self assert: TestSupervisorCustom maxRestarts equals: 3

  testSupervisorSubclassOverridesRestartWindow =>
    self assert: TestSupervisorCustom restartWindow equals: 30

  testSupervisorSubclassIsSupervisorTrue =>
    self assert: TestSupervisorCustom isSupervisor equals: true

entries.title: TestRunnerTest
entries.source:
// BUnit tests for TestRunner and TestResult (BT-762)

TestCase subclass: TestRunnerTest

  testResultTotal =>
    result := TestRunner run: SampleTestCase
    self assert: result total equals: 2

  testResultPassed =>
    result := TestRunner run: SampleTestCase
    self assert: result passed equals: 2

  testResultFailed =>
    result := TestRunner run: SampleTestCase
    self assert: result failed equals: 0

  testResultHasPassed =>
    result := TestRunner run: SampleTestCase
    self assert: result hasPassed

  testResultSummary =>
    result := TestRunner run: SampleTestCase
    self deny: (result summary == nil)

  testResultDuration =>
    result := TestRunner run: SampleTestCase
    self assert: (result duration >= 0.0)

  testResultPrintString =>
    result := TestRunner run: SampleTestCase
    self deny: (result printString == nil)

  testResultFailuresEmpty =>
    result := TestRunner run: SampleTestCase
    self assert: (result failures == nil) equals: false
entries.explanation: BUnit tests for TestRunner and TestResult (BT-762)

entries.title: Tier2BlockRoundTripTest
entries.explanation: BT-851/BT-852/BT-853: Tier 2 block round-trip tests (ADR 0041 Phase 1) Verifies the stateful block calling convention works end-to-end: a block with captured mutations is passed via self-send to a HOM, the HOM calls `value:` with the Tier 2 protocol, and the mutation propagates back to the caller

entries.title: TimeTest
entries.source:
// Stdlib tests for Time class (BT-1244)

TestCase subclass: TimeTest

  testNowSReturnsInteger =>
    s := Time nowS
    self assert: s class equals: Integer

  testNowMsReturnsInteger =>
    ms := Time nowMs
    self assert: ms class equals: Integer

  testNowUsReturnsInteger =>
    us := Time nowUs
    self assert: us class equals: Integer

  testNowSIsPositive => self assert: (Time nowS > 0)

  testNowMsIsPositive => self assert: (Time nowMs > 0)

  testNowUsIsPositive => self assert: (Time nowUs > 0)

  testNowMsGreaterThanNowS =>
    // Milliseconds > seconds for any time after epoch
    s := Time nowS
    ms := Time nowMs
    self assert: (ms > s)

  testNowUsGreaterThanNowMs =>
    // Microseconds > milliseconds for any time after epoch
    ms := Time nowMs
    us := Time nowUs
    self assert: (us > ms)

  testNowSIsReasonableEpoch =>
    // Should be well past 2020 (1577836800)
    self assert: (Time nowS > 1577836800)

  testNowMsAndNowUsArePositive =>
    // Sanity check: both values are well above zero
    self assert: (Time nowMs > 1000000000000)
    self assert: (Time nowUs > 1000000000000000)
entries.explanation: Stdlib tests for Time class (BT-1244)

entries.title: TimeoutProxyTest
entries.source:
// BUnit tests for TimeoutProxy (BT-1190)

TestCase subclass: TimeoutProxyTest
  field: actor = nil

  class serial -> Boolean => true

  setUp => self.actor := SlowActor spawn

  tearDown => self.actor isAlive ifTrue: [self.actor stop]

  testWithTimeoutReturnsProxy =>
    // withTimeout: returns a TimeoutProxy, not the original actor
    proxy := self.actor withTimeout: 30000
    self assert: proxy class equals: TimeoutProxy
    proxy stop

  testProxyForwardsMessages =>
    // Proxy forwards messages to the target actor
    proxy := self.actor withTimeout: 30000
    result := proxy getValue
    self assert: result equals: 42
    proxy stop

  testProxyForwardsWithSufficientTimeout =>
    // slowGet: sleeps 50ms; 2000ms timeout is plenty
    proxy := self.actor withTimeout: 2000
    result := proxy slowGet: 50
    self assert: result equals: 42
    proxy stop

  testProxyTimesOutWithShortTimeout =>
    // slowGet: sleeps 500ms; 100ms timeout should trigger timeout error
    proxy := self.actor withTimeout: 100
    self should: [proxy slowGet: 500] raise: #timeout
    proxy stop

  testInfinityTimeoutNeverTimesOut =>
    // #infinity timeout should never time out
    proxy := self.actor withTimeout: #infinity
    result := proxy slowGet: 50
    self assert: result equals: 42
    proxy stop

  testOneShotUsage =>
    // Capture proxy to stop it after use
    proxy := self.actor withTimeout: 2000
    result := proxy getValue
    self assert: result equals: 42
    proxy stop

  testProxyTimeoutExceedsDefaultFiveSeconds =>
    // Regression: caller→proxy hop must not use the default 5000ms timeout.
    // Without the fix, a 5200ms delay would timeout at the outer hop even
    // though the proxy is configured with a 10000ms timeout.
    proxy := self.actor withTimeout: 10000
    result := proxy slowGet: 5200
    self assert: result equals: 42
    proxy stop
entries.explanation: BUnit tests for TimeoutProxy (BT-1190)

entries.title: TimerTest
entries.source:
// Stdlib tests for Timer class (BT-1121)

TestCase subclass: TimerTest

  testSleepReturnsNil =>
    // Timer sleep: returns nil
    result := Timer sleep: 1
    self assert: result isNil

  testSleepZeroReturnsNil =>
    // Timer sleep: 0 returns nil immediately
    result := Timer sleep: 0
    self assert: result isNil

  testAfterReturnsTimer =>
    // Timer after:do: returns a Timer instance
    t := Timer after: 10000 do: []
    self assert: t class equals: Timer
    t cancel

  testEveryReturnsTimer =>
    // Timer every:do: returns a Timer instance
    t := Timer every: 10000 do: []
    self assert: t class equals: Timer
    t cancel

  testCancelPreventsExecution =>
    // Cancelled after: timer process is dead (block never ran)
    t := Timer after: 50 do: []
    self assert: t isActive
    t cancel
    // after cancel, process is immediately dead — block never ran
    self assert: t isActive not

  testAfterFiresAndBecomesInactive =>
    // after: block fires after delay; process exits, so isActive becomes false
    t := Timer after: 20 do: []
    self assert: t isActive
    Timer sleep: 100
    self assert: t isActive not

  testEveryStaysActiveAcrossMultipleIntervals =>
    // every: process stays alive across multiple intervals (repeating behaviour)
    t := Timer every: 20 do: []
    // still active after 3+ intervals have elapsed
    Timer sleep: 100
    self assert: t isActive
    t cancel
    self assert: t isActive not

  testCancelReturnsTrueWhenActive =>
    // cancel returns true for an active timer
    t := Timer after: 10000 do: []
    result := t cancel
    self assert: result

  testCancelReturnsFalseWhenInactive =>
    // cancel returns false for an already-cancelled timer
    t := Timer after: 10000 do: []
    t cancel
    result := t cancel
    self assert: result not

  testIsActiveWhenPending =>
    // isActive returns true for a pending timer
    t := Timer after: 10000 do: []
    self assert: t isActive
    t cancel

  testIsActiveAfterCancel =>
    // isActive returns false after cancel
    t := Timer after: 10000 do: []
    t cancel
    self assert: t isActive not

  testIsActiveAfterFired =>
    // isActive returns false after a one-shot timer fires
    t := Timer after: 10 do: []
    Timer sleep: 100
    self assert: t isActive not

  testPrintString =>
    // printString returns a String
    t := Timer after: 10000 do: []
    self assert: t printString class equals: String
    t cancel

  testErrorNegativeDurationAfter =>
    // after: with negative ms raises type_error
    self should: [Timer after: -1 do: []] raise: #type_error

  testErrorNegativeDurationSleep =>
    // sleep: with negative ms raises type_error
    self should: [Timer sleep: -1] raise: #type_error

  testErrorNonIntegerSleep =>
    // sleep: with non-integer raises type_error
    @expect type
    self should: [Timer sleep: "bad"] raise: #type_error

  testErrorZeroDurationEvery =>
    // every: with zero ms raises type_error (must be positive)
    self should: [Timer every: 0 do: []] raise: #type_error

  testErrorNegativeDurationEvery =>
    // every: with negative ms raises type_error
    self should: [Timer every: -1 do: []] raise: #type_error
entries.explanation: Stdlib tests for Timer class (BT-1121)

entries.title: TimesRepeatSimpleTest
entries.source:
// Regression tests for timesRepeat: simple path
//
// The simple path (no local mutations, no self-sends, no field writes)
// previously generated `let _ = body_code in ...` without ever applying the
// body closure, so the block body silently never executed
//
// The mutation path (local writes / self-sends) was unaffected

TestCase subclass: TimesRepeatSimpleTest

  testZeroTimesIsNoOp =>
    c := Counter spawn
    0 timesRepeat: [c increment]
    self assert: c getValue equals: 0

  testOneTimeRunsBlockOnce =>
    c := Counter spawn
    1 timesRepeat: [c increment]
    self assert: c getValue equals: 1

  testThreeTimesRunsBlockThreeTimes =>
    c := Counter spawn
    3 timesRepeat: [c increment]
    self assert: c getValue equals: 3

  testFiveTimesRunsBlockFiveTimes =>
    c := Counter spawn
    5 timesRepeat: [c increment]
    self assert: c getValue equals: 5
entries.explanation: Regression tests for timesRepeat: simple path  The simple path (no local mutations, no self-sends, no field writes) previously generated `let _ = body_code in ...` without ever applying the body closure, so the block body silently never executed  The mutation path (local writes / self-sends) was unaffected

We test the API surface, error behavior, and live health rather than trace data content.

entries.title: TypedClassesTest
entries.source:
// Consolidated type annotation tests (BT-673)

TestCase subclass: TypedClassesTest

  testAnnotationSyntaxVariations =>
    // Test various type annotation syntax forms
    math := TypedMath new
    self assert: (math identity: 4) equals: 4
    self assert: (math identityWithSpace: 5) equals: 5
    self assert: (math sum: 2 with: 3) equals: 5
    self assert: (math maybeName: true) equals: 1
    self assert: (math maybeName: false) equals: "none"
    // asType: cast
    self assert: ((41 asType: Integer) + 1) equals: 42

  testActorBehavior =>
    // Test actor instances with typed methods
    counter := TypedCounter spawn
    self assert: counter increment equals: 1
    self assert: (counter add: 5) equals: 6
    self assert: counter current equals: 6
    account := TypedAccount spawn
    self assert: (account setOwner: "Alice") equals: "Alice"
    self assert: (account deposit: 10) equals: 10
    self assert: (account withdraw: 3) equals: 7
    self assert: account balance equals: 7
    self assert: account getOwner equals: "Alice"

  testNoFalsePositivesRegression =>
    // Regression tests for permissive typing paths
    ledger := TypedLedger spawn
    self assert: ledger addOne equals: 1
    self assert: ledger addOne equals: 2
    untyped := UntypedContainer spawn
    self assert: (untyped setItem: "hello") equals: "hello"
    self assert: (untyped setItem: 99) equals: 99
    typed := TypedAccount spawn
    self assert: (typed deposit: 20) equals: 20
    self assert: (typed deposit: (5 asType: Integer)) equals: 25
    self assert: typed balance equals: 25
    math2 := TypedMath new
    self assert: (math2 maybeName: false) equals: "none"
    self assert: (math2 maybeName: true) equals: 1
entries.explanation: Consolidated type annotation tests (BT-673)

entries.title: UnicodeTest
entries.source:
// BT-388: Tests that string operations are Unicode-aware (grapheme clusters) —
// equality with CJK and emoji, size in graphemes, at: indexing, reverse, and
// includesSubstring: for multi-byte characters

TestCase subclass: UnicodeTest

  testUnicodeStringOperations =>
    self assert: "世界" equals: "世界"
    // Emoji (4-byte UTF-8)
    self assert: "🌍" equals: "🌍"
    // Mixed ASCII and Unicode
    self assert: "Hello 世界 🌍" equals: "Hello 世界 🌍"
    // Japanese
    self assert: "こんにちは" equals: "こんにちは"
    // Korean
    self assert: "안녕하세요" equals: "안녕하세요"
    self assert: "世界" size equals: 2
    self assert: "🌍" size equals: 1
    self assert: "Hello 世界 🌍" size equals: 10
    self assert: "世界" =:= "世界"
    self deny: "世界" =:= "hello"
    self assert: ("Hello 世界" at: 7) equals: "世"
    self assert: ("Hello 世界" at: 8) equals: "界"
    self assert: "世界" reverse equals: "界世"
    self assert: ("Hello 世界" includesSubstring: "世")
    self deny: ("Hello 世界" includesSubstring: "🌍")
entries.explanation: BT-388: Tests that string operations are Unicode-aware (grapheme clusters) — equality with CJK and emoji, size in graphemes, at: indexing, reverse, and includesSubstring: for multi-byte characters

entries.title: ValueObjectSelfSendTest

// BT-892: Tests that inject:into:, do:, and collect: with self sends
// work correctly in value-object methods (no State variable)

TestCase subclass: ValueObjectSelfSendTest

  testInjectIntoWithSelfSend =>
    f := StringFormatter new: #{#prefix => "<", #suffix => ">"}
    result := f joinFormatted: #("a", "b", "c")
    self assert: result equals: "[a][b][c]"

  testCollectWithSelfSend =>
    f := StringFormatter new

    self assert: result equals: #("[x]", "[y]")

entries.title: ValueSubclassTest
entries.source:
// BT-923: End-to-end tests for `Value subclass:` auto-generated slot methods
// Exercises: getters, with*: functional setters, keyword constructor, new, new:
// BT-924: Reflection tests — fieldAt: reads slots, fieldNames returns slot names,
//         fieldAt:put: raises immutable_value
// BT-996: Class method using ClassName slot: value routes to keyword constructor
//
// Fixtures: stdlib/test/fixtures/value_point.bt (ValuePoint)
//           stdlib/test/fixtures/tagged_value.bt (TaggedValue — BT-996)

TestCase subclass: ValueSubclassTest

  testAutoGetter =>
    p := ValuePoint new
    // Auto-generated getter returns the default value
    self assert: p x equals: 0
    self assert: p y equals: 0

  testAutoGetterAfterNew =>
    p := ValuePoint new: #{#x => 3, #y => 4}
    self assert: p x equals: 3
    self assert: p y equals: 4

  testAutoSetterReturnsNewInstance =>
    p := ValuePoint new: #{#x => 1, #y => 2}
    p2 := p withX: 10
    // Original unchanged
    self assert: p x equals: 1
    // New instance has updated x, same y
    self assert: p2 x equals: 10
    self assert: p2 y equals: 2

  testChainedWithSetters =>
    p := ValuePoint new
    p2 := (p withX: 5) withY: 7
    self assert: p2 x equals: 5
    self assert: p2 y equals: 7

  testKeywordConstructor =>
    p := ValuePoint x: 3 y: 4
    self assert: p x equals: 3
    self assert: p y equals: 4

  testUserDefinedMethodCoexists =>
    p := ValuePoint x: 3 y: 4
    self assert: p distanceSquared equals: 25

  testWithSetterPreservesClass =>
    p := ValuePoint x: 1 y: 2
    p2 := p withX: 99
    self assert: p2 class equals: ValuePoint

  testNewDefaultsAllZero =>
    p := ValuePoint new
    self assert: p x equals: 0
    self assert: p y equals: 0

  // BT-924: fieldAt: reads slot values from value objects
  testFieldAtReadsSlot =>
    p := ValuePoint x: 3 y: 4
    self assert: (p fieldAt: #x) equals: 3
    self assert: (p fieldAt: #y) equals: 4
    // Non-existent field returns nil (Smalltalk-80 semantics)
    self assert: (p fieldAt: #z) equals: nil

  // BT-924: fieldNames returns the slot names of the value object
  testFieldNamesReturnsSlotCount =>
    p := ValuePoint x: 1 y: 2
    names := p fieldNames
    // ValuePoint has two slots: x and y
    self assert: names size equals: 2

  // BT-924: fieldAt:put: raises immutable_value at runtime — use with*: methods instead
  // Compile-time rejection of direct slot mutation (self.slot :=) is a separate feature
  testFieldAtPutRaisesImmutableValue =>
    p := ValuePoint x: 1 y: 2
    self should: [p fieldAt: #x put: 99] raise: #immutable_value

  // BT-996: `ClassName slot: value` inside a class method must route to the class-side
  // keyword constructor, not the instance getter. Without the fix this caused {badmap}.
  testClassMethodSlotSendRoutesToConstructor =>
    v := TaggedValue fromTag: "hello"
    self assert: v tag equals: "hello"

  testClassMethodSlotSendClassIsCorrect =>
    v := TaggedValue fromTag: "world"
    self assert: v class equals: TaggedValue

  // BT-1408: Value subclass with 20+ long camelCase fields — previously triggered
  // core_scan_error because the keyword constructor atom exceeded 255 chars.
  testManyFieldsNew =>
    v := ValueManyFields new
    self assert: v codexInputTokens equals: 0
    self assert: v codexModelName equals: ""

  testManyFieldsWithSetter =>
    v := ValueManyFields new
    v2 := v withCodexInputTokens: 42
    self assert: v2 codexInputTokens equals: 42
    // Original unchanged
    self assert: v codexInputTokens equals: 0

  testManyFieldsNewMap =>
    v := ValueManyFields new: #{#codexInputTokens => 100, #errorCount => 5}
    self assert: v codexInputTokens equals: 100
    self assert: v errorCount equals: 5
    // Unset fields use defaults
    self assert: v codexOutputTokens equals: 0

  // BT-1408: Keyword constructor with 20 long-named fields — exercises the
  // class_send path which also generates a long selector atom.
  testManyFieldsKeywordConstructor =>
    v := ValueManyFields
      codexInputTokens: 10
      codexOutputTokens: 20
      codexModelName: "gpt"
      codexCachedTokens: 5
      codexReasoning: "r"
      codexReasoningEffort: "high"
      codexMaxOutputTokens: 100
      codexSystemFingerprint: "fp"
      codexServiceTier: "t1"
      operationTokens: 30
      operationCost: 1
      operationDuration: 50
      apiCallCount: 3
      errorCount: 0
      retryCount: 1
      totalLatency: 200
      averageLatency: 67
      peakMemoryUsage: 512
      networkBandwidth: 1000
      concurrentRequests: 8
    self assert: v codexInputTokens equals: 10
    self assert: v codexModelName equals: "gpt"
    self assert: v concurrentRequests equals: 8
entries.explanation: BT-923: End-to-end tests for `Value subclass:` auto-generated slot methods Exercises: getters, with*: functional setters, keyword constructor, new, new: BT-924: Reflection tests — fieldAt: reads slots, fieldNames returns slot names,         fieldAt:put: raises immutable_value BT-996: Class method using ClassName slot: value routes to keyword constructor  Fixtures: stdlib/test/fixtures/value_point.bt (ValuePoint)           stdlib/test/fixtures/tagged_value.bt (TaggedValue — BT-996)

entries.title: ValueTypeMultiExprTest
entries.source:
// BT-367: Tests for multi-expression value-type method codegen sequencing

TestCase subclass: ValueTypeMultiExprTest

  testTwoExpressionMethod =>
    calc := MultiExprCalc new
    self assert: (calc doubleAfterAdd: 5) equals: 10
    self assert: (calc doubleAfterAdd: 0) equals: 0
    self assert: (calc doubleAfterAdd: -3) equals: -6

  testThreeExpressionMethod =>
    calc := MultiExprCalc new
    // Only the last expression (a * b) is returned
    self assert: (calc compute: 3 with: 4) equals: 12
    // First two expressions execute but results are discarded
    self assert: (calc compute: 5 with: 0) equals: 0
    self assert: (calc compute: -2 with: 3) equals: -6
entries.explanation: BT-367: Tests for multi-expression value-type method codegen sequencing

entries.title: ValueTypeNonLocalReturnTest

// BT-754: Tests non-local return (^) inside block arguments in Object subclass
// (non-actor) methods — empty/non-empty arrays, classify, check, double, and
// on:do: with NLR

TestCase subclass: ValueTypeNonLocalReturnTest

  testNonLocalReturnInValueTypeMethods =>
    self assert: (NlrReturnTest new test: #()) equals: "empty"
    self assert: (NlrReturnTest new test: #(1)) equals: "not empty"
    self assert: (NlrFoo new classify: 5) equals: "positive"
    self assert: (NlrFoo new classify: -1) equals: "non-positive"
    self assert: (NlrFoo new classify: 0) equals: "non-positive"

    self assert: (NlrBar new check: #(1)) equals: "not empty"

    self assert: (NlrSkipper new test: #(1, 2, 3)) equals: "middle"
    self assert: (NlrDoubler new test: 5) equals: 10
    self assert: (NlrDoubler new test: -1) equals: 0
    self assert: (NlrOnDo new test: #()) equals: "early"
    self assert: (NlrOnDo new test: #(1)) equals: "not early"
entries.explanation: BT-754: Tests non-local return (^) inside block arguments in Object subclass (non-actor) methods — empty/non-empty arrays, classify, check, double, and on:do: with NLR

entries.title: ValueTypeUpdateTest
entries.source:
// BT-833: Tests for immutable-update semantics on value types
// Field assignments use Self-threading (Self -> Self1 -> Self{N}) so that
// `self` in expression position resolves to the latest snapshot

TestCase subclass: ValueTypeUpdateTest

  testSingleFieldUpdate =>
    b := ValueTypeBuilder new
    b2 := b withX: 42
    // The returned object has the updated field
    self assert: b2 getX equals: 42
    // The original object is unchanged (immutable semantics)
    self assert: b getX equals: 0

  testChainedSetters =>
    b := ValueTypeBuilder new
    b2 := (b withX: 10) withY: 20
    self assert: b2 getX equals: 10
    self assert: b2 getY equals: 20

  testMultipleFieldAssignmentsInOneMethod =>
    b := ValueTypeBuilder new
    b2 := b setX: 5 y: 7
    self assert: b2 getX equals: 5
    self assert: b2 getY equals: 7

  testReadThenWrite =>
    b := ValueTypeBuilder new withX: 10
    b2 := b incrementX: 5
    self assert: b2 getX equals: 15
    // Original unchanged
    self assert: b getX equals: 10
entries.explanation: BT-833: Tests for immutable-update semantics on value types Field assignments use Self-threading (Self -> Self1 -> Self{N}) so that `self` in expression position resolves to the latest snapshot

entries.title: WhileValueTest
entries.source:
// BT-1609: Regression tests for whileTrue:/whileFalse: in value-type context.
// The bug: body mutations were silently discarded because the value-type method body
// wrapper did not extract threaded locals from the loop's {'nil', StateAcc} result tuple.

TestCase subclass: WhileValueTest

  // Core bug: body mutation must be visible to condition and outer scope
  testWhileTrueBodyMutationVisibleToCondition =>
    counter := 3
    steps := 0
    [counter > 0]
      whileTrue: [
        counter := counter - 1
        steps := steps + 1
      ]
    self assert: steps equals: 3
    self assert: counter equals: 0

  // Condition reads variable mutated in body — sum accumulates correctly
  testWhileTrueAccumulation =>
    i := 1
    sum := 0
    [i <= 5]
      whileTrue: [
        sum := sum + i
        i := i + 1
      ]
    self assert: sum equals: 15
    self assert: i equals: 6

  // whileFalse: variant
  testWhileFalseBodyMutation =>
    counter := 0
    [counter >= 3] whileFalse: [counter := counter + 1]
    self assert: counter equals: 3

  // whileTrue: with false condition — no iterations, returns nil
  testWhileTrueImmediateExit =>
    x := 42
    [false] whileTrue: [x := 0]
    self assert: x equals: 42
entries.explanation: BT-1609: Regression tests for whileTrue:/whileFalse: in value-type context. The bug: body mutations were silently discarded because the value-type method body wrapper did not extract threaded locals from the loop's {'nil', StateAcc} result tuple.

entries.title: WorkspaceInterfaceTest
entries.source:
// BUnit tests for WorkspaceInterface facade API (BT-849 / ADR 0040 Phase 6)
//
// Tests use class-level introspection (includesSelector:, inheritsFrom:) since
// the Workspace singleton is not available in the BUnit test context
// (workspace_mode: false). Live functionality tests are in E2E:
// tests/e2e/cases/workspace_load.bt, workspace_native_api.bt
//
// Covers: classes, testClasses, globals, load:, test, test:, actors,
//         actorAt:, actorsOf:, bind:as:, unbind:

TestCase subclass: WorkspaceInterfaceTest

  testIncludesSelectorClasses =>
    self assert: (WorkspaceInterface includesSelector: #classes)

  testIncludesSelectorTestClasses =>
    self assert: (WorkspaceInterface includesSelector: #testClasses)

  testIncludesSelectorGlobals =>
    self assert: (WorkspaceInterface includesSelector: #globals)

  testIncludesSelectorLoadColon =>
    self assert: (WorkspaceInterface includesSelector: #load:)

  testIncludesSelectorTest =>
    self assert: (WorkspaceInterface includesSelector: #test)

  testIncludesSelectorTestColon =>
    self assert: (WorkspaceInterface includesSelector: #test:)

  testIncludesSelectorActors =>
    self assert: (WorkspaceInterface includesSelector: #actors)

  testIncludesSelectorActorAt =>
    self assert: (WorkspaceInterface includesSelector: #actorAt:)

  testIncludesSelectorActorsOf =>
    self assert: (WorkspaceInterface includesSelector: #actorsOf:)

  testIncludesSelectorBindAs =>
    self assert: (WorkspaceInterface includesSelector: #bind:as:)

  testIncludesSelectorUnbind =>
    self assert: (WorkspaceInterface includesSelector: #unbind:)

  testInheritsFromObject =>
    self assert: (WorkspaceInterface inheritsFrom: Object)
entries.explanation: BUnit tests for WorkspaceInterface facade API (BT-849 / ADR 0040 Phase 6)  Tests use class-level introspection (includesSelector:, inheritsFrom:) since the Workspace singleton is not available in the BUnit test context (workspace_mode: false). Live functionality tests are in E2E: tests/e2e/cases/workspace_load.bt, workspace_native_api.bt  Covers: classes, testClasses, globals, load:, test, test:, actors,         actorAt:, actorsOf:, bind:as:, unbind:

entries.title: Bank
entries.source:
// Bank fixture for E2E tests (BT-569)
// Registry and factory for Account actors using Dictionary

Actor subclass: Bank
  state: accounts = #{}
  state: lastCreated = nil

  openAccount: name =>
    self.lastCreated := Account spawn
    self.lastCreated setOwner: name
    self.accounts := self.accounts at: name put: self.lastCreated
    ^self.lastCreated

  accountFor: name =>
    ^self.accounts at: name ifAbsent: [
      self error: "No account found for: " ++ name
    ]

  allAccounts => ^self.accounts
entries.explanation: Bank fixture for E2E tests (BT-569) Registry and factory for Account actors using Dictionary

entries.title: Account
entries.source:
// Account fixture for E2E tests (BT-569)
// An actor managing a single bank balance with overdraft protection

Actor subclass: Account
  state: balance = 0
  state: owner = ""

  deposit: amount =>
    amount < 0 ifTrue: [self error: "Amount must be non-negative"]
    self.balance := self.balance + amount
    ^self.balance

  withdraw: amount =>
    amount < 0 ifTrue: [self error: "Amount must be non-negative"]
    self.balance < amount
      ifTrue: [
        self
          error: "Insufficient funds (balance: "
            ++ self.balance printString
            ++ ", requested: "
            ++ amount printString
            ++ ")"
      ]
    self.balance := self.balance - amount
    ^self.balance

  balance => ^self.balance

  setOwner: name => self.owner := name

  getOwner => ^self.owner
entries.explanation: Account fixture for E2E tests (BT-569) An actor managing a single bank balance with overdraft protection

entries.title: Hanoi
entries.source:
// Towers of Hanoi — e2e test fixture.
// Canonical source lives in examples/getting-started/src/hanoi.bt.

Value subclass: Hanoi

  solve: n from: source to: target via: auxiliary =>
    (n > 0)
      ifTrue: [
        self
          solve: n - 1
          from: source
          to: auxiliary
          via: target
        TranscriptStream current show: "Move disk from " ++ source ++ " to " ++ target
        TranscriptStream current cr
        self
          solve: n - 1
          from: auxiliary
          to: target
          via: source
      ]
      ifFalse: [nil]
entries.explanation: Towers of Hanoi — e2e test fixture. Canonical source lives in examples/getting-started/src/hanoi.bt.

// Moderator actor fixture for E2E tests (BT-568)
// Demonstrates: Actor inheritance (ChatMember subclass), super calls

  kick: member => self.room leave: member

  mute: memberName => self.muted := self.muted add: memberName

  unmute: memberName => self.muted := self.muted remove: memberName

  isMuted: memberName => self.muted includes: memberName
